documents.title: IoTable System Architecture
documents.raw_text:
document:
  id: 02-system-architecture.system-architecture
  title: IoTable System Architecture
  project: IoTable
  version: 1.0.0
  language: en
  format: yaml
  schema_required: true
  schema_file: schemas/02_system_architecture.schema.json
  status: draft
architecture_style:
  purpose:
    rule: Define the primary architecture style for IoTable implementation.
    applies_to:
    - backend_application
    - module_boundaries
    - runtime_deployment
    - database_access
    - inter_module_integration
    - implementation_planning
  selected_architecture:
    primary_style: modular_monolith
    backend_deployable: single_backend_deployable
    module_structure: business_capability_modules
    internal_module_architecture: clean_architecture_per_module
    inter_module_integration: in_process_application_contracts_and_domain_events
    external_service_split: not_allowed_by_default
    database_model:
      platform_database: single_platform_database
      tenant_database: database_per_tenant
    frontend_deployment_split: two_separate_nextjs_apps
  rationale:
    selected_because:
    - IoTable requires strong module boundaries and does not require independent service
      deployment.
    - Database-per-tenant already creates operational complexity; microservices would
      add unnecessary deployment, observability, and data consistency overhead.
    - Ubuntu VPS deployment requires a low-complexity runtime topology.
    - A modular monolith is easier for LLM coding agents to implement consistently
      when module ownership, contracts, and tests are explicit.
    - Business capability modules remain inside the modular monolith unless an owner-approved
      architecture decision changes the service boundary.
    rejected_alternatives:
    - id: microservices
      reason: Rejected because it increases distributed system complexity without
        a validated need for independent deployment.
    - id: layered_monolith_without_module_boundaries
      reason: Rejected because it does not protect business capability ownership strongly
        enough.
    - id: frontend_backend_split_as_primary_architecture
      reason: Rejected because frontend/backend is an implementation axis, not a business
        capability boundary.
  module_boundary_rules:
  - id: MBR-001
    rule: Business capability modules are the primary architecture boundary.
  - id: MBR-002
    rule: A module MUST own its application services, domain rules, data contracts,
      API contracts, and tests for its capability.
  - id: MBR-003
    rule: A module MUST NOT directly mutate another module's owned data.
  - id: MBR-004
    rule: Cross-module behavior MUST use explicit application contracts, domain events,
      or documented integration points.
  - id: MBR-005
    rule: Shared modules MUST provide infrastructure behavior and MUST NOT own product
      behavior decisions.
  - id: MBR-006
    rule: Platform-scope modules MUST NOT own tenant operational data.
  - id: MBR-007
    rule: Tenant-scope modules MUST NOT own platform control-plane data.
  clean_architecture_rules:
  - id: CAR-001
    rule: Each business module SHOULD be structured around domain, application, infrastructure,
      and presentation/API concerns.
  - id: CAR-002
    rule: Domain rules MUST NOT depend on database, HTTP, UI, or framework-specific
      implementation details.
  - id: CAR-003
    rule: Application services coordinate use cases and enforce module-level workflow
      rules.
  - id: CAR-004
    rule: Infrastructure code implements persistence, external integrations, messaging,
      and framework adapters.
  - id: CAR-005
    rule: API endpoints MUST call application services rather than directly mutating
      persistence models.
  - id: CAR-006
    rule: Module tests MUST cover domain rules, application use cases, API contracts,
      and persistence boundaries when touched.
  runtime_topology:
    backend:
      deployable_count: 1
      style: single_fastapi_backend
      runtime: Python 3.14
      responsibilities:
      - platform_api
      - tenant_internal_api
      - customer_public_api
      - device_realtime_endpoint
      - background_jobs
      - health_checks
    database:
      engine: PostgreSQL 17
      platform_database: iotable_platform
      tenant_database_pattern: iotable_tenant_{tenantSlug}
    frontend:
      stack:
      - Next.js 16.2.4
      - React 19.2.5
      - TypeScript 6.0.3
      - Tailwind CSS v4.0
      - Node.js 24 LTS
      - pnpm 11.0.3
      deployment_split: two_separate_nextjs_apps
    hosting:
      target: ubuntu_vps
  inter_module_communication:
    default_mode: in_process
    allowed_patterns:
    - application_service_call
    - domain_event_published_in_process
    - explicit_module_contract
    prohibited_patterns:
    - direct_cross_module_database_write
    - implicit_shared_mutable_state
    - untracked_runtime_dependency
    - service_extraction_without_owner_approved_architecture_decision
  background_processing:
    default_location: inside_backend_deployable
    owns:
    - tenant_provisioning_jobs
    - tenant_schema_change_jobs
    - maintenance_jobs
    rule: Background jobs remain inside the modular monolith unless an owner-approved
      architecture decision explicitly separates them.
  migration_policy:
    platform_database_migrations:
      path: src/backend/alembic/platform/
      execution: executed_for_platform_database_on_deployment
    tenant_database_migrations:
      path: src/backend/alembic/tenant/
      execution: executed_by_platform_level_migration_orchestrator
    orchestration_rules:
    - Alembic migrations MUST be separated into two distinct directories under src/backend/alembic/ to prevent schema cross-contamination.
    - Platform-level migrations (src/backend/alembic/platform/) manage control-plane tables (tenants, audit_logs, etc.) and MUST be executed synchronously during deployment.
    - Tenant-level migrations (src/backend/alembic/tenant/) manage the isolated schema layout for operational tables (tables, menus, orders, etc.) and MUST be executed dynamically for each tenant by the Celery background worker (`app.worker`).
    - The dynamic tenant migrator MUST programmatically set the database connection URL and search path (tenant schema) at runtime for each migration run.
    rule: Tenant schema changes MUST use the platform-level migration orchestrator
      defined by the product baseline.
  service_extraction_policy:
    default: not_allowed
    allowed_only_when:
    - module_boundary_is_stable
    - operational_need_is_documented
    - data_ownership_is_clear
    - deployment_and_observability_cost_is_accepted
    - project_owner_approves_new_architecture_decision
    rule: No module may be extracted into a separate service without an owner-approved
      architecture decision.
  open_decisions: []
  done_when:
  - primary_architecture_style_is_selected
  - rejected_architecture_styles_are_recorded
  - module_boundary_rules_are_defined
  - clean_architecture_rules_are_defined
  - runtime_topology_is_defined
  - inter_module_communication_is_defined
  - migration_policy_is_defined
  - service_extraction_policy_is_defined
  - open_architecture_decisions_are_recorded
backend_solution_structure:
  purpose:
    rule: Define the backend Python package layout, runtime hosts, module directory structure, dependency boundaries, and package management for IoTable.
    applies_to:
    - backend_application
    - modular_monolith
    - background_jobs
    - module_boundaries
    - llm_coding_agent_implementation
  backend_stack:
    runtime: Python 3.14
    api_host_framework: FastAPI
    request_validation_and_serialization: Pydantic v2
    package_manager: uv
    database_engine: PostgreSQL 17
    orm: SQLAlchemy & Alembic
    background_jobs: Celery with Redis/PostgreSQL storage
    distributed_cache: Redis
    architecture_style: modular_monolith
    module_internal_architecture: clean_folders_per_module
  solution_policy:
    backend_codebase_model: single_modular_monolith_package
    package_manager: uv
    runtime_hosts:
    - app.main:app
    - app.worker
    module_folder_per_business_capability: true
    clean_architecture_inside_each_module: true
    direct_cross_module_database_write_allowed: false
    rules:
    - IoTable backend MUST be implemented as a modular FastAPI package.
    - IoTable backend MUST use the 'uv' package manager for deterministic virtualenv and dependency resolution.
    - Business capability modules MUST be implemented as separate packages inside app/modules/.
    - Modules MUST NOT directly mutate another module's owned database tables.
    - Cross-module behavior MUST use documented application services or internal events.
  technology_decision_references:
  - id: TDR-001
    topic: Background jobs, cache, and locking
    canonical_document: specs/02_system_architecture.yaml
  repository_layout:
    root:
    - specs/
    - schemas/
    - src/
    - tests/
    - tools/
    backend_source_root: src/backend
    backend_test_root: tests/backend
    structure:
      src_backend:
      - src/backend/app/main.py
      - src/backend/app/worker.py
      - src/backend/app/core/
      - src/backend/app/modules/
      - src/backend/alembic/platform/
      - src/backend/alembic/tenant/
      tests_backend:
      - tests/backend/unit/
      - tests/backend/integration/
      - tests/backend/contracts/
      - tests/backend/architecture/
  host_projects:
  - id: app.main:app
    path: src/backend/app/main.py
    type: fastapi_http_host
    responsibilities:
    - platform_api
    - tenant_internal_api
    - customer_public_api
    - device_realtime_endpoint
    - health_checks
    - authentication_entrypoints
    - authorization_policy_enforcement
    rules:
    - app.main:app MUST compose module API endpoints using FastAPI routers.
    - app.main:app MUST NOT contain business rules directly.
    - app.main:app MUST call module application services.
    - app.main:app MUST expose health endpoints.
  - id: app.worker
    path: src/backend/app/worker.py
    type: background_job_host
    background_job_library: Celery
    background_job_storage: Redis/PostgreSQL
    responsibilities:
    - tenant_provisioning_jobs
    - tenant_schema_change_jobs
    - maintenance_jobs
    - scheduled_operational_tasks
    rules:
    - app.worker MUST execute platform-owned Celery background tasks.
    - app.worker MUST NOT expose HTTP API endpoints.
    - app.worker MUST use Celery with Redis/PostgreSQL for background task execution.
  building_blocks:
  - id: app.core.domain
    path: src/backend/app/core/domain
    purpose: Shared domain primitives without product-specific business ownership.
    allowed_contents:
    - Entity
    - ValueObject
    - DomainEvent
    - Result
    - DomainException
  - id: app.core.application
    path: src/backend/app/core/application
    purpose: Shared application abstractions.
    allowed_contents:
    - Command
    - Query
    - ApplicationResult
    - Clock abstraction
    - Current user abstraction
    - Validation abstractions
  - id: app.core.infrastructure
    path: src/backend/app/core/infrastructure
    purpose: Shared infrastructure adapters and cross-cutting implementations.
    allowed_contents:
    - PostgreSQL helpers
    - Outbox infrastructure
    - Idempotency infrastructure
    - Logging helpers
    - Correlation id handling
    - Redis cache abstractions
    - Celery task helpers
  - id: app.core.api
    path: src/backend/app/core/api
    purpose: Shared API primitives.
    allowed_contents:
    - ProblemDetails mapping
    - Endpoint conventions
    - Pagination contracts
    - Request correlation middleware
  module_project_pattern:
    root_pattern: src/backend/app/modules/{ModuleName}
    projects:
    - suffix: domain
      path_pattern: src/backend/app/modules/{ModuleName}/domain
      allowed_dependencies:
      - app.core.domain
      forbidden_dependencies:
      - FastAPI
      - SQLAlchemy
      - psycopg
      - other_module_infrastructure
    - suffix: application
      path_pattern: src/backend/app/modules/{ModuleName}/application
      allowed_dependencies:
      - app.core.domain
      - app.core.application
      - app.modules.{ModuleName}.domain
      forbidden_dependencies:
      - FastAPI controllers/endpoints
      - SQLAlchemy concrete session
    - suffix: infrastructure
      path_pattern: src/backend/app/modules/{ModuleName}/infrastructure
      allowed_dependencies:
      - app.core.infrastructure
      - app.modules.{ModuleName}.domain
      - app.modules.{ModuleName}.application
      responsibilities:
      - persistence
      - database mappings
      - external adapters
      - module repository implementations
    - suffix: api
      path_pattern: src/backend/app/modules/{ModuleName}/api
      allowed_dependencies:
      - app.core.api
      - app.modules.{ModuleName}.application
      responsibilities:
      - endpoint registration
      - request response mapping
      - authorization policy usage
  modules:
    platform_scope:
    - Platform
    - PlatformProvisioning
    - PlatformSchemaOrchestration
    - PlatformIdentityAccess
    - PlatformAudit
    - PlatformSettings
    tenant_scope:
    - Tenancy
    - TenantIdentityAccess
    - TenantSetupConfiguration
    - Tables
    - Stations
    - MenuCatalog
    - CustomerAccess
    - CustomerSessions
    - Cart
    - Ordering
    - StationRouting
    - BillSessions
    - Checkout
    - FloorService
    - TenantAudit
    - TableDeviceQrDisplay
    shared_scope:
    - SharedAuthenticationRuntime
    - TechnicalOperations
    - SharedObservability
    - SharedRateLimiting
  initial_backend_tree:
    src_backend:
    - app/main.py
    - app/worker.py
    - app/core/domain/
    - app/core/application/
    - app/core/infrastructure/
    - app/core/api/
    - app/modules/Platform/domain/
    - app/modules/Platform/application/
    - app/modules/Platform/infrastructure/
    - app/modules/Platform/api/
    - app/modules/Tenancy/domain/
    - app/modules/Tenancy/application/
    - app/modules/Tenancy/infrastructure/
    - app/modules/Tenancy/api/
    - app/modules/CustomerAccess/domain/
    - app/modules/CustomerAccess/application/
    - app/modules/CustomerAccess/infrastructure/
    - app/modules/CustomerAccess/api/
    - app/modules/Ordering/domain/
    - app/modules/Ordering/application/
    - app/modules/Ordering/infrastructure/
    - app/modules/Ordering/api/
    tests_backend:
    - unit/Platform/
    - unit/CustomerAccess/
    - unit/Ordering/
    - integration/
    - contracts/
    - architecture/
  project_dependency_rules:
  - id: BSS-001
    rule: Domain layers MUST NOT depend on Infrastructure or Api layers.
  - id: BSS-002
    rule: Application layers MUST NOT depend on Api layers.
  - id: BSS-003
    rule: Api layers MUST NOT contain domain behavior.
  - id: BSS-004
    rule: Infrastructure layers MAY depend on their own module Application and Domain layers.
  - id: BSS-005
    rule: FastAPI entrypoints MAY reference module Api and Infrastructure routers.
  - id: BSS-006
    rule: Cross-module direct Infrastructure layer dependency is forbidden.
  - id: BSS-007
    rule: Shared core packages MUST NOT contain IoTable product decisions.
  configuration_rules:
  - id: BSC-001
    rule: Platform database connection configuration MUST be resolved by app.main and app.worker configurations.
  - id: BSC-002
    rule: Tenant database connection resolution MUST use tenant context and tenant database registry.
  - id: BSC-003
    rule: Secrets MUST NOT be stored in source code or specification examples.
  - id: BSC-004
    rule: Platform and tenant frontend applications MUST NOT be hosted inside backend repositories.
  - id: BSC-005
    rule: Redis configuration MUST be resolved by backend configuration and MUST be treated as ephemeral infrastructure.
  - id: BSC-006
    rule: Celery configuration MUST be resolved by app.worker configuration.
  verification_rules:
    required_test_categories:
    - unit_tests_per_module
    - integration_tests_for_database_boundaries
    - api_contract_tests
    - architecture_dependency_tests
    rules:
    - Architecture tests MUST verify forbidden layer dependencies using tools like import-linter.
    - Contract tests MUST verify platform, tenant internal, and customer public API contracts.
    - Integration tests MUST verify platform database and tenant database boundary behavior.
  forbidden_behavior:
  - single_file_backend
  - business_logic_inside_api_host
  - background_jobs_inside_http_request_pipeline_only
  - direct_cross_module_database_write
  - domain_layer_depending_on_sqlalchemy
  - platform_frontend_inside_backend_host
  - tenant_frontend_inside_backend_host
  - redis_as_source_of_truth_for_durable_state
  - celery_broker_queues_as_platform_job_source_of_truth
  open_decisions: []
  done_when:
  - backend_solution_policy_is_defined
  - runtime_hosts_are_defined
  - background_cache_and_locking_decision_is_referenced
  - host_projects_are_defined
  - building_blocks_are_defined
  - module_project_pattern_is_defined
  - module_inventory_mapping_is_defined
  - initial_backend_tree_is_defined
  - project_dependency_rules_are_defined
  - configuration_rules_are_defined
  - verification_rules_are_defined
  - forbidden_backend_structure_behavior_is_defined
frontend_application_split:
  purpose:
    rule: Define frontend application boundaries, deployment split, domain ownership,
      and isolation rules for IoTable.
    applies_to:
    - platform_control_plane
    - tenant_console
    - tenant_staff_app
    - station_board
    - customer_ordering
  frontend_stack:
    runtime: Node.js 24 LTS
    package_manager: pnpm 11.0.3
    framework: Next.js 16.2.4
    ui_runtime: React 19.2.5
    language: TypeScript 6.0.3
    styling: Tailwind CSS v4.0
  selected_split:
    backend: single_modular_monolith_backend
    frontend_deployables:
    - id: platform_web
      type: separate_nextjs_app
      domain: platform.iotables.net
      audience:
      - platform_owner
      owns_surfaces:
      - platform_control_plane
      api_base: https://platform.iotables.net/api/platform
    - id: tenant_web
      type: separate_nextjs_app
      domain: '{tenant}.iotables.net'
      audience:
      - tenant_owner
      - tenant_admin
      - tenant_manager
      - cashier
      - floor_staff
      - station_operator
      - customer_diner
      owns_surfaces:
      - tenant_setup_console
      - floor_cash_workspace
      - station_board
      - customer_ordering
      - identity_activation
      api_bases:
        tenant_internal_api: https://{tenant}.iotables.net/api/internal
        customer_public_api: https://{tenant}.iotables.net/api/public
        device_realtime: wss://{tenant}.iotables.net/ws/devices/tables/{tableId}
  rationale:
    selected_because:
    - Platform control plane and tenant operations solve different problems and have
      different risk profiles.
    - Separate frontend deployables reduce accidental route, session, and authorization
      coupling.
    - Platform UI must not expose tenant operational workflows.
    - Tenant UI must not contain platform lifecycle or provisioning controls.
    - Domain-level separation improves operational clarity and security isolation.
    rejected_alternatives:
    - id: single_nextjs_app_for_platform_and_tenant
      reason: Rejected because platform and tenant surfaces have different audiences,
        routes, permissions, and isolation requirements.
    - id: screen_level_split_inside_one_frontend
      reason: Rejected because screen-level separation is weaker than application-level
        separation.
  isolation_rules:
  - id: FAS-001
    rule: platform_web and tenant_web MUST be separate Next.js applications.
  - id: FAS-002
    rule: platform_web MUST be served only from platform.iotables.net.
  - id: FAS-003
    rule: tenant_web MUST be served only from tenant domains matching {tenant}.iotables.net.
  - id: FAS-004
    rule: platform_web MUST call only platform API routes under /api/platform/**.
  - id: FAS-005
    rule: tenant_web MUST NOT call platform API routes.
  - id: FAS-006
    rule: platform_web MUST NOT include tenant menu, order, station, checkout, or
      customer session screens.
  - id: FAS-007
    rule: tenant_web MUST NOT include tenant lifecycle, provisioning, schema job,
      platform settings, or platform audit screens.
  - id: FAS-008
    rule: Platform authentication state MUST be separate from tenant authentication
      state.
  - id: FAS-009
    rule: Platform cookies, tenant staff cookies, and customer guest continuity state
      MUST use separate names and scopes.
  - id: FAS-010
    rule: Shared UI code MAY exist only as framework-independent packages without
      product authorization behavior.
  shared_frontend_packages:
    allowed:
    - id: ui_primitives
      purpose: Shared low-level UI components without product-specific permissions
        or data access.
    - id: design_tokens
      purpose: Shared typography, spacing, color tokens, and Tailwind configuration
        primitives.
    - id: api_client_base
      purpose: Shared HTTP client primitives without embedded platform or tenant endpoint
        ownership.
    - id: validation_helpers
      purpose: Shared pure validation helpers.
    forbidden:
    - shared_authorization_logic_that_mixes_platform_and_tenant_permissions
    - shared_route_registry_for_platform_and_tenant_apps
    - shared_state_store_between_platform_and_tenant_apps
    - shared_customer_session_logic_inside_platform_web
  routing_contract:
    platform_web:
      root_domain: platform.iotables.net
      allowed_route_families:
      - /
      - /tenants/**
      - /jobs/**
      - /audit
      - /settings
      - /health
      forbidden_route_families:
      - /console/**
      - /app/**
      - /g/**
      - /menu
      - /cart
      - /stations/**
    tenant_web:
      root_domain: '{tenant}.iotables.net'
      allowed_route_families:
      - /console/**
      - /app/**
      - /g/{token}/**
      - /login
      - /change-password
      - /activate
      - /login-2fa
      forbidden_route_families:
      - /tenants/**
      - /jobs/**
      - /audit
      - /settings
      - /api/platform/**
  build_and_deployment:
    platform_web:
      deployable_name: iotable-platform-web
      build_scope: platform_web_only
      environment_scope: platform
    tenant_web:
      deployable_name: iotable-tenant-web
      build_scope: tenant_web_only
      environment_scope: tenant
    rules:
    - Platform and tenant frontend deployables MUST be buildable independently.
    - Platform and tenant frontend deployables MUST be deployable independently.
    - A platform_web deployment MUST NOT require rebuilding tenant_web.
    - A tenant_web deployment MUST NOT require rebuilding platform_web.
    - Both frontend deployables MAY consume shared frontend packages.
  open_decisions: []
  forbidden_behavior:
  - single_nextjs_app_for_all_surfaces
  - platform_routes_inside_tenant_web
  - tenant_operation_routes_inside_platform_web
  - shared_platform_and_tenant_auth_cookie
  - tenant_web_calling_platform_api
  - platform_web_calling_tenant_internal_api
  done_when:
  - frontend_deployables_are_defined
  - platform_web_boundary_is_defined
  - tenant_web_boundary_is_defined
  - frontend_isolation_rules_are_defined
  - shared_frontend_package_rules_are_defined
  - routing_contract_is_defined
  - build_and_deployment_rules_are_defined
  - forbidden_frontend_behavior_is_defined
technology_version_baseline:
  purpose:
    rule: Define the sealed runtime, framework, language, package-manager, and infrastructure
      version baseline for IoTable implementation.
    applies_to:
    - backend_runtime
    - frontend_runtime
    - frontend_framework
    - ui_runtime
    - language_tooling
    - package_management
    - database_runtime
    - deployment_environment
  baseline:
    backend:
      runtime: Python 3.14
      web_framework: FastAPI
      architecture_role: single_modular_monolith_backend
    frontend:
      application_framework: Next.js 16.2.4
      ui_runtime: React 19.2.5
      language: TypeScript 6.0.3
      styling: Tailwind CSS v4.0
      application_split:
      - platform_web
      - tenant_web
    node_tooling:
      runtime: Node.js 24 LTS
      package_manager: pnpm 11.0.3
    data:
      relational_database: PostgreSQL 17
    deployment:
      operating_system: Ubuntu VPS
  binding_rules:
  - id: TVB-001
    rule: IoTable backend implementation MUST use Python 3.14 and FastAPI.
  - id: TVB-002
    rule: IoTable frontend implementation MUST use Next.js 16.2.4 and React 19.2.5.
  - id: TVB-003
    rule: IoTable frontend implementation MUST use TypeScript 6.0.3.
  - id: TVB-004
    rule: IoTable frontend styling baseline MUST use Tailwind CSS v4.0.
  - id: TVB-005
    rule: IoTable Node tooling MUST use Node.js 24 LTS and pnpm 11.0.3.
  - id: TVB-006
    rule: IoTable persistent relational storage MUST use PostgreSQL 17.
  - id: TVB-007
    rule: IoTable frontend deployables MUST remain Next.js applications named platform_web
      and tenant_web.
  - id: TVB-008
    rule: A version change in this baseline MUST update every dependent architecture,
      implementation, testing, and deployment specification in the same specification
      change.
  forbidden_technology_substitutions:
  - id: TVB-FORBID-001
    substitution: Blazor
    reason: Blazor is not part of the owner-approved IoTable frontend baseline.
  - id: TVB-FORBID-002
    substitution: Razor UI
    reason: Razor UI is not part of the owner-approved IoTable frontend baseline.
  - id: TVB-FORBID-003
    substitution: single_frontend_app_for_platform_and_tenant
    reason: Frontend isolation is defined as separate Next.js platform_web and tenant_web
      applications.
  - id: TVB-FORBID-004
    substitution: frontend_framework_replacement_without_owner_decision
    reason: Replacing Next.js or React changes the owner-approved implementation baseline.
  - id: TVB-FORBID-005
    substitution: JavaScript (pure/vanilla JS)
    reason: Frontend codebase MUST use TypeScript 6.0.3 exclusively; vanilla or pure JavaScript files are strictly prohibited.
  source_alignment:
    authoritative_project_documents:
    - specs/02_system_architecture.yaml
    - specs/02_system_architecture.yaml
    - package.json
    reference_documents_used_as_inspiration:
    - C:\Users\Victus\Desktop\dr\2\docs\reference\architecture\technology-inventory.md
    - C:\Users\Victus\Desktop\dr\2\docs\reference\tunable-parameters.md
    reference_fit_rule: Reference documents may inspire inventory shape but MUST NOT
      override IoTable's Python plus Next.js plus React baseline.
  done_when:
  - backend_runtime_is_defined
  - frontend_framework_and_ui_runtime_are_defined
  - node_tooling_versions_are_defined
  - database_runtime_is_defined
  - forbidden_frontend_substitutions_are_defined
  - dependent_specifications_are_listed
background_cache_locking_decision:
  purpose:
    rule: Define the selected background job library, job storage, distributed cache,
      and distributed locking technologies for IoTable.
    applies_to:
    - app.worker
    - platform_provisioning_jobs
    - platform_schema_orchestration
    - customer_access_security
    - rate_limiting
    - runtime_coordination
  selected_technologies:
    background_jobs:
      library: Celery
      storage: Redis/PostgreSQL
      host: app.worker
      dashboard_policy: disabled_or_platform_owner_only
    distributed_cache:
      technology: Redis
      python_abstraction: redis-py
      package_family: redis
    distributed_locking:
      platform_jobs: PostgreSQL advisory locks
      rate_limit_and_ephemeral_coordination: Redis
  research_record:
    decision_id: BCL-DEC-001
    topic: Background jobs, distributed cache, and distributed locking
    status: accepted
    problem: IoTable requires durable background processing, ephemeral runtime coordination,
      and critical per-tenant concurrency protection without introducing microservices.
    selected_option: celery_postgresql_redis_postgresql_advisory_locks
    options:
    - id: celery_postgresql_redis_postgresql_advisory_locks
      description: Use Celery with Redis as broker and PostgreSQL as backend, Redis-py
        for distributed cache, PostgreSQL advisory locks for critical platform jobs,
        and Redis locks only for short-lived runtime coordination.
      advantages:
      - Keeps durable job state in PostgreSQL-backed infrastructure.
      - Supports recurring (Celery Beat), delayed, and task chain workflows.
      - Keeps critical tenant lifecycle and schema mutation locking close to platform
        database state.
      - Uses Redis only for ephemeral cache and coordination state.
      disadvantages:
      - Requires explicit IoTable platform job tables in PostgreSQL because Celery
        queues are not the application source of truth.
      - Requires advisory lock key discipline for per-tenant critical sections.
      fit_score: 5
      source_support:
      - SRC-BCL-001
      - SRC-BCL-002
      - SRC-BCL-003
      - SRC-BCL-004
    - id: celery_redis_locks_for_all_coordination
      description: Use Celery and Redis locks for both ephemeral and durable mutation
        coordination.
      advantages:
      - Uses one runtime coordination dependency.
      disadvantages:
      - Allows durable lifecycle mutation safety to depend on ephemeral infrastructure.
      - Conflicts with IoTable platform database source-of-truth requirements.
      fit_score: 2
      source_support:
      - SRC-BCL-002
      - SRC-BCL-004
    - id: custom_background_worker_without_celery
      description: Implement background job persistence, retry, scheduling, and inspection
        directly inside IoTable.
      advantages:
      - Avoids dependency on a background job framework.
      disadvantages:
      - Creates custom scheduling and retry infrastructure.
      - Increases implementation risk for LLM coding agents.
      fit_score: 1
      source_support:
      - SRC-BCL-001
    source_records:
    - source_id: SRC-BCL-001
      source_type: official_technical_documentation
      authority_level: high
      locator: https://docs.celeryq.dev/
      retrieved_at: '2026-05-24'
      used_for:
      - background_job_library_fit
      extracted_facts:
      - Celery provides persistent distributed task queue processing for Python applications.
      - Celery supports delay, retry, and task chains natively.
      limitations:
      - Celery documentation does not define IoTable product job visibility requirements.
      spec_impact:
      - app.worker uses Celery for background task processing.
    - source_id: SRC-BCL-002
      source_type: official_technical_documentation
      authority_level: high
      locator: https://redis-py.readthedocs.io/
      retrieved_at: '2026-05-24'
      used_for:
      - distributed_cache_abstraction
      extracted_facts:
      - Python applications interact with Redis through the official redis-py library.
      - redis-py supports connection pooling, pub/sub, and pipeline execution.
      limitations:
      - Caching documentation does not define durable application state ownership.
      spec_impact:
      - Redis is used through redis-py and treated as ephemeral infrastructure.
    - source_id: SRC-BCL-003
      source_type: official_technical_documentation
      authority_level: high
      locator: https://www.postgresql.org/docs/17/explicit-locking.html#ADVISORY-LOCKS
      retrieved_at: '2026-05-24'
      used_for:
      - critical_platform_job_locking
      extracted_facts:
      - PostgreSQL provides advisory locks that applications can use for application-defined
        locking.
      limitations:
      - PostgreSQL documentation defines locking primitives, not IoTable job semantics.
      spec_impact:
      - Critical platform tenant lifecycle and schema jobs use PostgreSQL advisory
        locks.
    - source_id: SRC-BCL-004
      source_type: official_technical_documentation
      authority_level: high
      locator: https://redis.io/docs/latest/develop/
      retrieved_at: '2026-05-24'
      used_for:
      - ephemeral_cache_and_runtime_coordination
      extracted_facts:
      - Redis is an in-memory data store used for caching and short-lived coordination
        patterns.
      limitations:
      - Redis documentation does not define IoTable durable state recovery requirements.
      spec_impact:
      - Redis is not the source of truth for IoTable platform or tenant operational
        state.
    fit_assessment:
      correctness: selected_option_keeps_durable_job_truth_in_platform_tables_and_postgresql
      security: selected_option_separates_ephemeral_cache_from_durable_lifecycle_state
      maintainability: selected_option_uses_documented_framework_and_database_primitives
      operational_simplicity: selected_option_avoids_custom_job_scheduler_and_microservice_split
      llm_implementability: selected_option_has_explicit_library_and_locking_boundaries
      performance: selected_option_uses_redis_for_short_lived_cache_and_postgresql_for_critical_serialisation
      developer_convenience: selected_option_uses_common_python_libraries
      cost_efficiency: selected_option_uses_postgresql_for_job_storage_and_critical_locks
    risks:
    - id: BCL-RISK-001
      risk: Celery broker queues may be mistaken for IoTable platform job source-of-truth
        tables.
      mitigation: IoTable platform job tables in PostgreSQL remain the application-facing
        source of truth.
    - id: BCL-RISK-002
      risk: Redis may be used for durable lifecycle state by mistake.
      mitigation: Redis durable state usage is forbidden by integration rules and
        forbidden behavior.
    - id: BCL-RISK-003
      risk: Advisory lock keys may collide if not derived deterministically.
      mitigation: Lock keys are derived from stable job type and tenant identifier.
  background_job_decision:
    selected: Celery
    storage: Redis/PostgreSQL
    used_for:
    - tenant_provisioning_jobs
    - tenant_schema_change_jobs
    - maintenance_jobs
    - scheduled_operational_tasks
    rules:
    - Background jobs MUST execute through app.worker Celery package.
    - Celery task broker MUST use Redis, and database backend MUST use PostgreSQL
      for task state durability.
    - Provisioning jobs MUST remain resumable and inspectable through platform APIs
      and platform UI.
    - Schema change jobs MUST remain resumable and inspectable through platform APIs
      and platform UI.
    - Celery dashboard (e.g. Flower) MUST NOT be publicly exposed.
    - If Flower dashboard is enabled, it MUST be protected by platform_owner authorization.
    - Application-facing job status MUST be represented in IoTable platform tables,
      not only in Celery internal broker state.
    rationale:
    - Celery is practical for persistent background processing in Python.
    - Celery supports delayed, recurring, and task chain workflows needed by IoTable.
    - PostgreSQL storage backend avoids introducing an additional durable job database.
    - IoTable requires operationally visible provisioning and schema job state.
  cache_decision:
    selected: Redis
    abstraction: redis-py
    used_for:
    - rate_limit_counters
    - abuse_detection_counters
    - short_lived_customer_access_runtime_state
    - ephemeral_runtime_coordination
    not_used_for:
    - source_of_truth_data
    - tenant_operational_data
    - platform_audit_log
    - provisioning_job_source_of_truth
    key_naming_conventions:
      pattern: "iotable:{context}:{scope}:{identifier}:{purpose}"
      standards:
      - id: rate_limiting
        template: "iotable:platform:rate_limit:{ip_address}"
        ttl: 60s
      - id: session_security
        template: "iotable:tenant:{tenantSlug}:session:{sessionId}"
        ttl: 86400s
      - id: table_order_lock
        template: "iotable:tenant:{tenantSlug}:table:{tableId}:lock"
        ttl: 10s
      - id: websocket_coordination
        template: "iotable:tenant:{tenantSlug}:ws:client:{clientId}"
        ttl: 300s
    rules:
    - Redis MUST NOT be the source of truth for platform lifecycle state.
    - Redis MUST NOT be the source of truth for tenant operational state.
    - Redis MAY store short-lived counters and runtime coordination state.
    - Redis entries MUST have explicit TTL unless a documented exception exists.
    - Critical state MUST be recoverable without Redis data.
    - All Redis keys MUST strictly follow the designated key naming pattern `iotable:{context}:{scope}:{identifier}:{purpose}` to prevent key collisions across platform and tenant boundaries.
  locking_decision:
    platform_job_locking:
      selected: PostgreSQL advisory locks
      used_for:
      - tenant_provisioning_job_exclusion
      - tenant_schema_change_job_exclusion
      - per_tenant_platform_job_serialisation
      rules:
      - Platform provisioning and schema jobs MUST use PostgreSQL advisory locks for
        critical per-tenant execution exclusion.
      - Advisory locks MUST be scoped by stable lock keys derived from job type and
        tenant identifier.
      - Critical lock acquisition and state transition MUST be atomic with platform
        database state checks where applicable.
      - The system MUST reject or defer concurrent platform jobs that would mutate
        the same tenant lifecycle or schema state.
    ephemeral_locking:
      selected: Redis
      used_for:
      - rate_limit_coordination
      - short_lived_abuse_control
      - non_critical_ephemeral_runtime_coordination
      rules:
      - Redis locks MUST NOT protect durable platform lifecycle mutations.
      - Redis locks MAY protect short-lived abuse-control flows.
      - Redis locks MUST use TTL.
  integration_rules:
  - id: BCL-001
    rule: app.worker MUST run background Celery tasks outside the HTTP request pipeline.
  - id: BCL-002
    rule: Platform job source-of-truth state MUST be stored in IoTable platform tables.
  - id: BCL-003
    rule: Celery internal broker state MUST NOT replace IoTable platform job contract
      tables.
  - id: BCL-004
    rule: Redis MUST be treated as ephemeral infrastructure.
  - id: BCL-005
    rule: PostgreSQL advisory locks MUST protect critical platform job concurrency.
  - id: BCL-006
    rule: Celery broker and result backend keys in Redis MUST strictly use the dedicated namespace prefixes `iotable:platform:broker:` and `iotable:platform:results:` to avoid namespace pollution and enforce multi-tenant isolation.
  - id: BCL-007
    rule: PostgreSQL advisory lock keys for platform jobs MUST be derived using a two-integer schema `pg_advisory_xact_lock(job_type_category_id, tenant_hash_id)`, where job_type_category_id is a static 32-bit enum (100 = Provisioning, 200 = Schema Migration, 300 = Backup) and tenant_hash_id is a MurmurHash3 32-bit hash of the UUID string, to prevent key collisions.
  - id: BCL-008
    rule: Distributed cache and lock failures MUST fail safely for security-sensitive
      customer flows.
  forbidden_behavior:
  - background_jobs_only_inside_app.main_request_pipeline
  - celery_dashboard_publicly_exposed
  - redis_as_source_of_truth_for_platform_state
  - redis_as_source_of_truth_for_tenant_operational_state
  - redis_lock_for_critical_tenant_schema_mutation_without_database_state_guard
  - platform_job_without_concurrency_guard
  open_decisions: []
  done_when:
  - background_job_library_is_selected
  - background_job_storage_is_selected
  - distributed_cache_is_selected
  - platform_job_locking_is_selected
  - ephemeral_locking_policy_is_defined
  - integration_rules_are_defined
  - forbidden_behavior_is_defined

infrastructure_and_dns_policy:
  purpose:
    rule: Define the reverse proxy configuration, wildcard domain routing, and automatic SSL/TLS certificate management rules for IoTable multi-tenancy.
    applies_to:
    - platform_control_plane
    - tenant_sites
    - dynamic_subdomain_resolution
    - ssl_certificate_provisioning
  reverse_proxy_selection:
    proxy_server: Caddy
    version: 2.7+
    reason: Caddy provides built-in On-Demand TLS out-of-the-box, allowing dynamic issuance of Let's Encrypt SSL certificates during runtime on the first TLS handshake of a new tenant, without requiring custom DNS challenge cron jobs or Nginx reload scripts.
    on_demand_tls_limit: 1000
    on_demand_ask_endpoint: https://platform.iotables.net/api/platform/tenants/validate-domain
  dns_wildcard_policy:
    dns_provider: Cloudflare
    wildcard_record: "*.iotables.net"
    record_type: CNAME
    ttl: 300
    rules:
    - A wildcard DNS record MUST point to the public IP of the Caddy reverse proxy.
    - Automated dynamic SSL certificate issuance MUST use Caddy's On-Demand TLS feature.
    - Caddy MUST call the platform tenant validation API endpoint /api/platform/tenants/validate-domain to verify the domain before issuing a TLS certificate.
    - Non-registered subdomains MUST be rejected immediately with HTTP 404 at the reverse proxy or during the TLS handshake.
  rules:
  - id: FAS-011
    rule: Multi-tenant SSL certificates MUST be provisioned dynamically and automatically using Caddy On-Demand TLS.
  - id: FAS-012
    rule: The reverse proxy MUST validate the existence of the tenant slug via the platform API before completing the TLS handshake.
  - id: FAS-013
    rule: Subdomain routing MUST map {tenant}.iotables.net/app/** and /console/** to the tenant_web Next.js application.
  - id: FAS-014
    rule: Platform routing MUST map platform.iotables.net/** to the platform_web Next.js application.

ui_design_system_tokens:
  purpose:
    rule: Define the core CSS variables and design tokens for the IoTable frontend to ensure aesthetic consistency and premium dark-mode support.
    applies_to:
    - platform_web
    - tenant_web
    - tailwind_config
  color_palette:
    brand:
      primary: "#6366f1"
      primary_hover: "#4f46e5"
      secondary: "#10b981"
      secondary_hover: "#059669"
    neutral_light_mode:
      background: "#ffffff"
      surface: "#f8fafc"
      text_primary: "#0f172a"
      text_secondary: "#475569"
      border: "#e2e8f0"
    neutral_dark_mode:
      background: "#09090b"
      surface: "#18181b"
      text_primary: "#fafafa"
      text_secondary: "#a1a1aa"
      border: "#27272a"
    accents:
      warning: "#f59e0b"
      danger: "#ef4444"
      info: "#0ea5e9"
  typography:
    sans_serif_font: "Inter, system-ui, sans-serif"
    heading_font: "Outfit, system-ui, sans-serif"
  spacing_scale:
    base_unit: 4px
    scale:
      xs: 4px
      sm: 8px
      md: 16px
      lg: 24px
      xl: 32px
      xxl: 48px
  glassmorphism:
    blur: 12px
    background_opacity: 0.6
    border_opacity: 0.1
  rules:
  - id: FAS-015
    rule: Frontend UI MUST dynamically resolve user theme preferences (light/dark mode) and store it locally.
  - id: FAS-016
    rule: Brand colors and background variables MUST strictly use HSL/Hex values defined in ui_design_system_tokens.
  - id: FAS-017
    rule: Interactive UI elements MUST use micro-animations with transition-duration of 200ms and ease-in-out timing function.

documents.title: IoTable Domain Model
documents.raw_text:
document:
  id: 03-domain.domain-model
  title: IoTable Domain Model
  project: IoTable
  version: 1.0.0
  language: en
  format: yaml
  schema_required: true
  schema_file: schemas/03_domain_model.schema.json
  status: draft
bill_session_workflow:
  purpose:
    rule: Define adisyon/bill session lifecycle, table bill ownership, order attachment,
      bill viewing, manual payment recording, bill closing, bill reopening, and forbidden
      payment-provider assumptions.
    applies_to:
    - customer_order_submit
    - staff_pda_order_submit
    - bill_session
    - cashier_workspace
    - floor_staff_workspace
    - tenant_operations
  bill_session_model:
    one_active_bill_per_table: true
    active_bill_scope: table
    payment_provider_integration: false
    manual_payment_recording: true
    customer_payment_collection: false
    rules:
    - A table MUST NOT have more than one active bill session.
    - A bill session belongs to exactly one table.
    - IoTable MUST NOT integrate online payment providers.
    - IoTable MUST record manual payment status only.
    - Customer-facing payment collection MUST NOT be supported.
  bill_lifecycle_states:
    allowed_states:
    - open
    - closing
    - closed
    - reopened
    - cancelled
    initial_state: open
    terminal_states:
    - closed
    - cancelled
    transitions:
    - from: none
      to: open
      trigger: first_order_for_table_or_staff_open_bill
    - from: open
      to: closing
      trigger: authorized_staff_starts_bill_close
    - from: closing
      to: closed
      trigger: manual_payment_recorded_and_close_confirmed
    - from: closed
      to: reopened
      trigger: authorized_manager_admin_owner_reopens_bill
    - from: reopened
      to: open
      trigger: reopen_completed
    - from: open
      to: cancelled
      trigger: authorized_cancel_when_no_submitted_orders_or_admin_exception
    rules:
    - Only open or reopened bill sessions MAY accept new orders.
    - Closed bill sessions MUST NOT accept new orders unless reopened.
    - Cancelled bill sessions MUST NOT accept new orders.
    - Reopened bill sessions MUST become open after reopen completion.
  order_attachment_policy:
    customer_order_submit_auto_opens_bill_if_none_exists: true
    staff_pda_order_submit_auto_opens_bill_if_none_exists: true
    customer_orders_attach_to_active_bill: true
    staff_pda_orders_attach_to_active_bill: true
    rules:
    - Customer order submit MUST attach the order to the table's active bill session.
    - Staff PDA order submit MUST attach the order to the table's active bill session.
    - If no active bill session exists for the table, order submit MUST atomically
      open one.
    - Opening a bill session and attaching the order MUST be part of the same transaction
      as order submit.
    - Order submit MUST fail if the table has a closing, closed, or cancelled bill
      session and no new active bill can be opened according to table state rules.
  commands:
  - id: OpenBillSessionForTable
    actor: system_or_authorized_staff
    required_permissions_when_staff_initiated:
    - tenant.bills.open
    emits:
    - BillSessionOpened
  - id: AttachOrderToBillSession
    actor: system
    emits:
    - OrderAttachedToBillSession
  - id: ViewBillSession
    actor: authorized_staff_or_active_customer_session
    emits:
    - BillSessionViewed
  - id: StartBillClose
    actor: authorized_staff
    required_permissions:
    - tenant.bills.close
    emits:
    - BillSessionClosingStarted
  - id: RecordManualPayment
    actor: authorized_staff
    required_permissions:
    - tenant.manual_payments.record
    emits:
    - ManualPaymentRecorded
  - id: CloseBillSession
    actor: authorized_staff
    required_permissions:
    - tenant.bills.close
    emits:
    - BillSessionClosed
  - id: ReopenBillSession
    actor: tenant_manager_or_tenant_admin_or_tenant_owner
    required_permissions:
    - tenant.bills.reopen
    emits:
    - BillSessionReopened
  workflows:
    order_submit_bill_attachment:
      trigger:
      - customer_order_submit
      - staff_pda_order_submit
      transaction: same_transaction_as_order_submit
      steps:
      - resolve table
      - lock table active bill session lookup
      - open bill session if none exists
      - attach order to active bill session
      - recalculate bill totals
      rules:
      - Order submit and bill attachment MUST be atomic.
      - Bill total recalculation MUST use server-side order item snapshots.
      - Client-provided bill totals MUST NOT be trusted.
    customer_bill_view:
      actor: active_customer_session
      requires:
      - active_customer_session
      - session_table_matches_bill_table
      returns:
      - bill summary
      - attached submitted orders
      - order item snapshots
      - manual payment status if closed or closing
      rules:
      - Customer bill view MUST require active customer session.
      - Stale QR MUST NOT reveal bill state.
      - Customer bill view MUST only show the bill for the session table.
    staff_bill_view:
      actor: authorized_staff
      required_permissions:
      - tenant.bills.view
      returns:
      - bill detail
      - orders
      - order items
      - station status summary
      - manual payment records
      rules:
      - Staff bill view MUST be permission-protected.
      - Staff bill view MUST execute only inside the resolved tenant database.
    bill_close:
      actor: authorized_cashier_manager_admin_owner
      required_permissions:
      - tenant.bills.close
      - tenant.manual_payments.record
      validation:
      - bill_is_open_or_reopened
      - all_non_cancelled_orders_attached
      - manual_payment_amount_covers_or_matches_bill_total
      - payment_method_recorded
      result:
      - manual_payment_record_created
      - bill_status_closed
      - table_bill_state_cleared_or_marked_closed
      rules:
      - Bill close MUST record manual payment; it MUST NOT process a real payment
        provider charge.
      - Bill close MUST be audited.
      - Closed bill MUST NOT accept new orders unless reopened.
    bill_reopen:
      actor: tenant_manager_or_tenant_admin_or_tenant_owner
      required_permissions:
      - tenant.bills.reopen
      validation:
      - bill_is_closed
      - table_has_no_other_active_bill_session
      - reason_present
      result:
      - bill_status_reopened_then_open
      - reopen_audit_record_created
      rules:
      - Bill reopen MUST require reason.
      - Bill reopen MUST be limited to tenant_manager, tenant_admin, or tenant_owner
        through fixed role-permission bundles.
      - Bill reopen MUST be audited.
  manual_payment_policy:
    provider_payment_supported: false
    manual_payment_required_to_close: true
    partial_payment_supported: false
    overpayment_allowed: false
    payment_methods:
    - cash
    - card_external_pos
    - bank_transfer
    - other
    rules:
    - Manual payment record MUST represent an external/offline payment event.
    - Manual payment record MUST NOT claim that IoTable processed the payment.
    - IoTable MUST require one manual payment record matching bill total to close.
    - Partial payments MUST NOT be supported.
    - Overpayments MUST NOT be supported.
  authorization_policy:
    view_bill:
      staff_permission: tenant.bills.view
      customer_condition: active_customer_session_for_same_table
    close_bill:
      required_permissions:
      - tenant.bills.close
      - tenant.manual_payments.record
    reopen_bill:
      required_permission: tenant.bills.reopen
      allowed_roles:
      - tenant_owner
      - tenant_admin
      - tenant_manager
    rules:
    - Customer bill view MUST be session-scoped.
    - Staff bill operations MUST be permission-scoped.
    - Accountant role MAY view bills and manual payments but MUST NOT close or reopen
      bills.
  bill_total_policy:
    source_of_truth: server_side_order_item_snapshots
    client_total_trusted: false
    recalculation_required_on:
    - order_attached
    - order_cancelled
    - bill_close_started
    - bill_close_confirmed
    rules:
    - Bill totals MUST be calculated from non-cancelled order items attached to the
      bill.
    - Client-provided totals MUST NOT be trusted.
    - Cancelled order items MUST be excluded from payable total according to cancellation
      policy.
  customer_visibility_policy:
    stale_qr_bill_visibility: false
    active_session_bill_visibility: true
    after_order_submit_requires_new_fresh_qr: true
    rules:
    - Stale QR MUST NOT reveal whether a bill exists.
    - Customer bill visibility MUST require active customer session.
    - After order submit, the customer session is offline and bill view requires joining
      again with a fresh QR.
  anti_abuse_rules:
  - One table MUST NOT have more than one active bill session.
  - Bill close MUST be staff-authorized.
  - Bill reopen MUST require manager/admin/owner authority and reason.
  - Customer stale QR MUST NOT reveal bill state.
  - Client totals MUST NOT be trusted.
  - Payment provider processing MUST NOT be implied by manual payment records.
  forbidden_behavior:
  - multiple_active_bills_per_table
  - customer_stale_qr_bill_view
  - bill_close_without_manual_payment_record
  - bill_close_by_unauthorized_staff
  - bill_reopen_by_cashier_or_floor_staff
  - bill_reopen_without_reason
  - closed_bill_accepting_new_orders_without_reopen
  - client_provided_bill_total_as_source_of_truth
  - payment_provider_charge
  - partial_payments
  - overpayments
  open_decisions: []
  done_when:
  - bill_session_model_is_defined
  - bill_lifecycle_states_are_defined
  - order_attachment_policy_is_defined
  - bill_commands_are_defined
  - order_submit_bill_attachment_workflow_is_defined
  - customer_bill_view_workflow_is_defined
  - staff_bill_view_workflow_is_defined
  - bill_close_workflow_is_defined
  - bill_reopen_workflow_is_defined
  - manual_payment_policy_is_defined
  - authorization_policy_is_defined
  - bill_total_policy_is_defined
  - customer_visibility_policy_is_defined
  - anti_abuse_rules_are_defined
  - forbidden_bill_behavior_is_defined
customer_order_session_workflow:
  purpose:
    rule: Define customer QR access, fresh QR session join, customer session lifetime,
      cart mutation, bill view, order submission, session expiration, and anti-manipulation
      rules.
    applies_to:
    - customer_menu_routes
    - customer_qr_access
    - customer_sessions
    - customer_cart
    - order_submission
    - bill_view
    - table_qr_security
  access_model:
    stale_qr:
      menu_access: true
      bill_access: false
      cart_mutation: false
      order_submit: false
      session_join: false
    fresh_qr:
      menu_access: true
      bill_access: true
      cart_mutation: true
      order_submit: true
      session_join: true
    rules:
    - Stale QR MUST allow menu viewing only.
    - Stale QR MUST NOT reveal active bill/adisyon state.
    - Stale QR MUST NOT allow customer session join.
    - Stale QR MUST NOT allow cart mutation.
    - Stale QR MUST NOT allow order submission.
    - Fresh QR MUST be required to join or create a customer session.
    - Fresh QR MUST be required before bill view, cart mutation, and order submit
      become available.
  session_lifetime:
    initial_ttl_minutes: 10
    extension_allowed: true
    extension_duration_minutes: 10
    max_extensions: 3
    max_total_lifetime_minutes: 40
    extension_requires_active_session: true
    extension_requires_recent_activity: true
    extension_requires_fresh_qr: false
    passive_auto_extend_allowed: false
    background_extend_allowed: false
    user_visible_extend_action_required: true
    expires_on_order_submit: true
    rules:
    - A fresh QR joined customer session MUST initially be active for 10 minutes.
    - An active customer session MAY be extended by explicit user-visible action.
    - Each extension MUST add at most 10 minutes.
    - A customer session MUST NOT be extended more than 3 times.
    - A customer session MUST NOT exceed 40 minutes total lifetime.
    - A session extension MUST require an active session.
    - A session extension MUST require recent user activity.
    - A session extension MUST NOT require scanning a new fresh QR.
    - A session MUST NOT be passively auto-extended in the background.
    - A session MUST move offline immediately after successful order submission.
  session_states:
    allowed_states:
    - active
    - offline
    - expired
    - submitted
    transitions:
    - from: none
      to: active
      trigger: fresh_qr_session_joined
    - from: active
      to: active
      trigger: explicit_user_extension_within_limits
    - from: active
      to: submitted
      trigger: order_submit_succeeded
    - from: submitted
      to: offline
      trigger: post_submit_session_shutdown
    - from: active
      to: expired
      trigger: ttl_expired
    - from: expired
      to: offline
      trigger: session_cleanup
    rules:
    - Only active sessions MAY view bill, mutate cart, or submit order.
    - Offline, expired, and submitted sessions MUST NOT view bill, mutate cart, or
      submit order.
    - A submitted session MUST be treated as offline for further customer actions.
  commands:
  - id: OpenPublicMenuFromQr
    authentication: none
    fresh_qr_required: false
    emits:
    - PublicMenuViewed
  - id: JoinCustomerSessionWithFreshQr
    authentication: fresh_qr_token
    fresh_qr_required: true
    emits:
    - CustomerSessionJoined
    - FreshQrConsumed
    - TableQrRotated
  - id: ExtendCustomerSession
    authentication: active_customer_session
    fresh_qr_required: false
    emits:
    - CustomerSessionExtended
  - id: ViewBillFromCustomerSession
    authentication: active_customer_session
    fresh_qr_required: session_must_have_originated_from_fresh_qr
    emits:
    - CustomerBillViewed
  - id: MutateCustomerCart
    authentication: active_customer_session
    fresh_qr_required: session_must_have_originated_from_fresh_qr
    emits:
    - CustomerCartMutated
  - id: SubmitCustomerOrder
    authentication: active_customer_session
    fresh_qr_required: session_must_have_originated_from_fresh_qr
    emits:
    - CustomerOrderSubmitted
    - CustomerSessionSubmitted
    - CustomerSessionMovedOffline
  workflows:
    public_menu_access:
      input: any_valid_table_qr_or_menu_url
      result: menu_visible_without_bill_or_cart_access
      rules:
      - Menu viewing MUST be available from stale QR.
      - Menu viewing MUST NOT imply session access.
      - Menu viewing MUST NOT expose active bill/adisyon contents.
    fresh_qr_session_join:
      input: fresh_qr_token
      validation:
      - token_exists
      - token_not_expired
      - token_matches_tenant_and_table
      - tenant_lifecycle_active
      - tenant_operations_active
      - table_active
      result:
      - customer_session_active
      - session_expiry_set_to_now_plus_10_minutes
      - fresh_qr_consumed
      - table_qr_rotated
      rules:
      - Fresh QR token MUST be single-use for session join.
      - After successful consume, server MUST rotate table QR.
    session_extension:
      input: active_customer_session
      validation:
      - session_active
      - session_not_submitted
      - session_not_expired
      - extension_count_less_than_3
      - total_lifetime_less_than_40_minutes
      - recent_user_activity_present
      - explicit_user_visible_action_present
      result:
      - session_expiry_extended_by_10_minutes_or_to_max_total_lifetime
      - extension_count_incremented
      rules:
      - Session extension MUST be explicit and user-visible.
      - Session extension MUST NOT happen from passive background timers.
    cart_mutation:
      input: active_customer_session
      validation:
      - session_active
      - menu_item_exists
      - menu_item_active
      - menu_item_orderable
      - menu_item_has_price
      - menu_item_has_station_routing
      result:
      - cart_updated
      rules:
      - Cart mutation MUST require active customer session.
      - Cart mutation MUST NOT be allowed from stale QR menu access.
    order_submit:
      input: active_customer_session_with_cart
      validation:
      - session_active
      - cart_not_empty
      - all_items_orderable
      - all_items_have_current_price
      - all_items_have_active_station_routing
      - tenant_operations_active
      - table_active
      result:
      - order_created
      - station_tickets_created_or_queued
      - cart_cleared_or_locked
      - session_moved_offline
      rules:
      - Successful order submit MUST immediately move the customer session offline.
      - After order submit, viewing bill, mutating cart, or submitting another order
        MUST require joining again with a fresh QR.
  anti_manipulation_rules:
  - A customer session MUST be short-lived by default.
  - A customer session MUST NOT remain active after order submission.
  - A customer session MUST NOT be extended silently.
  - A customer session MUST NOT exceed 40 minutes total lifetime.
  - A stale QR MUST NOT allow a customer to discover whether an active bill exists.
  - A stale QR MUST NOT allow joining an existing customer session.
  - Fresh QR consume MUST rotate the server-side QR token.
  - Order submit MUST revalidate menu item price and station routing at submit time.
  customer_identity_policy:
    login_required: false
    phone_required: false
    name_required: false
    rules:
    - Customer ordering MUST NOT require customer login in the initial product version.
    - Customer ordering MUST NOT require phone number in the initial product version.
    - Customer ordering MUST be protected by fresh QR session access instead of customer
      identity.
  domain_events:
  - PublicMenuViewed
  - FreshQrConsumed
  - TableQrRotated
  - CustomerSessionJoined
  - CustomerSessionExtended
  - CustomerSessionExpired
  - CustomerBillViewed
  - CustomerCartMutated
  - CustomerOrderSubmitted
  - CustomerSessionSubmitted
  - CustomerSessionMovedOffline
  forbidden_behavior:
  - stale_qr_bill_access
  - stale_qr_session_join
  - stale_qr_cart_mutation
  - stale_qr_order_submit
  - passive_customer_session_auto_extend
  - background_customer_session_extend
  - customer_session_lifetime_above_40_minutes
  - customer_session_extension_count_above_3
  - customer_session_remaining_active_after_order_submit
  - cart_mutation_without_active_customer_session
  - order_submit_without_active_customer_session
  - order_submit_without_revalidating_price_and_station_routing
  - customer_login_required_initial_version
  - customer_phone_required_initial_version
  open_decisions: []
  done_when:
  - customer_qr_access_model_is_defined
  - customer_session_lifetime_is_defined
  - customer_session_states_are_defined
  - customer_commands_are_defined
  - public_menu_access_workflow_is_defined
  - fresh_qr_session_join_workflow_is_defined
  - session_extension_workflow_is_defined
  - cart_mutation_workflow_is_defined
  - order_submit_workflow_is_defined
  - anti_manipulation_rules_are_defined
  - customer_identity_policy_is_defined
  - domain_events_are_defined
  - forbidden_customer_session_behavior_is_defined
module_inventory:
  purpose:
    rule: Define the complete business capability module inventory used to structure
      IoTable architecture, data ownership, API ownership, UI surface ownership, and
      implementation planning.
    partitioning_method: business_capability_boundary
    forbidden_primary_partitioning_methods:
    - frontend_backend_split
    - stakeholder_split
    - screen_split
    - route_split
    rule_detail: Frontend, backend, data, API, tests, and operations are implementation
      aspects inside capability modules.
  module_classification:
    platform_scope: Module owns platform-level control plane behavior or platform
      database data.
    tenant_scope: Module owns tenant operational behavior or tenant database data.
    shared_scope: Module provides cross-cutting behavior used by platform and tenant
      modules.
    external_scope: Module represents out-of-system behavior or explicitly excluded
      integration.
  modules:
  - id: platform_tenant_lifecycle
    name: Platform Tenant Lifecycle
    scope: platform_scope
    responsibility: Own tenant registry, tenant domain records, lifecycle status,
      provisioning initiation, suspension, deactivation, and reactivation.
    owns_data:
    - tenant_registry
    - tenant_domain_registry
    - tenant_lifecycle_status
    owns_surfaces:
    - platform_control_plane
    owns_api_families:
    - platform_api
    depends_on:
    - platform_identity_access
    - platform_provisioning_jobs
    - platform_audit
    done_when:
    - tenant_lifecycle_rules_defined
    - tenant_status_transitions_defined
    - tenant_registry_contracts_defined
  - id: platform_provisioning_jobs
    name: Platform Provisioning Jobs
    scope: platform_scope
    responsibility: Own tenant database creation, schema application, seed execution,
      activation invite creation, provisioning job tracking, and resumable provisioning
      failure handling.
    owns_data:
    - tenant_provisioning_jobs
    - tenant_database_registry
    - provisioning_job_runs
    owns_surfaces:
    - platform_control_plane
    owns_api_families:
    - platform_api
    depends_on:
    - platform_tenant_lifecycle
    - platform_database_schema_orchestration
    - platform_audit
    done_when:
    - provisioning_flow_defined
    - failure_and_retry_policy_defined
    - job_visibility_defined
  - id: platform_database_schema_orchestration
    name: Platform Database Schema Orchestration
    scope: platform_scope
    responsibility: Own platform-level execution of tenant database schema change
      jobs, per-tenant schema version inspection, failure isolation, and resumable
      schema updates.
    owns_data:
    - tenant_schema_change_jobs
    - tenant_schema_change_job_runs
    - tenant_schema_versions
    owns_surfaces:
    - platform_control_plane
    owns_api_families:
    - platform_api
    depends_on:
    - platform_tenant_lifecycle
    - platform_audit
    done_when:
    - tenant_schema_change_job_model_defined
    - per_tenant_version_tracking_defined
    - resumability_defined
  - id: platform_identity_access
    name: Platform Identity and Access
    scope: platform_scope
    responsibility: Own platform users, platform roles, platform permissions, authentication
      integration, and platform authorization enforcement.
    owns_data:
    - platform_users
    - platform_roles
    - platform_permissions
    - platform_user_role_assignments
    owns_surfaces:
    - identity_activation
    - platform_control_plane
    owns_api_families:
    - platform_api
    depends_on:
    - shared_authentication_runtime
    - platform_audit
    done_when:
    - platform_permission_catalog_defined
    - platform_role_permission_matrix_defined
    - platform_authentication_flow_defined
  - id: tenant_identity_access
    name: Tenant Identity and Access
    scope: tenant_scope
    responsibility: Own tenant users, tenant roles, tenant permissions, tenant activation,
      tenant login behavior, and tenant authorization enforcement.
    owns_data:
    - tenant_users
    - tenant_roles
    - tenant_permissions
    - tenant_user_role_assignments
    owns_surfaces:
    - identity_activation
    - tenant_setup_console
    - floor_cash_workspace
    - station_board
    owns_api_families:
    - tenant_internal_api
    depends_on:
    - shared_authentication_runtime
    - tenant_audit
    done_when:
    - tenant_permission_catalog_defined
    - tenant_role_permission_matrix_defined
    - tenant_activation_flow_defined
  - id: shared_authentication_runtime
    name: Shared Authentication Runtime
    scope: shared_scope
    responsibility: Own FastAPI Users, PyJWT, token issuing, token validation,
      login, password change, 2FA, and policy authorization runtime rules.
    owns_data:
    - identity_users
    - identity_credentials
    - oidc_applications
    - oidc_tokens
    - auth_sessions
    owns_surfaces:
    - identity_activation
    owns_api_families:
    - platform_api
    - tenant_internal_api
    depends_on: []
    done_when:
    - authentication_stack_contracts_defined
    - token_claim_contracts_defined
    - policy_authorization_contracts_defined
  - id: tenant_setup_configuration
    name: Tenant Setup and Configuration
    scope: tenant_scope
    responsibility: Own tenant-level settings required before operation, including
      restaurant profile, operational preferences, table setup, station setup, and
      initial configuration readiness.
    owns_data:
    - tenant_settings
    - restaurant_profile
    - setup_completion_state
    owns_surfaces:
    - tenant_setup_console
    owns_api_families:
    - tenant_internal_api
    depends_on:
    - tenant_identity_access
    - tenant_tables
    - tenant_stations
    - tenant_menu_catalog
    done_when:
    - setup_required_fields_defined
    - setup_completion_rules_defined
    - configuration_validation_defined
  - id: tenant_tables
    name: Tenant Tables
    scope: tenant_scope
    responsibility: Own restaurant table records, table identifiers, table display
      numbers, table operational state, and table availability used by floor, customer,
      and device flows.
    owns_data:
    - tables
    - table_numbers
    - table_operational_state
    owns_surfaces:
    - tenant_setup_console
    - floor_cash_workspace
    owns_api_families:
    - tenant_internal_api
    - customer_public_api
    depends_on:
    - tenant_identity_access
    done_when:
    - table_identity_contract_defined
    - table_state_contract_defined
    - table_number_rules_defined
  - id: tenant_stations
    name: Tenant Stations
    scope: tenant_scope
    responsibility: Own preparation station records, station codes, station active
      state, station assignment eligibility, and station board visibility.
    owns_data:
    - stations
    - station_codes
    - station_active_state
    owns_surfaces:
    - tenant_setup_console
    - station_board
    owns_api_families:
    - tenant_internal_api
    depends_on:
    - tenant_identity_access
    done_when:
    - station_identity_contract_defined
    - station_code_stability_rule_defined
    - station_active_state_rules_defined
  - id: tenant_menu_catalog
    name: Tenant Menu Catalog
    scope: tenant_scope
    responsibility: Own menu categories, menu items, prices, item availability, orderability,
      and menu item station assignment.
    owns_data:
    - menu_categories
    - menu_items
    - menu_item_prices
    - menu_item_availability
    - menu_item_station_assignment
    owns_surfaces:
    - tenant_setup_console
    - customer_ordering
    owns_api_families:
    - tenant_internal_api
    - customer_public_api
    depends_on:
    - tenant_stations
    - tenant_identity_access
    done_when:
    - menu_item_contract_defined
    - price_rules_defined
    - station_assignment_required_for_orderable_items
  - id: customer_qr_presence
    name: Customer QR Presence
    scope: tenant_scope
    responsibility: Own server-generated rotating QR tokens, token hashing, token
      validation, single-use token behavior, invalid QR recovery pages, and presence
      proof for customer actions.
    owns_data:
    - qr_token_registry
    - qr_token_hashes
    - qr_rotation_state
    - qr_invalid_attempt_log
    owns_surfaces:
    - customer_ordering
    - table_device_endpoint
    owns_api_families:
    - customer_public_api
    depends_on:
    - tenant_tables
    - tenant_customer_sessions
    - tenant_audit
    done_when:
    - qr_rotation_registry_design_defined
    - atomic_consume_rules_defined
    - invalid_qr_recovery_behavior_defined
  - id: tenant_customer_sessions
    name: Tenant Customer Sessions
    scope: tenant_scope
    responsibility: Own customer guest access, customer session continuity, customer
      relationship to table context, and customer ability to use menu, cart, order,
      and bill views.
    owns_data:
    - guest_sessions
    - guest_session_table_context
    - guest_session_bill_context
    owns_surfaces:
    - customer_ordering
    owns_api_families:
    - customer_public_api
    depends_on:
    - customer_qr_presence
    - tenant_tables
    status: defined
    source_documents:
    - specs/03_domain_model.yaml
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
    done_when:
    - customer_session_authority_defined
    - bill_membership_rules_defined
    - guest_continuity_rules_defined
  - id: tenant_cart
    name: Tenant Cart
    scope: tenant_scope
    responsibility: Own customer cart state, cart item mutation, cart validation against
      current menu and station configuration, and conversion from cart to submitted
      order.
    owns_data:
    - carts
    - cart_items
    - cart_validation_state
    owns_surfaces:
    - customer_ordering
    owns_api_families:
    - customer_public_api
    depends_on:
    - tenant_customer_sessions
    - customer_qr_presence
    - tenant_menu_catalog
    done_when:
    - cart_storage_authority_defined
    - cart_mutation_authorization_defined
    - cart_to_order_conversion_defined
  - id: tenant_ordering
    name: Tenant Ordering
    scope: tenant_scope
    responsibility: Own submitted orders, order items, item status, order item lifecycle,
      order cancellation before station acceptance, and customer order tracking.
    owns_data:
    - orders
    - order_items
    - order_item_status_history
    - order_cancellations
    owns_surfaces:
    - customer_ordering
    - floor_cash_workspace
    owns_api_families:
    - customer_public_api
    - tenant_internal_api
    depends_on:
    - tenant_cart
    - tenant_bill_sessions
    - tenant_station_routing
    - tenant_audit
    done_when:
    - order_submission_contract_defined
    - order_item_lifecycle_defined
    - order_cancellation_rules_defined
  - id: tenant_station_routing
    name: Tenant Station Routing
    scope: tenant_scope
    responsibility: Own order item routing to stations, station ticket creation, station
      ticket lifecycle, station accepted/preparing/ready states, and station board
      operational workflow.
    owns_data:
    - station_tickets
    - station_ticket_items
    - station_ticket_status_history
    owns_surfaces:
    - station_board
    - floor_cash_workspace
    - customer_ordering
    owns_api_families:
    - tenant_internal_api
    - customer_public_api
    depends_on:
    - tenant_ordering
    - tenant_stations
    - tenant_menu_catalog
    source_documents:
    - specs/03_domain_model.yaml
    done_when:
    - station_ticket_creation_rules_defined
    - station_status_flow_defined
    - station_visibility_defined
  - id: tenant_bill_sessions
    name: Tenant Bill Sessions
    scope: tenant_scope
    responsibility: Own table-based adisyon sessions, bill totals, bill item aggregation,
      bill visibility, and bill closure after external manual payment.
    owns_data:
    - bill_sessions
    - bill_session_items
    - bill_totals
    - bill_closure_records
    owns_surfaces:
    - customer_ordering
    - floor_cash_workspace
    owns_api_families:
    - customer_public_api
    - tenant_internal_api
    depends_on:
    - tenant_tables
    - tenant_ordering
    - tenant_checkout
    done_when:
    - bill_session_creation_rule_defined
    - bill_total_calculation_defined
    - bill_closure_rule_defined
  - id: tenant_checkout
    name: Tenant Checkout
    scope: tenant_scope
    responsibility: Own manual payment received records, manual payment method tracking,
      checkout state, and cashier-driven bill close workflow.
    owns_data:
    - checkout_records
    - manual_payment_records
    - payment_method_labels
    owns_surfaces:
    - floor_cash_workspace
    owns_api_families:
    - tenant_internal_api
    depends_on:
    - tenant_bill_sessions
    - tenant_identity_access
    - tenant_audit
    explicit_non_goals:
    - online_payment_collection
    - payment_gateway_integration
    - pos_integration
    - fiscal_device_integration
    - e_invoice_or_e_archive_generation
    done_when:
    - manual_payment_record_contract_defined
    - bill_close_authorization_defined
    - non_goals_preserved
  - id: tenant_floor_service
    name: Tenant Floor Service
    scope: tenant_scope
    responsibility: Own floor staff view of tables, ready items, served marking, service
      progress, and floor-level intervention before privileged escalation.
    owns_data:
    - served_mark_records
    - floor_service_events
    owns_surfaces:
    - floor_cash_workspace
    owns_api_families:
    - tenant_internal_api
    depends_on:
    - tenant_tables
    - tenant_ordering
    - tenant_station_routing
    - tenant_identity_access
    done_when:
    - served_marking_rules_defined
    - floor_visibility_rules_defined
    - floor_intervention_rules_defined
  - id: tenant_audit
    name: Tenant Audit
    scope: tenant_scope
    responsibility: Own tenant-scoped audit records for sensitive operational changes,
      privileged cancellation, bill closure, security-relevant customer QR behavior,
      and administrative actions.
    owns_data:
    - tenant_audit_log
    owns_surfaces:
    - tenant_setup_console
    - floor_cash_workspace
    owns_api_families:
    - tenant_internal_api
    depends_on:
    - tenant_identity_access
    done_when:
    - tenant_audit_event_catalog_defined
    - tenant_audit_retention_defined
    - sensitive_action_audit_rules_defined
  - id: platform_audit
    name: Platform Audit
    scope: platform_scope
    responsibility: Own platform-scoped audit records for tenant lifecycle changes,
      provisioning jobs, schema change jobs, platform user actions, and support access.
    owns_data:
    - platform_audit_log
    owns_surfaces:
    - platform_control_plane
    owns_api_families:
    - platform_api
    depends_on:
    - platform_identity_access
    done_when:
    - platform_audit_event_catalog_defined
    - platform_audit_retention_defined
    - support_action_audit_rules_defined
  - id: table_device_qr_display
    name: Table Device QR Display
    scope: tenant_scope
    responsibility: Own ESP32 TFT QR display behavior, device connection route, device
      authentication, QR refresh display loop, and device health visibility.
    owns_data:
    - table_devices
    - device_keys
    - device_bindings
    - device_heartbeat
    owns_surfaces:
    - table_device_endpoint
    - tenant_setup_console
    owns_api_families:
    - tenant_internal_api
    depends_on:
    - tenant_tables
    - customer_qr_presence
    status: defined
    source_documents:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
    done_when:
    - device_identity_model_defined
    - device_binding_rules_defined
    - qr_display_refresh_protocol_defined
  - id: technical_operations
    name: Technical Operations
    scope: shared_scope
    responsibility: Own health checks, readiness checks, deployment operations, recovery
      procedures, backup and restore procedures, secret rotation, and operational
      maintenance metadata.
    owns_data:
    - technical_operations_metadata
    - health_check_results
    - maintenance_records
    owns_surfaces:
    - technical_operations
    owns_api_families:
    - platform_api
    - tenant_internal_api
    depends_on:
    - platform_tenant_lifecycle
    - platform_database_schema_orchestration
    done_when:
    - health_checks_defined
    - backup_restore_policy_defined
    - deployment_topology_defined
  - id: shared_observability
    name: Shared Observability
    scope: shared_scope
    responsibility: Own structured logging, metrics, tracing conventions, correlation
      identifiers, error reporting, and operational visibility standards.
    owns_data:
    - logs
    - metrics
    - traces
    - correlation_ids
    owns_surfaces:
    - technical_operations
    owns_api_families: []
    depends_on: []
    done_when:
    - structured_logging_policy_defined
    - metric_catalog_defined
    - correlation_id_policy_defined
  - id: shared_rate_limiting
    name: Shared Rate Limiting
    scope: shared_scope
    responsibility: Own rate limits for public customer APIs, QR endpoints, device
