      endpoints, auth endpoints, and abuse-sensitive operations.
    owns_data:
    - rate_limit_counters
    - abuse_detection_events
    owns_surfaces:
    - customer_ordering
    - table_device_endpoint
    - identity_activation
    owns_api_families:
    - customer_public_api
    - tenant_internal_api
    - platform_api
    depends_on:
    - shared_observability
    done_when:
    - rate_limit_dimensions_defined
    - qr_abuse_limits_defined
    - auth_rate_limits_defined
  external_or_excluded_modules:
  - id: external_payment_gateway
    status: excluded
    reason: IoTable does not collect payments.
  - id: external_pos_device
    status: excluded
    reason: IoTable does not integrate with POS devices.
  - id: external_fiscal_device
    status: excluded
    reason: IoTable does not integrate with fiscal devices.
  - id: external_e_invoice_or_archive
    status: excluded
    reason: IoTable does not generate e-invoice or e-archive documents.
  module_dependency_rules:
  - id: MDR-001
    rule: A module MUST own its primary data contracts.
  - id: MDR-002
    rule: A module MUST NOT write another module's owned data except through that
      module's defined API or application service contract.
  - id: MDR-003
    rule: Platform-scope modules MUST NOT directly own tenant operational data.
  - id: MDR-004
    rule: Tenant-scope modules MUST NOT directly own platform control-plane data.
  - id: MDR-005
    rule: Shared modules MUST provide infrastructure behavior without owning business
      decisions.
  - id: MDR-006
    rule: Excluded modules MUST remain out of implementation scope unless a new owner
      decision changes the product baseline.
  open_decisions: []
  done_when:
  - all_major_business_capability_modules_are_defined
  - platform_scope_modules_are_defined
  - tenant_scope_modules_are_defined
  - shared_scope_modules_are_defined
  - excluded_modules_are_defined
  - module_data_ownership_is_defined
  - module_surface_ownership_is_defined
  - module_api_family_ownership_is_defined
  - module_dependencies_are_defined
  - open_module_decisions_are_recorded
station_order_routing:
  purpose:
    rule: Define how submitted customer orders are split, routed, accepted, prepared,
      completed, served, and cancelled across tenant stations.
    applies_to:
    - customer_ordering
    - station_board
    - floor_cash_workspace
    - tenant_setup_console
    - tenant_internal_api
    - customer_public_api
  core_model:
    order:
      role: Customer-submitted commercial request grouped under a table bill session.
      owns:
      - order_items
      routed_to_station: false
    order_item:
      role: Smallest routable preparation unit.
      routed_to_station: true
      station_assignment_source: menu_item_configuration
    station_ticket:
      role: Station-specific work package created from order items belonging to the
        same submitted order and same station.
      creation_rule: One station ticket MUST be created per target station per submitted
        order when that order contains one or more items for the station.
  routing_rules:
    routing_unit: order_item
    menu_item_requires_station: true
    station_assignment_source: menu_item_configuration
    order_can_split_into_multiple_station_tickets: true
    order_split_strategy: group_order_items_by_station
    station_ticket_created_per_station_per_order: true
    rules:
    - Every orderable menu item MUST be assigned to exactly one preparation station.
    - Order items MUST be routed according to the station assigned to their menu item.
    - A single order MAY create multiple station tickets.
    - A station ticket MUST contain only order items routed to the same station.
    - An order item MUST NOT be routed directly by free-text category name.
    - Station assignment MUST be configured by tenant owner, tenant admin, or tenant
      manager.
  status_model:
    order_item_status_flow:
    - submitted
    - routed_to_station
    - accepted
    - preparing
    - ready
    - served
    station_ticket_status_flow:
    - created
    - accepted
    - preparing
    - ready
    - closed
    status_meanings:
      submitted: The customer submitted the item as part of an order.
      routed_to_station: The system assigned the item to its target station.
      accepted: The station acknowledged the item as accepted work.
      preparing: The station is actively preparing the item.
      ready: The station marked the item as ready for service.
      served: Floor staff marked the item as delivered to the table.
      cancelled: The item was cancelled according to the cancellation policy.
      failed_to_route: The item could not be routed because station configuration
        is invalid or missing.
  station_workflow:
    accepted_required: true
    preparing_required: true
    ready_required: true
    served_required: true
    partial_station_ticket_acceptance_allowed: false
    floor_staff_bulk_mark_ready_items_served_allowed: true
    served_marking_actor: floor_staff
    rules:
    - Station operators MUST acknowledge accepted station work.
    - A station ticket MUST be accepted as a whole ticket.
    - Station tickets MUST NOT support partial acceptance per item.
    - Accepted and preparing are separate statuses.
    - Accepted means the station has seen and accepted the work.
    - Preparing means the station is actively working on the item.
    - Ready means the station has completed preparation.
    - Served MUST be marked by floor staff after the item is delivered to the table.
    - Floor staff MAY mark all ready items on a table as served in one bulk action.
    - Station operators MUST NOT mark an item as served.
  customer_visibility:
    customer_can_see_all_order_item_statuses: true
    customer_visible_status_labels:
      submitted: Order received
      routed_to_station: Sent to preparation
      accepted: Accepted
      preparing: Preparing
      ready: Ready
      served: Served
      cancelled: Cancelled
    rules:
    - Customers MAY see all customer-safe order item statuses.
    - Customer-facing labels MUST NOT expose internal implementation terms.
    - Ready status MAY be shown to customers, but the UI MUST communicate that service
      is still pending when served is not reached.
  staff_visibility:
    staff_visible_statuses:
    - submitted
    - routed_to_station
    - accepted
    - preparing
    - ready
    - served
    - cancelled
    - failed_to_route
    rules:
    - Station staff MUST see station tickets assigned to their station.
    - Floor staff MUST see item readiness and served state.
    - Tenant manager and tenant admin MAY see all station and order item statuses.
  cancellation_policy:
    staff_can_cancel_until_order_item_status: accepted
    after_accepted_staff_cancel_allowed: false
    privileged_cancellation_after_acceptance:
      allowed: true
      allowed_roles:
      - tenant_manager
      - tenant_admin
      requires_reason: true
      affects_bill_total: true
      audit_required: true
    rules:
    - Staff MAY cancel an order item before the item reaches accepted status.
    - Staff MUST NOT cancel an order item after accepted status.
    - Tenant manager or tenant admin MAY cancel an accepted, preparing, ready, or
      served order item only with a required reason.
    - Privileged cancellation after acceptance MUST be audited.
    - Privileged cancellation after acceptance MUST update the table bill total.
    - Cancellation does not trigger refund behavior because IoTable does not collect
      payments.
  configuration_requirements:
    menu_item_station_assignment_required: true
    station_code_required: true
    station_active_state_required: true
    invalid_station_assignment_blocks_order_submission: true
    rules:
    - A tenant MUST define preparation stations before orderable menu items can be
      fully configured.
    - A menu item without a valid active station assignment MUST NOT be orderable.
    - Station code MUST be stable within the tenant.
    - Station display name MAY change without changing station code.
  failure_handling:
    failed_to_route_behavior: block_order_submission
    missing_station_assignment_behavior: menu_item_not_orderable
    inactive_station_behavior: menu_item_not_orderable
    rules:
    - The system MUST prevent customer submission of an item that cannot be routed.
    - The system MUST expose configuration errors to tenant manager or tenant admin.
    - The system MUST NOT silently route an item to a fallback station.
  forbidden_behavior:
  - station_ticket_partial_acceptance_per_item
  - station_operator_marking_item_served
  - fallback_station_silent_routing
  owner_decisions:
  - id: SOR-OD-001
    topic: Station tickets are accepted as whole tickets and partial per-item station
      ticket acceptance is not supported.
    decision: accepted
  - id: SOR-OD-002
    topic: Floor staff can mark all ready items on a table as served in one bulk action.
    decision: accepted
  open_decisions: []
  done_when:
  - routing_unit_is_defined
  - menu_item_station_assignment_rule_is_defined
  - order_split_strategy_is_defined
  - order_item_status_flow_is_defined
  - station_ticket_status_flow_is_defined
  - customer_visibility_is_defined
  - staff_visibility_is_defined
  - cancellation_policy_is_defined
  - configuration_requirements_are_defined
  - failure_handling_is_defined
  - station_partial_acceptance_policy_is_defined
  - bulk_served_marking_policy_is_defined
  - forbidden_station_routing_behavior_is_defined
tenant_setup_workflows:
  purpose:
    rule: Define tenant setup domain workflows, commands, validations, domain events,
      automatic setup progress, activation gates, and operation activation behavior.
    applies_to:
    - tenant_setup_console
    - tenant_setup_api
    - tenant_setup_data
    - tenant_operations_activation
    - table_specific_firmware_generation
  workflow_policy:
    setup_step_completion_mode: automatic_from_data_state
    manual_step_completion_allowed: false
    domain_event_delivery: in_process
    distributed_message_bus_required: false
    rules:
    - Setup step completion MUST be derived from tenant database state.
    - Users MUST NOT manually mark setup steps as completed.
    - Activation gates MUST be evaluated from current tenant database state.
    - Tenant operations activation MUST be an explicit command.
    - Domain events MAY be handled in-process in the initial product version.
    - Distributed event bus MUST NOT be required for tenant setup workflows in the
      initial product version.
  commands:
  - id: UpdateTenantBusinessProfile
    actor_permissions:
    - tenant.profile.update
    emits:
    - TenantBusinessProfileUpdated
    - TenantSetupProgressEvaluated
  - id: UpdateTenantOperatingDefaults
    actor_permissions:
    - tenant.profile.update
    emits:
    - TenantOperatingDefaultsUpdated
    - TenantSetupProgressEvaluated
  - id: CreateOperationalTable
    actor_permissions:
    - tenant.tables.create
    emits:
    - TenantOperationalTableCreated
    - TenantSetupProgressEvaluated
    conditional_emits:
      has_qr_device_true:
      - TableQrDeviceConfigured
      - TableQrDeviceFirmwareGenerated
  - id: UpdateTable
    actor_permissions:
    - tenant.tables.update
    emits:
    - TenantTableUpdated
    - TenantSetupProgressEvaluated
  - id: GenerateTableDeviceFirmware
    actor_permissions:
    - tenant.tables.update
    emits:
    - TableQrDeviceFirmwareGenerated
    - TenantSetupProgressEvaluated
  - id: CreateStation
    actor_permissions:
    - tenant.stations.create
    emits:
    - StationCreated
    - TenantSetupProgressEvaluated
  - id: UpdateStation
    actor_permissions:
    - tenant.stations.update
    emits:
    - StationUpdated
    - TenantSetupProgressEvaluated
  - id: CreateMenuCategory
    actor_permissions:
    - tenant.menu.categories.manage
    emits:
    - MenuCategoryCreated
    - TenantSetupProgressEvaluated
  - id: UpdateMenuCategory
    actor_permissions:
    - tenant.menu.categories.manage
    emits:
    - MenuCategoryUpdated
    - TenantSetupProgressEvaluated
  - id: CreateMenuItem
    actor_permissions:
    - tenant.menu.items.create
    emits:
    - MenuItemCreated
    - MenuItemRoutingAssigned
    - TenantSetupProgressEvaluated
  - id: UpdateMenuItem
    actor_permissions:
    - tenant.menu.items.update
    emits:
    - MenuItemUpdated
    - MenuItemRoutingAssigned
    - TenantSetupProgressEvaluated
  - id: CreateStaffInvites
    actor_permissions:
    - tenant.staff.invite
    - tenant.staff.assign_roles
    emits:
    - StaffInvitesCreated
  - id: EvaluateTenantActivationGates
    actor_permissions:
    - tenant.setup.view
    emits:
    - TenantActivationGatesEvaluated
  - id: ActivateTenantOperations
    actor_permissions:
    - tenant.setup.complete
    emits:
    - TenantActivationGatesEvaluated
    - TenantOperationsActivated
    rules:
    - TenantOperationsActivated MUST be emitted only when all required gates pass.
  seed_workflows:
    tenant_provisioning_seed:
      runs_during: tenant_database_provisioning
      seed_entities:
      - system_test_table_000
      - system_test_table_999
      - default_station_MAIN
      - tenant_setup_state
      emits:
      - TenantSystemTestTableSeeded
      - TenantDefaultStationSeeded
      - TenantSetupStateInitialized
      rules:
      - Table 000 MUST be seeded as system_test.
      - Table 999 MUST be seeded as system_test.
      - MAIN station MUST be seeded as active.
      - Seeded system_test tables MUST NOT count as operational activation tables.
  setup_progress_evaluation:
    mode: automatic_from_data_state
    evaluated_after_commands:
    - UpdateTenantBusinessProfile
    - UpdateTenantOperatingDefaults
    - CreateOperationalTable
    - UpdateTable
    - GenerateTableDeviceFirmware
    - CreateStation
    - UpdateStation
    - CreateMenuCategory
    - UpdateMenuCategory
    - CreateMenuItem
    - UpdateMenuItem
    completed_step_rules:
      business_profile:
        completed_when:
        - business_display_name_present
        - business_type_valid
        - default_currency_present
        - default_timezone_present
      operating_defaults:
        completed_when:
        - default_service_mode_valid
        - default_tax_display_mode_valid
      tables:
        completed_when:
        - at_least_one_active_operational_table_exists
        exclusions:
        - system_test_tables_000_and_999
      stations:
        completed_when:
        - at_least_one_active_station_exists
      menu_categories:
        completed_when:
        - at_least_one_active_menu_category_exists
      menu_items:
        completed_when:
        - at_least_one_active_orderable_menu_item_exists
        - every_active_orderable_menu_item_has_price
        - every_active_orderable_menu_item_has_image
        - every_active_orderable_menu_item_has_active_station_routing
      staff_users:
        completed_when:
        - always_optional
      devices:
        completed_when:
        - always_optional
    rules:
    - Setup progress MUST be recalculated from tenant database state.
    - Setup progress MUST NOT trust client-provided completed step flags.
    - Optional setup steps MUST NOT block operations activation.
  activation_gate_evaluation:
    command: EvaluateTenantActivationGates
    source_of_truth: resolved_tenant_database
    required_gates:
    - id: business_profile_completed
      passes_when: business_profile step is completed
    - id: operating_defaults_completed
      passes_when: operating_defaults step is completed
    - id: at_least_one_active_operational_table_created
      passes_when: tables contains at least one active operational table
      excludes:
      - table_000
      - table_999
      - system_test_tables
    - id: at_least_one_active_station_created
      passes_when: stations contains at least one active station
    - id: at_least_one_active_menu_category_created
      passes_when: menu_categories contains at least one active category
    - id: at_least_one_active_orderable_menu_item_created
      passes_when: menu_items contains at least one active orderable item
    - id: every_orderable_menu_item_has_price
      passes_when: all active orderable menu items have price
    - id: every_orderable_menu_item_has_image
      passes_when: all active orderable menu items have image_asset_id
    - id: every_orderable_menu_item_has_station_routing
      passes_when: all active orderable menu items route to an active station
    - id: tenant_owner_activation_completed
      passes_when: tenant owner account is activated
    optional_gates:
    - staff_users_invited
    - esp32_table_firmware_downloaded
    - opening_hours_configured
    - service_charge_configured
    rules:
    - Activation gates MUST be evaluated inside the resolved tenant database context.
    - Activation gates MUST NOT query another tenant database.
    - Activation gates MUST NOT query platform database for tenant operational state.
    - System test tables MUST NOT satisfy the active operational table gate.
    - ESP32 firmware download MUST NOT be required for operations activation.
  workflows:
    business_profile_updated:
      command: UpdateTenantBusinessProfile
      validation:
      - business_display_name_required
      - business_type_allowed
      - default_currency_required
      - default_timezone_required
      state_changes:
      - tenant_profile_updated
      - setup_progress_recalculated
    operating_defaults_updated:
      command: UpdateTenantOperatingDefaults
      validation:
      - default_service_mode_must_be_dine_in_initial_version
      - default_tax_display_mode_allowed
      state_changes:
      - tenant_profile_operating_defaults_updated
      - setup_progress_recalculated
    operational_table_created:
      command: CreateOperationalTable
      validation:
      - table_number_unique
      - table_number_not_000_or_999_for_operational_table
      - has_qr_device_required
      state_changes:
      - table_created
      - staff_pda_ordering_enabled
      - setup_progress_recalculated
    table_qr_device_configured:
      trigger: CreateOperationalTable_has_qr_device_true_or_enable_qr_device
      validation:
      - one_active_qr_display_device_per_table
      - firmware_secret_context_available
      state_changes:
      - device_id_generated
      - device_secret_generated_and_hash_stored
      - ws_identity_generated
      - table_device_config_created
    table_specific_firmware_generated:
      command: GenerateTableDeviceFirmware
      validation:
      - table_has_qr_device_true
      - actor_authorized_for_firmware_generation
      - wifi_credentials_present_when_generation_requires_them
      state_changes:
      - firmware_artifact_generated
      - firmware_config_version_incremented
      - old_device_secret_revoked_when_regenerating
      audit_required: true
    menu_item_created:
      command: CreateMenuItem
      validation:
      - category_exists
      - image_asset_id_required
      - orderable_item_requires_price
      - orderable_item_requires_active_station
      - variants_not_supported
      - modifiers_not_supported
      state_changes:
      - menu_item_created
      - setup_progress_recalculated
    tenant_operations_activated:
      command: ActivateTenantOperations
      validation:
      - all_required_activation_gates_pass
      state_changes:
      - tenant_setup_state_activation_status_active
      - activated_at_utc_set
      - activated_by_user_id_set
      audit_required: true
  domain_events:
  - TenantBusinessProfileUpdated
  - TenantOperatingDefaultsUpdated
  - TenantOperationalTableCreated
  - TenantTableUpdated
  - TenantSystemTestTableSeeded
  - TenantDefaultStationSeeded
  - TenantSetupStateInitialized
  - TableQrDeviceConfigured
  - TableQrDeviceFirmwareGenerated
  - StationCreated
  - StationUpdated
  - MenuCategoryCreated
  - MenuCategoryUpdated
  - MenuItemCreated
  - MenuItemUpdated
  - MenuItemRoutingAssigned
  - StaffInvitesCreated
  - TenantSetupProgressEvaluated
  - TenantActivationGatesEvaluated
  - TenantOperationsActivated
  audit_required_for:
  - firmware_generation
  - firmware_download
  - tenant_operations_activation
  - staff_invites_created
  - device_secret_regeneration
  forbidden_behavior:
  - manual_setup_step_completion
  - client_provided_completed_steps_as_source_of_truth
  - activation_without_gate_evaluation
  - activation_with_failed_required_gate
  - activation_gate_counting_system_test_tables
  - activation_gate_requiring_staff_invites
  - activation_gate_requiring_esp32_firmware_download
  - distributed_message_bus_required_for_initial_setup
  - tenant_setup_workflow_cross_tenant_query
  - tenant_setup_workflow_using_platform_database_for_tenant_operational_state
  - firmware_regeneration_without_old_secret_revocation
  open_decisions: []
  done_when:
  - workflow_policy_is_defined
  - commands_are_defined
  - seed_workflows_are_defined
  - setup_progress_evaluation_is_defined
  - activation_gate_evaluation_is_defined
  - tenant_setup_workflows_are_defined
  - domain_events_are_defined
  - audit_required_workflows_are_defined
  - forbidden_workflow_behavior_is_defined
tenant_lifecycle_provisioning:
  purpose:
    rule: Define tenant lifecycle statuses, platform tenant creation, tenant database
      provisioning, schema application, seed data insertion, owner activation invite
      creation, failure handling, retry behavior, and lifecycle versus operations
      activation boundaries.
    applies_to:
    - platform_control_plane
    - tenant_lifecycle
    - tenant_provisioning
    - tenant_database_registry
    - tenant_domain_registry
    - tenant_schema_jobs
    - tenant_owner_activation
  lifecycle_statuses:
    allowed_statuses:
    - draft
    - provisioning
    - active
    - suspended
    - deactivated
    - provisioning_failed
    initial_status: draft
    terminal_statuses:
    - deactivated
    recoverable_statuses:
    - provisioning_failed
    - suspended
    rules:
    - Tenant lifecycle status is platform-owned metadata.
    - Tenant lifecycle active MUST mean the tenant technical environment is provisioned
      and available.
    - Tenant lifecycle active MUST NOT mean tenant operations are customer-ordering
      active.
    - Tenant operations activation MUST be stored and evaluated inside the tenant
      database.
    - Suspended tenants MAY be reactivated.
    - Deactivated tenants MUST NOT be reactivated in the initial product version.
  lifecycle_vs_operations_activation:
    tenant_lifecycle_active:
      owner: platform_database
      meaning: Tenant technical environment exists, tenant domain resolves, tenant
        database is provisioned, schema is applied, seed data exists, and owner activation
        invite has been created.
    tenant_operations_active:
      owner: tenant_database
      meaning: Tenant setup activation gates passed and tenant operations were explicitly
        activated from tenant setup console.
    rules:
    - Tenant lifecycle active and tenant operations active MUST be separate states.
    - A tenant MAY be lifecycle active while tenant operations are not_active.
    - Customer ordering MUST depend on tenant operations active, not only tenant lifecycle
      active.
    - Platform control plane MUST NOT directly activate tenant operations.
  provisioning_flow:
    initiated_by: platform_owner
    command: CreateTenant
    steps:
    - step: 1
      id: create_tenant_registry_row
      owner: platform_database
      result: tenant_registry row created with status draft or provisioning
    - step: 2
      id: reserve_tenant_slug
      owner: platform_database
      result: tenant slug reserved and marked immutable
    - step: 3
      id: register_tenant_domain
      owner: platform_database
      result: '{tenant}.iotables.net domain mapping inserted into tenant_domain_registry'
    - step: 4
      id: create_tenant_database_registry_row
      owner: platform_database
      result: tenant database name and connection reference registered
    - step: 5
      id: create_tenant_database
      owner: database_server
      result: dedicated tenant PostgreSQL database created
    - step: 6
      id: apply_tenant_schema
      owner: tenant_database
      result: current tenant schema applied
    - step: 7
      id: insert_tenant_seed_data
      owner: tenant_database
      result: tenant setup seed data inserted
    - step: 8
      id: create_tenant_owner_activation_invite
      owner: tenant_database
      result: tenant owner activation invite created
    - step: 9
      id: mark_tenant_lifecycle_active
      owner: platform_database
      result: tenant lifecycle status set to active
    rules:
    - Provisioning MUST be tracked as a platform job.
    - Provisioning steps MUST be idempotent where possible.
    - Provisioning MUST create exactly one dedicated tenant database.
    - Provisioning MUST insert required tenant seed data before lifecycle active.
    - Provisioning MUST create tenant owner activation invite before lifecycle active.
  slug_policy:
    source: platform_owner_input
    immutable_after_creation: true
    used_for:
    - tenant_domain
    - tenant_database_name_generation
    - tenant_registry_lookup
    - audit_correlation
    rules:
    - Tenant slug MUST be unique.
    - Tenant slug MUST be immutable after tenant creation.
    - Tenant slug MUST be safe for subdomain use.
    - Tenant slug changes MUST NOT be supported in the initial product version.
  database_naming_policy:
    pattern: iotable_tenant_{tenantSlug}
    generated_by_system: true
    connection_resolution: platform_tenant_database_registry
    rules:
    - Tenant database name MUST be generated by the system.
    - Tenant database name MUST follow the configured database naming pattern.
    - Connection strings or connection secrets MUST NOT be built from client input.
    - Tenant database connection MUST be resolved from trusted platform registry and
      server secret configuration.
  seed_data:
    inserted_during: tenant_database_provisioning
    required_seed_entities:
      tenant_setup_state:
        status: not_started
        activation_status: not_active
      system_test_tables:
      - table_number: '000'
        table_type: system_test
        has_qr_device: false
        customer_self_ordering_enabled: false
        staff_pda_ordering_enabled: true
      - table_number: '999'
        table_type: system_test
        has_qr_device: false
        customer_self_ordering_enabled: false
        staff_pda_ordering_enabled: true
      default_station:
        station_code: MAIN
        display_name: Main Station
        station_type: kitchen
        status: active
    rules:
    - Tenant setup state MUST be seeded during provisioning.
    - System test tables 000 and 999 MUST be seeded during provisioning.
    - MAIN station MUST be seeded during provisioning.
    - Seed data MUST be idempotent.
    - Seed data MUST be inserted only into the tenant database.
  owner_activation_invite:
    created_during_provisioning: true
    ttl_hours: 168
    storage_scope: tenant_database
    token_storage: hash_only
    role_assigned: tenant_owner
    rules:
    - Tenant owner activation invite MUST be created during provisioning.
    - Tenant owner activation invite MUST expire after 168 hours.
    - Tenant owner activation token MUST be stored hash-only.
    - Tenant owner activation invite MUST assign tenant_owner role.
    - Tenant owner activation invite MUST NOT be created by tenant staff invite API.
    - Tenant owner activation invite creation MUST be audited.
  provisioning_success_criteria:
    lifecycle_active_when:
    - tenant_registry_row_created
    - tenant_domain_registered
    - tenant_database_registry_row_created
    - tenant_database_created
    - tenant_schema_applied
    - tenant_seed_data_inserted
    - tenant_owner_activation_invite_created
    rules:
    - Tenant MUST NOT be marked lifecycle active before all success criteria pass.
    - Tenant lifecycle active MUST NOT bypass tenant setup activation gates.
  provisioning_failure:
    failure_status: provisioning_failed
    retry_allowed: true
    partial_resources_tracked: true
    automatic_destructive_cleanup: false
    failure_metadata_required:
    - failed_step
    - error_code
    - error_message_safe
    - occurred_at_utc
    rules:
    - Provisioning failure MUST set tenant lifecycle status to provisioning_failed.
    - Provisioning failure MUST record failed step and safe error details.
    - Provisioning failure MUST track partial resources that were created.
    - Provisioning failure MUST NOT automatically delete partial resources.
    - Provisioning retry MUST be initiated from platform control plane by authorized
      platform_owner.
    - Provisioning retry MUST be idempotent and safe against already-created resources.
  retry_policy:
    allowed_for_statuses:
    - provisioning_failed
    actor_permission: platform.provisioning.retry
    retry_strategy: resume_from_failed_or_idempotently_recheck_prior_steps
    destructive_retry_allowed: false
    rules:
    - Retry MUST NOT blindly recreate resources without checking existing state.
    - Retry MUST NOT drop an existing tenant database automatically.
    - Retry MUST revalidate tenant slug, domain registry, database registry, schema
      state, seed state, and owner invite state.
    - Retry result MUST be audited in platform audit.
  schema_versioning:
    tenant_schema_version_tracked: true
    owner: platform_schema_jobs_and_tenant_database_metadata
    rules:
    - Tenant schema version MUST be tracked per tenant database.
    - Schema application during provisioning MUST record applied schema version.
    - Schema jobs after provisioning MUST target tenant databases explicitly through
      platform schema job orchestration.
    - Schema jobs MUST NOT run cross-tenant operations from tenant runtime requests.
  forbidden_behavior:
  - tenant_lifecycle_active_implying_operations_active
  - platform_control_plane_directly_activating_tenant_operations
  - tenant_slug_edit_after_creation_initial_version
  - tenant_database_name_from_client_input
  - tenant_provisioning_without_dedicated_database
  - tenant_lifecycle_active_before_seed_data
  - tenant_lifecycle_active_before_owner_activation_invite
  - automatic_destructive_cleanup_after_provisioning_failure
  - blind_retry_recreating_existing_resources
  - retry_dropping_existing_tenant_database
  - owner_activation_invite_token_plaintext_storage
  - tenant_seed_data_inserted_into_platform_database
  open_decisions: []
  done_when:
  - lifecycle_statuses_are_defined
  - lifecycle_vs_operations_activation_boundary_is_defined
  - provisioning_flow_is_defined
  - slug_policy_is_defined
  - database_naming_policy_is_defined
  - seed_data_is_defined
  - owner_activation_invite_is_defined
  - provisioning_success_criteria_are_defined
  - provisioning_failure_policy_is_defined
  - retry_policy_is_defined
  - schema_versioning_policy_is_defined
  - forbidden_lifecycle_behavior_is_defined
tenant_resolution:
  purpose:
    rule: Define tenant domain policy, tenant resolution from host subdomain, tenant
      database context creation, tenant status handling, connection resolution, and
      forbidden cross-tenant behavior.
    applies_to:
    - tenant_internal_api
    - tenant_staff_identity_api
    - tenant_setup_console
    - tenant_web
    - customer_menu_routes
    - device_websocket_routes
    - tenant_database_isolation
  canonical_domains:
    platform_domain: platform.iotables.net
    tenant_domain_pattern: '{tenant}.iotables.net'
    custom_tenant_domains_supported: false
    custom_tenant_domains_future_scope: false
    examples:
      platform: platform.iotables.net
      tenant: demo.iotables.net
    rules:
    - Platform control plane MUST use platform.iotables.net.
    - Tenant applications MUST use {tenant}.iotables.net.
    - Tenant resolution MUST be based on the request host.
    - Restaurant-owned custom domains such as restoranadi.com MUST NOT be supported.
    - Customer-facing routes, tenant staff routes, tenant APIs, and device WebSocket
      routes MUST use the canonical tenant domain.
    - Custom domain DNS ownership verification, custom TLS certificate lifecycle,
      and tenant-owned host routing are out of scope.
  tenant_resolution_model:
    source: host_subdomain
    platform_lookup_database: iotable_platform
    platform_lookup_table: tenant_domain_registry
    resolved_context_fields:
    - tenant_id
    - tenant_slug
    - tenant_domain
    - tenant_status
    - tenant_database_name
    - tenant_database_connection_ref
    - tenant_schema_version
    rules:
    - TenantContext MUST be resolved before executing any tenant route.
    - TenantContext MUST be resolved from the trusted request host only.
    - Client-provided tenant_id MUST NOT be used for tenant resolution.
    - Client-provided tenant slug in request body, query string, or headers MUST NOT
      override host-based tenant resolution.
    - Tenant database connection MUST be selected from the resolved TenantContext.
    - Tenant routes MUST NOT execute without a resolved TenantContext.
  resolution_flow:
    steps:
    - step: 1
      id: read_request_host
      rule: Read normalized request host from trusted server request metadata.
    - step: 2
      id: classify_host
      rule: Classify host as platform domain, tenant domain, or invalid domain.
    - step: 3
      id: extract_tenant_slug
      rule: For tenant domains, extract the tenant slug from {tenant}.iotables.net.
    - step: 4
      id: lookup_tenant_domain_registry
      rule: Lookup tenant slug/domain in platform tenant_domain_registry.
    - step: 5
      id: validate_tenant_status
      rule: Validate tenant lifecycle status before allowing tenant route execution.
    - step: 6
      id: resolve_tenant_database
      rule: Resolve tenant database name and connection reference from platform tenant
        database registry.
    - step: 7
      id: create_tenant_context
      rule: Create immutable TenantContext for the request.
    - step: 8
      id: execute_tenant_pipeline
      rule: Execute tenant API/auth/websocket pipeline only with the resolved TenantContext.
    failure_results:
      invalid_domain: 404 invalid_domain
      tenant_not_found: 404 tenant_not_found
      tenant_not_active: tenant_status_specific_response
      tenant_database_unavailable: 503 tenant_database_unavailable
  status_behavior:
    statuses:
      provisioning:
        tenant_api: unavailable
        customer_routes: blocked
        staff_login: blocked
        device_websocket: blocked
        response: 503 tenant_provisioning
      active:
        tenant_api: available
        customer_routes: available
        staff_login: available
        device_websocket: available
        response: normal
      suspended:
        tenant_api: blocked
        customer_routes: blocked
        staff_login: blocked
        device_websocket: blocked
        tenant_owner_message_page: true
        platform_support_access: platform_only
        response: 423 tenant_suspended
      deactivated:
        tenant_api: blocked
        customer_routes: blocked
        staff_login: blocked
        device_websocket: blocked
        response: 410 tenant_deactivated
      provisioning_failed:
        tenant_api: unavailable
        customer_routes: blocked
        staff_login: blocked
        device_websocket: blocked
        response: 503 tenant_provisioning_failed
    rules:
    - Only active tenants MAY serve customer routes, staff login, tenant APIs, and
      device WebSocket connections.
    - Suspended tenants MUST block customer routes, staff login, tenant APIs, and
      device WebSocket connections.
    - Suspended tenants MAY show a tenant owner message page.
    - Platform support access to tenant lifecycle metadata MUST occur from platform
      control plane only.
  connection_resolution:
    model: database_per_tenant
    connection_source: platform_tenant_database_registry
    connection_secret_storage: server_secret_store_or_encrypted_config
    connection_cache_allowed: true
    cache_key: tenant_id
    cache_must_respect_tenant_status: true
    cache_invalidation_required_for:
    - tenant_suspended
    - tenant_reactivated
    - tenant_deactivated
    - tenant_database_connection_rotated
    - tenant_schema_job_completed
    rules:
    - Tenant database connection MUST come from platform tenant database registry
      or trusted server secret configuration.
    - Tenant database connection MUST NOT be accepted from client input.
    - Connection cache MAY be used for performance.
    - Connection cache MUST be invalidated when tenant status or database connection
      reference changes.
    - Resolved tenant database connection MUST target exactly one tenant database.
  security_rules:
    tenant_context_required_for_tenant_routes: true
    tenant_db_resolved_per_request: true
    client_supplied_tenant_id_allowed: false
    tenant_id_header_trusted: false
    cross_tenant_context_switch_allowed: false
    custom_domain_routing_allowed: false
    rules:
    - TenantContext MUST be immutable after creation for a request.
    - Tenant routes MUST NOT switch TenantContext during request execution.
    - Tenant routes MUST NOT query multiple tenant databases in one request.
    - Tenant staff authentication MUST use the resolved tenant database.
    - Customer session and QR flows MUST use the resolved tenant database.
    - ESP32 device WebSocket authentication MUST use the resolved tenant database.
  platform_vs_tenant_route_boundary:
    platform_routes:
      domain: platform.iotables.net
      may_access:
      - platform_database
      - tenant_lifecycle_metadata
      - tenant_database_registry
      - provisioning_jobs
      - schema_jobs
      must_not_access_for_runtime_operations:
      - tenant_menu_items
      - tenant_orders
      - tenant_customer_sessions
      - tenant_table_devices
    tenant_routes:
      domain_pattern: '{tenant}.iotables.net'
      may_access:
      - resolved_tenant_database
      may_lookup_before_context_creation:
      - tenant_domain_registry
      - tenant_database_registry
      must_not_access:
      - another_tenant_database
      - platform_control_plane_mutation
    rules:
    - Platform routes and tenant routes MUST remain domain-separated.
    - Tenant routes MAY use platform registry only to resolve TenantContext.
    - Tenant routes MUST NOT mutate platform lifecycle records.
  forbidden_behavior:
  - custom_tenant_domain_support
  - tenant_owned_domain_routing
  - restaurant_domain_routing
  - tenant_resolution_from_request_body
  - tenant_resolution_from_untrusted_header
  - tenant_resolution_from_client_supplied_tenant_id
  - tenant_route_without_tenant_context
  - tenant_context_switch_during_request
  - tenant_route_querying_multiple_tenant_databases
  - tenant_api_served_from_platform_domain
  - platform_control_plane_served_from_tenant_domain
  - suspended_tenant_customer_route_available
  - suspended_tenant_staff_login_available
  - suspended_tenant_device_websocket_available
  open_decisions: []
  done_when:
  - canonical_domains_are_defined
  - tenant_resolution_model_is_defined
  - resolution_flow_is_defined
  - tenant_status_behavior_is_defined
  - connection_resolution_is_defined
  - security_rules_are_defined
  - platform_vs_tenant_route_boundary_is_defined
  - forbidden_tenant_resolution_behavior_is_defined
permission_catalog:
  purpose:
    rule: Define platform and tenant roles, permission namespaces, fixed role-permission
      bundles, and backend enforcement rules.
    applies_to:
    - platform_control_plane
    - tenant_setup_console
    - floor_cash_workspace
    - station_board
    - tenant_internal_api
    - platform_api
  permission_model:
    model: fixed_roles_with_fixed_permission_bundles
    custom_roles: false
    custom_permission_editing: false
    ui_assignment_unit: role
    backend_enforcement_unit: permission
    rules:
    - Users MUST be assigned roles for operational manageability.
    - Backend authorization MUST enforce permissions, not role names.
    - Role permission bundles MUST be fixed in the current product version.
    - Tenant users MUST NOT create custom roles.
    - Tenant users MUST NOT edit permission bundles.
    - Platform permissions and tenant permissions MUST use separate namespaces.
    - Customer diner access MUST NOT use staff roles or tenant staff permissions.
  role_catalog:
    platform_roles:
    - id: platform_owner
      scope: platform
      purpose: Manage tenant lifecycle, provisioning, platform jobs, platform audit,
        platform settings, and system health.
      permission_bundle: all_platform_permissions
    tenant_roles:
    - id: tenant_owner
      scope: tenant
      purpose: Own and administer the tenant with full tenant authority.
      permission_bundle: all_tenant_permissions
    - id: tenant_admin
      scope: tenant
      purpose: Administer tenant configuration and operations except ownership and
        danger-zone actions.
    - id: tenant_manager
      scope: tenant
      purpose: Manage day-to-day tenant configuration and operations.
    - id: cashier
      scope: tenant
      purpose: Manage table bills, manual payment recording, and bill closure.
    - id: floor_staff
      scope: tenant
      purpose: Track table and order service flow and mark served items.
    - id: station_operator
      scope: tenant
      purpose: Operate assigned station work regardless of station type.
    - id: accountant
      scope: tenant
      purpose: Read adisyon and manual payment records without operational mutation
        authority.
  permission_namespaces:
    platform: platform.*
    tenant: tenant.*
    rules:
    - platform.* permissions MUST NOT be granted to tenant roles.
    - tenant.* permissions MUST NOT grant platform control-plane authority.
  platform_permissions:
    tenant_lifecycle:
    - platform.tenants.view
    - platform.tenants.create
    - platform.tenants.suspend
    - platform.tenants.reactivate
    - platform.tenants.deactivate
    provisioning:
    - platform.provisioning.view
    - platform.provisioning.retry
    schema_jobs:
    - platform.schema_jobs.view
    - platform.schema_jobs.create
    jobs:
    - platform.jobs.view
    audit:
    - platform.audit.view
    settings:
    - platform.settings.view
    - platform.settings.update
    health:
    - platform.health.view
  tenant_permissions:
    tenant_profile:
    - tenant.profile.view
    - tenant.profile.update
    setup:
    - tenant.setup.view
    - tenant.setup.complete
    staff:
    - tenant.staff.view
    - tenant.staff.invite
    - tenant.staff.update
    - tenant.staff.disable
    - tenant.staff.assign_roles
    tables:
    - tenant.tables.view
    - tenant.tables.create
    - tenant.tables.update
    - tenant.tables.disable
    firmware:
    - tenant.firmware.generate
    - tenant.firmware.download
    stations:
    - tenant.stations.view
    - tenant.stations.create
    - tenant.stations.update
    - tenant.stations.disable
    menu:
    - tenant.menu.view
    - tenant.menu.categories.manage
    - tenant.menu.items.create
    - tenant.menu.items.update
    - tenant.menu.items.disable
    - tenant.menu.prices.update
    - tenant.menu.item_station_routing.update
    service_floor:
    - tenant.floor.view
    - tenant.tables.state.view
    - tenant.orders.view
    - tenant.orders.cancel_before_station_acceptance
    - tenant.orders.items.mark_served
    stations_workflow:
    - tenant.station_tickets.view_assigned
    - tenant.station_tickets.accept
    - tenant.station_tickets.mark_preparing
    - tenant.station_tickets.mark_ready
    bills:
    - tenant.bills.view
    - tenant.bills.close
    - tenant.bills.reopen
    manual_payments:
    - tenant.manual_payments.view
    - tenant.manual_payments.record
    tenant_audit:
    - tenant.audit.view
    settings:
    - tenant.settings.view
    - tenant.settings.update
  role_permission_matrix:
    platform_owner:
      includes: all_platform_permissions
    tenant_owner:
      includes: all_tenant_permissions
    tenant_admin:
      include_groups:
      - tenant_profile
      - setup
      - staff
      - tables
      - firmware
      - stations
      - menu
      - service_floor
      - stations_workflow
      - bills
      - manual_payments
      - tenant_audit
      - settings
      exclusions:
      - tenant.danger_zone.*
      - tenant.ownership.transfer
    tenant_manager:
      permissions:
      - tenant.profile.view
      - tenant.setup.view
      - tenant.tables.view
      - tenant.tables.create
      - tenant.tables.update
      - tenant.stations.view
      - tenant.stations.create
      - tenant.stations.update
      - tenant.menu.view
      - tenant.menu.categories.manage
      - tenant.menu.items.create
      - tenant.menu.items.update
      - tenant.menu.items.disable
      - tenant.menu.prices.update
      - tenant.menu.item_station_routing.update
      - tenant.floor.view
      - tenant.tables.state.view
      - tenant.orders.view
      - tenant.orders.cancel_before_station_acceptance
      - tenant.orders.items.mark_served
      - tenant.station_tickets.view_assigned
      - tenant.station_tickets.accept
      - tenant.station_tickets.mark_preparing
      - tenant.station_tickets.mark_ready
      - tenant.bills.view
      - tenant.manual_payments.view
      - tenant.settings.view
    cashier:
      permissions:
      - tenant.floor.view
      - tenant.tables.state.view
      - tenant.orders.view
      - tenant.orders.cancel_before_station_acceptance
      - tenant.bills.view
      - tenant.bills.close
      - tenant.manual_payments.view
      - tenant.manual_payments.record
    floor_staff:
      permissions:
      - tenant.floor.view
      - tenant.tables.state.view
      - tenant.orders.view
      - tenant.orders.cancel_before_station_acceptance
      - tenant.orders.items.mark_served
      - tenant.bills.view
    station_operator:
      permissions:
      - tenant.station_tickets.view_assigned
      - tenant.station_tickets.accept
      - tenant.station_tickets.mark_preparing
      - tenant.station_tickets.mark_ready
    accountant:
      read_only: true
      permissions:
      - tenant.bills.view
      - tenant.manual_payments.view
      - tenant.audit.view
  operation_authorization_rules:
  - id: PAR-001
    operation: platform tenant lifecycle actions
    required_permissions:
    - platform.tenants.suspend
    - platform.tenants.reactivate
    - platform.tenants.deactivate
    rules:
    - Only platform_owner MAY perform platform tenant lifecycle actions.
  - id: PAR-002
    operation: tenant staff role assignment
    required_permission: tenant.staff.assign_roles
    rules:
    - Tenant role assignment MUST remain tenant-scoped.
    - Tenant role assignment MUST NOT grant platform permissions.
  - id: PAR-003
    operation: order cancellation before station acceptance
    required_permission: tenant.orders.cancel_before_station_acceptance
    rules:
    - Staff MAY cancel an order only before station acceptance.
    - After station acceptance, cancellation requires an authorized tenant manager
      or tenant admin flow.
  - id: PAR-004
    operation: station workflow mutation
    required_permissions:
    - tenant.station_tickets.accept
    - tenant.station_tickets.mark_preparing
    - tenant.station_tickets.mark_ready
    rules:
    - Station workflow mutations MUST be limited to assigned station context when
      station assignment exists.
  - id: PAR-005
    operation: bill closure
    required_permission: tenant.bills.close
    rules:
    - Bill closure MUST be performed by authorized cashier, tenant_manager, tenant_admin,
      or tenant_owner.
    - Bill closure records external manual payment status only.
  - id: PAR-006
    operation: accountant access
    required_permissions:
    - tenant.bills.view
    - tenant.manual_payments.view
    rules:
    - Accountant access MUST be read-only.
    - Accountant access MUST NOT mutate bills, payments, menu, stations, tables, staff,
      or orders.
  - id: PAR-007
    operation: table-specific firmware generation and download
    required_permissions:
    - tenant.firmware.generate
    - tenant.firmware.download
    rules:
    - Firmware generation and download MUST be limited to tenant_owner and tenant_admin
      in the initial product version.
    - tenant_manager MUST NOT receive firmware generation or firmware download permissions
      in the initial product version.
    - Firmware artifacts MUST be treated as tenant secrets.
  forbidden_behavior:
  - custom_roles_in_first_version
  - tenant_editable_permission_bundles
  - backend_authorization_by_role_name_only
  - tenant_role_granting_platform_permission
  - platform_role_used_as_tenant_staff_role
  - customer_diner_using_staff_permission_model
  - kitchen_operator_role
  - accountant_mutation_permissions
  - firmware_permission_granted_to_tenant_manager_initial_version
  open_decisions: []
  done_when:
  - permission_model_is_defined
  - platform_role_catalog_is_defined
  - tenant_role_catalog_is_defined
  - permission_namespaces_are_defined
  - platform_permissions_are_defined
  - tenant_permissions_are_defined
  - firmware_permissions_are_defined
  - role_permission_matrix_is_defined
  - operation_authorization_rules_are_defined
  - forbidden_permission_behavior_is_defined
tenant_staff_identity:
  purpose:
    rule: Define tenant staff identity scope, invite-based activation, password policy,
      two-factor policy, email uniqueness, staff disable behavior, and forbidden identity
      behavior.
    applies_to:
    - tenant_setup_console
    - tenant_staff_invites
    - tenant_staff_login
    - tenant_identity_activation
    - tenant_internal_api
  identity_scope:
    storage_scope: tenant_database
    isolation_model: database_per_tenant
    same_email_across_tenants_allowed: true
    duplicate_active_email_within_tenant_allowed: false
    rules:
    - Tenant staff identity MUST be tenant-scoped.
    - Tenant staff identity records MUST be stored in the resolved tenant database.
    - The same email MAY exist in different tenant databases.
    - The same tenant database MUST NOT have more than one active user with the same
      normalized email.
    - Tenant staff identity MUST NOT be stored in the platform database.
    - Platform users and tenant staff users MUST remain separate identity scopes.
  activation_model:
    model: invite_based_activation
    invite_required_for_staff: true
    tenant_owner_creation_source: platform_tenant_owner_activation
    staff_invite_can_assign_tenant_owner: false
    invite_token_storage: hash_only
    invite_token_ttl_hours: 72
    activation_steps:
    - step: 1
      id: authorized_user_creates_staff_invite
      actor: tenant_owner_or_authorized_staff_admin
      result: A tenant staff invite is created with fixed tenant role assignment.
    - step: 2
      id: system_sends_invite_email
      actor: system
      result: An invite link containing a one-time token is sent to the invitee email.
    - step: 3
      id: invitee_opens_invite_link
      actor: invitee
      result: The invite token is validated against hash, status, tenant database,
        and TTL.
    - step: 4
      id: invitee_sets_password
      actor: invitee
      result: The invitee sets an initial password matching tenant password policy.
    - step: 5
      id: tenant_staff_user_activated
      actor: system
      result: A tenant staff user becomes active with the role assigned by the invite.
    - step: 6
      id: invite_marked_accepted
      actor: system
      result: The invite is marked accepted and cannot be reused.
    rules:
    - Tenant staff activation MUST require a valid invite token.
    - Invite tokens MUST be stored as hashes only.
    - Invite tokens MUST be one-time-use.
    - Invite tokens MUST expire after 72 hours.
    - Expired invites MUST NOT activate users.
    - Accepted invites MUST NOT be reusable.
    - Tenant owner role MUST NOT be assignable through staff invite flow.
  staff_invite_policy:
    allowed_invitable_roles:
    - tenant_admin
    - tenant_manager
    - cashier
    - floor_staff
    - station_operator
    - accountant
    forbidden_invitable_roles:
    - tenant_owner
    - platform_owner
    required_permissions:
      create_invite:
      - tenant.staff.invite
      - tenant.staff.assign_roles
    resend_allowed: true
    revoke_allowed: true
    rules:
    - Only fixed tenant roles listed in allowed_invitable_roles MAY be assigned by
      staff invite.
    - Platform roles MUST NOT be assignable by tenant staff invite flow.
    - Tenant owner MUST NOT be assignable by tenant staff invite flow.
    - Invite resend MUST issue a new token and invalidate the previous pending token.
    - Invite revocation MUST prevent future activation from that invite token.
  password_policy:
    min_length: 10
    require_uppercase: true
    require_lowercase: true
    require_digit: true
    require_symbol: false
    password_hashing: bcrypt_or_argon2
    rules:
    - Tenant staff password MUST be set by the invited user during activation.
    - Tenant staff password MUST NOT be sent by email.
    - Password hashes MUST be stored using bcrypt or argon2 secure hashing.
    - Password policy MUST require at least 10 characters.
    - Password policy MUST require uppercase, lowercase, and digit.
    - Password policy MUST NOT require symbol in the initial product version.
  two_factor_policy:
    supported: true
    required_initial_version: false
    allowed_methods:
    - authenticator_app
    - email_code
    rules:
    - Two-factor authentication MAY be supported in the initial product version.
    - Two-factor authentication MUST NOT be required for all tenant staff in the initial
      product version.
    - Two-factor authentication MAY be enabled per user when supported.
  login_policy:
    tenant_domain_required: true
    platform_domain_login_for_tenant_staff: false
    disabled_user_login_allowed: false
    rules:
    - Tenant staff MUST login through the tenant domain.
    - Tenant staff MUST NOT login through platform.iotables.net.
    - Tenant staff login MUST resolve the tenant database from the subdomain.
    - Disabled staff users MUST NOT be allowed to login.
    - Tenant staff authentication MUST produce tenant-scoped claims and permissions.
  staff_lifecycle:
    hard_delete_allowed: false
    disable_allowed: true
    disabled_user_login_allowed: false
    historical_records_preserved: true
    role_change_allowed: true
    rules:
    - Tenant staff users MUST NOT be hard-deleted in the initial product version.
    - Tenant staff users MAY be disabled by authorized users.
    - Disabled users MUST retain historical audit and operational references.
    - Role changes MUST be audited.
    - Disabling a user MUST be audited.
  data_contract_expectations:
    tenant_user_tables_required:
    - tenant_users
    - tenant_user_roles
    - staff_invites
    staff_invites_existing_contract: specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
    rules:
    - tenant_users and tenant_user_roles MUST be stored in the tenant database.
    - staff_invites MUST store invite_token_hash only.
    - tenant_users MUST enforce unique active normalized email within the same tenant
      database.
  security_and_audit:
    audit_required_for:
    - staff_invite_created
    - staff_invite_resent
    - staff_invite_revoked
    - staff_user_activated
    - staff_user_disabled
    - staff_role_changed
    - failed_invite_activation_due_to_expired_or_invalid_token
    secret_logging_forbidden:
    - invite_token_plaintext
    - password_plaintext
    - password_hash
    - two_factor_secret
    rules:
    - Invite token plaintext MUST NOT be logged.
    - Password plaintext MUST NOT be logged.
    - Two-factor secrets MUST NOT be logged.
    - Identity lifecycle actions MUST be auditable.
  forbidden_behavior:
  - tenant_staff_identity_in_platform_database
  - shared_tenant_identity_table_with_tenant_id_filter
  - duplicate_active_email_within_same_tenant
  - tenant_owner_created_from_staff_invite
  - platform_role_assigned_from_tenant_staff_invite
  - plaintext_invite_token_storage
  - plaintext_invite_token_logging
  - password_sent_by_email
  - password_plaintext_logging
  - tenant_staff_login_from_platform_domain
  - disabled_staff_user_login
  - hard_delete_staff_user_initial_version
  - mandatory_two_factor_for_all_staff_initial_version
  open_decisions: []
  done_when:
  - identity_scope_is_defined
  - activation_model_is_defined
  - staff_invite_policy_is_defined
  - password_policy_is_defined
  - two_factor_policy_is_defined
  - login_policy_is_defined
  - staff_lifecycle_is_defined
  - data_contract_expectations_are_defined
  - security_and_audit_rules_are_defined
  - forbidden_identity_behavior_is_defined

documents.title: IoTable Product Baseline
documents.raw_text:
document:
  id: 01-product.product-baseline
  title: IoTable Product Baseline
  project: IoTable
  version: 1.0.0
  language: en
  format: yaml
  schema_required: true
  schema_file: schemas/01_product_baseline.schema.json
  status: draft
product_baseline:
  product_identity:
    name: IoTable
    definition: IoTable is a multi-tenant cafe and restaurant operations platform.
    platform_domain: platform.iotables.net
    tenant_domain_pattern: '{tenant}.iotables.net'
  multi_tenancy:
    tenant_resolution: subdomain
    platform_domain: platform.iotables.net
    tenant_domain_pattern: '{tenant}.iotables.net'
    data_isolation_model: database_per_tenant
    platform_database: iotable_platform
    tenant_database_pattern: iotable_tenant_{tenantSlug}
    rules:
    - Platform data MUST be stored in the platform database.
    - Tenant operational data MUST be stored in the tenant database assigned to that
      tenant.
    - Tenant operational data MUST NOT be stored in the platform database.
    - Platform database records MAY reference tenant registry identifiers.
    - Tenant databases MUST NOT store global platform control-plane data.
  platform_scope:
    platform_user_model: single_platform_owner
    platform_roles:
    - platform_owner
    platform_responsibilities:
    - tenant_lifecycle
    - tenant_creation
    - tenant_provisioning
    - tenant_schema_jobs
    - platform_audit
    - system_health
    - platform_settings
    excluded_responsibilities:
    - tenant_customer_operations
    - tenant_menu_management
    - tenant_order_operations
    - tenant_station_operations
    - tenant_checkout_operations
    rules:
    - The platform control plane is operated by platform_owner.
    - The platform control plane MUST NOT expose tenant operational workflows.
    - The platform control plane MUST manage tenant lifecycle, provisioning, schema
      jobs, platform audit, system health, and platform settings.
  platform_database_scope:
    owns:
    - tenant_registry
    - tenant_domain_registry
    - tenant_provisioning_jobs
    - tenant_database_registry
    - tenant_schema_change_jobs
    - tenant_schema_change_job_runs
    - platform_users
    - platform_roles
    - platform_audit_log
    - global_settings
    - technical_operations_metadata
    does_not_own:
    - restaurant_menus
    - restaurant_tables
    - restaurant_orders
    - guest_sessions
    - kitchen_station_workflows
    - checkout_records
    - tenant_staff_operational_records
  provisioning_lifecycle:
    tenant_statuses:
    - draft
    - provisioning
    - pending_activation
    - active
    - suspended
    - deactivated
    creation_flow:
    - step: 1
      id: create_tenant_record
      actor: platform_owner
      result: A tenant registry record is created in the platform database.
    - step: 2
      id: assign_tenant_slug
      actor: system
      result: A unique tenant slug is assigned.
    - step: 3
      id: assign_tenant_domain
      actor: system
      result: The tenant domain is assigned using the {tenant}.iotables.net pattern.
    - step: 4
      id: create_tenant_database
      actor: system
      result: A dedicated PostgreSQL database is created for the tenant.
    - step: 5
      id: apply_tenant_database_schema
      actor: system
      result: The current tenant database schema is applied.
    - step: 6
      id: seed_tenant_database
      actor: system
      result: Required initial tenant seed data is inserted.
    - step: 7
      id: create_owner_activation_invite
      actor: system
      result: An activation invite is created for the tenant owner.
    - step: 8
      id: set_pending_activation
      actor: system
      result: Tenant status becomes pending_activation.
    - step: 9
      id: complete_owner_activation
      actor: tenant_owner
      result: The tenant owner completes activation.
    - step: 10
      id: set_active
      actor: system
      result: Tenant status becomes active.
    status_behavior:
      suspended:
        tenant_domain_access: available
        tenant_owner_login: allowed
        tenant_manager_login: allowed
        staff_operations: blocked
        customer_ordering: blocked
        table_device_connections: rejected
        tenant_data: preserved
        reactivation_actor: platform_owner
      deactivated:
        tenant_domain_access: blocked
        tenant_user_login: blocked
        customer_ordering: blocked
        table_device_connections: rejected
        tenant_database_state: preserved_locked
        reactivation_actor: platform_owner
  tenant_database_schema_change_orchestration:
    owner: platform
    execution_model: platform_level_migration_orchestrator
    execution_strategy: sequential_per_tenant
    version_tracking: per_tenant_database
    job_tracking: platform_database
    failure_isolation: true
    resumable: true
    new_tenant_schema: latest_available_schema
    rules:
    - Every tenant database MUST track its own schema version.
    - Every tenant database schema change job MUST be recorded in the platform database.
    - A failed tenant schema change MUST NOT corrupt other tenant databases.
    - A failed tenant schema change MUST be resumable from the platform control plane.
    - A new tenant database MUST be created with the latest available tenant schema.
    - Platform owner MUST be able to inspect tenant schema change job status per tenant.
  route_namespace_standard:
    platform_control_plane: https://platform.iotables.net/**
    tenant_staff_app: https://{tenant}.iotables.net/app/**
    tenant_console: https://{tenant}.iotables.net/console/**
    customer_guest: https://{tenant}.iotables.net/g/{token}/**
    technical_health:
    - https://platform.iotables.net/health/**
    - https://{tenant}.iotables.net/health/**
    platform_api: https://platform.iotables.net/api/platform/**
    tenant_internal_api: https://{tenant}.iotables.net/api/internal/**
    customer_public_api: https://{tenant}.iotables.net/api/public/**
    device_realtime: wss://{tenant}.iotables.net/ws/devices/tables/{tableId}
    rules:
    - Platform routes MUST be served only from platform.iotables.net.
    - Tenant staff routes MUST be served under /app on the tenant domain.
    - Tenant configuration routes MUST be served under /console on the tenant domain.
    - Customer guest routes MUST be scoped under /g/{token}.
    - Tenant internal APIs MUST be served under /api/internal on the tenant domain.
    - Customer public APIs MUST be served under /api/public on the tenant domain.
    - Device realtime routes MUST use tableId, not tableNumber, as the canonical table
      identifier.
    - Root-level tenant routes MUST NOT be used for tenant staff, console, customer
      cart, menu, or order-complete flows.
  authentication_authorization:
    authentication_stack:
      identity_store: FastAPI Users & SQLAlchemy
      oidc_oauth_server: OAuth2 Password Flow (JWT) & fastapi.security
      token_validation: PyJWT (HS256/RS256 Token Verification)
      authorization_runtime: FastAPI Dependency Injection & Custom Permission Guards
    authorization_model:
      model: fixed_roles_with_permission_enforcement
      custom_roles: false
      custom_permission_editing: false
      role_usage: roles_are_permission_bundles
      ui_assignment_unit: role
      enforcement_unit: permission
      backend_rule: Backend authorization MUST check permissions, not role names.
      namespace_rule: Platform permissions and tenant permissions MUST be separate
        namespaces.
      canonical_specification: specs/03_domain_model.yaml
    platform_roles:
    - platform_owner
    tenant_roles:
    - tenant_owner
    - tenant_admin
    - tenant_manager
    - cashier
    - floor_staff
    - station_operator
    - accountant
    rules:
    - Roles MUST be assigned to users for operational manageability.
    - Permissions MUST be used as the backend enforcement unit.
    - Owner roles MUST receive all permissions inside their scope.
    - Tenant role permission bundles MUST be fixed in the current product version.
    - Tenant users MUST NOT create custom roles.
    - Tenant users MUST NOT edit permission bundles.
    - The platform_owner role MUST NOT grant tenant database operational permissions
      unless explicitly mapped through tenant-scoped permissions.
    - Tenant roles MUST NOT grant platform control-plane permissions.
    - Authentication tokens MUST carry enough claims to resolve user identity, tenant
      context, and permission scope.
  payment_checkout_scope:
    mode: bill_tracking_and_manual_payment_recording
    menu_item_prices: true
    order_total_calculation: true
    bill_tracking: true
    manual_payment_status_tracking: true
    manual_payment_method_recording: true
    online_payment_collection: false
    payment_gateway_integration: false
    pos_integration: false
    fiscal_device_integration: false
    e_invoice_or_e_archive: false
    rules:
    - IoTable MUST store menu item prices.
    - IoTable MUST calculate order and bill totals from tenant menu prices and ordered
      items.
    - IoTable MUST support adisyon-style bill tracking.
    - IoTable MAY record manually entered payment method information for operational
      tracking.
    - IoTable MUST NOT collect money directly from customers.
    - IoTable MUST NOT integrate with payment gateways.
    - IoTable MUST NOT integrate with POS devices.
    - IoTable MUST NOT integrate with fiscal devices.
    - IoTable MUST NOT generate e-invoice or e-archive documents.
    - Payment collection is handled outside IoTable.
  customer_ordering_session_baseline:
    canonical_specification: specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
    menu_access:
      old_or_expired_qr_can_open_public_menu: true
      old_or_expired_qr_can_show_active_bill_indicator: false
      old_or_expired_qr_can_create_or_join_session: false
      old_or_expired_qr_can_view_bill: false
      old_or_expired_qr_can_mutate_cart: false
      old_or_expired_qr_can_submit_order: false
    fresh_qr_session_entry:
      required_for_session_entry: true
      creates_or_joins_session: true
      consumes_qr: true
      rotates_qr_after_consume: true
      requires_cart_item: false
    active_customer_session:
      can_view_bill: true
      can_mutate_cart: true
      can_submit_order: true
    order_submit_effect:
      creates_bill_session_when_first_submitted_order_for_table: true
      submitted_order_attaches_to_table_bill_session: true
      submitted_cart_becomes_immutable: true
      customer_session_becomes_offline_after_success: true
    table_bill_session:
      scope: table
      shared_by_multiple_customers: true
      closing_actor: cashier
      closing_trigger: manual_payment_received_recorded_by_cashier
    station_flow:
      submitted_items_route_to_station: true
      station_acceptance_required: true
      station_ready_mark_required: true
    rules:
    - A table bill session MUST NOT exist only because the menu was opened.
    - A table bill session MUST be created when the first order is submitted for the
      table.
    - Old or expired QR codes MUST expose public menu only.
    - Fresh QR session entry MUST NOT require a cart item.
    - Cart mutation MUST require an active customer session.
    - Cart mutation MUST NOT require a fresh QR for every item.
    - Order submission MUST require an active customer session.
    - Successful order submission MUST move the customer session offline.
    - Multiple customers MAY participate at the same table.
    - Customers joining the same table MUST write submitted orders to the same table
      bill session.
    - The cashier MUST close the table bill session manually after external payment
      collection.
    - Submitted order items MUST be routed to the relevant station workflow.
    - Stations MUST acknowledge accepted work.
    - Stations MUST mark produced items as ready.
  device_baseline:
    canonical_specification: specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
    table_device_type: esp32_tft_qr_display
    device_scope: tenant_table
    device_represents: one_physical_table_qr_display
    qr_generation: server_only
    device_authentication: device_secret
    device_configuration: table_specific_generated_firmware
    rules:
    - ESP32 devices MUST NOT generate QR tokens.
    - ESP32 devices MUST display only server-generated QR payloads.
    - One ESP32 QR display device MUST be assigned to exactly one tenant table.
    - A table MAY have at most one active QR display device.
    - Server MUST remain source of truth for QR state.
  stakeholders:
  - id: platform_owner
    primary_goal: Manage the IoTable platform control plane and observe tenant lifecycle
      state.
  - id: tenant_owner
    primary_goal: Configure the cafe or restaurant and complete initial setup.
  - id: tenant_manager
    primary_goal: Manage menu, staff, stations, tables, and tenant settings.
  - id: cashier_floor_staff
    primary_goal: Run floor flow, table state, and checkout workflow.
  - id: station_operator
    primary_goal: Progress assigned station work through station workflows.
  - id: customer_diner
    primary_goal: Join through QR, browse menu, place order, and track status.
  - id: technical_operator
    primary_goal: Deploy, check health, recover, rotate secrets, and perform maintenance.
  - id: table_device
    primary_goal: Display and refresh QR tokens through an ESP32 table device.
  application_surface_families:
  - id: platform_control_plane
    audience:
    - platform_owner
    route_family:
    - https://platform.iotables.net/**
  - id: tenant_setup_console
    audience:
    - tenant_owner
    - tenant_manager
    route_family:
    - https://{tenant}.iotables.net/console/**
  - id: floor_cash_workspace
    audience:
    - cashier_floor_staff
    - tenant_manager
    route_family:
    - https://{tenant}.iotables.net/app/service/**
    - https://{tenant}.iotables.net/app/tables/{tableId}/**
    - https://{tenant}.iotables.net/app/orders/{orderId}/**
  - id: station_board
    audience:
    - station_operator
    - tenant_manager
    route_family:
    - https://{tenant}.iotables.net/app/stations/**
    - https://{tenant}.iotables.net/app/stations/{stationCode}/**
  - id: customer_ordering
    audience:
    - customer_diner
    route_family:
    - https://{tenant}.iotables.net/g/{token}/**
  - id: identity_activation
    audience:
    - platform_owner
    - tenant_owner
    - tenant_manager
    - cashier_floor_staff
    - station_operator
    route_family:
    - https://platform.iotables.net/login
    - https://platform.iotables.net/change-password
    - https://{tenant}.iotables.net/login
    - https://{tenant}.iotables.net/change-password
    - https://{tenant}.iotables.net/activate
    - https://{tenant}.iotables.net/login-2fa
  - id: technical_operations
    audience:
    - technical_operator
    route_family:
    - https://platform.iotables.net/health/**
    - https://{tenant}.iotables.net/health/**
  - id: table_device_endpoint
    audience:
    - table_device
    route_family:
    - wss://{tenant}.iotables.net/ws/devices/tables/{tableId}
  api_families:
  - id: platform_api
    audience:
    - platform_owner
    route_family:
    - https://platform.iotables.net/api/platform/**
  - id: customer_public_api
    audience:
    - customer_diner
    route_family:
    - https://{tenant}.iotables.net/api/public/**
  - id: tenant_internal_api
    audience:
    - tenant_owner
    - tenant_manager
    - cashier_floor_staff
    - station_operator
    route_family:
    - https://{tenant}.iotables.net/api/internal/**
  product_decision_references:
  - id: PDR-001
    topic: Customer access security
    canonical_document: specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
  - id: PDR-002
    topic: Frontend application split
    canonical_document: specs/02_system_architecture.yaml
  - id: PDR-003
    topic: Platform control plane
    canonical_document: specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
  - id: PDR-004
    topic: Platform API contracts
    canonical_document: specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
  - id: PDR-005
    topic: Platform data contracts
    canonical_document: specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
  - id: PDR-006
    topic: Permission catalog and role-permission matrix
    canonical_document: specs/03_domain_model.yaml
  - id: PDR-007
    topic: ESP32 device identity
    canonical_document: specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
  open_product_decisions: []
  done_when:
  - product_identity_is_defined
  - multi_tenancy_baseline_is_defined
  - platform_scope_is_defined
  - single_platform_owner_model_is_defined
  - provisioning_lifecycle_is_defined
  - tenant_status_behavior_is_defined
  - tenant_database_schema_change_orchestration_is_defined
  - route_namespace_standard_is_defined
  - authentication_authorization_baseline_matches_permission_catalog
  - payment_checkout_scope_is_defined
  - customer_ordering_session_baseline_matches_customer_access_security
  - device_baseline_matches_esp32_device_identity
  - stakeholders_are_defined
  - application_surface_families_are_defined
  - api_families_are_defined
  - product_decision_references_are_defined

documents.title: IoTable Meta Policies
documents.raw_text:
document:
  id: 00-meta.meta-policies
  title: IoTable Meta Policies
  project: IoTable
  version: 1.0.0
  language: en
  format: yaml
  schema_required: true
  schema_file: schemas/00_meta_policies.schema.json
  status: draft
documentation_index:
  repository:
    canonical_branch: master
    source_root: specs
    schema_root: schemas
    tools_root: tools
  required_session_bootstrap:
    rule: Specification work MUST start by reading the required meta documents.
    documents:
    - path: specs/00_meta_policies.yaml
      purpose: Defines binding specification rules and LLM behavior.
    - path: specs/00_meta_policies.yaml
      purpose: Defines the specification document set and dependency order.
    - path: specs/00_meta_policies.yaml
      purpose: Defines source authority, allowed source usage, and conflict handling.
    - path: specs/00_meta_policies.yaml
      purpose: Defines research triggers, source order, fit criteria, and recommendation
        handling.
    - path: specs/00_meta_policies.yaml
      purpose: Defines mandatory behavior for LLM specification and coding agents.
  specification_sections:
  - id: 00-meta
    path: specs/00-meta
    schema_path: schemas/00-meta
    purpose: Defines repository-level specification rules, policies, indexes, and
      execution constraints.
    required: true
    documents:
    - id: 00-meta.specification-constitution
      path: specs/00_meta_policies.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/00-meta/specification-constitution.schema.json
      status: draft
    - id: 00-meta.documentation-index
      path: specs/00_meta_policies.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/00-meta/documentation-index.schema.json
      status: draft
    - id: 00-meta.source-policy
      path: specs/00_meta_policies.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/00-meta/source-policy.schema.json
      status: draft
    - id: 00-meta.research-policy
      path: specs/00_meta_policies.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/00-meta/research-policy.schema.json
      status: draft
    - id: 00-meta.llm-execution-policy
      path: specs/00_meta_policies.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/00-meta/llm-execution-policy.schema.json
      status: draft
  - id: 01-product
    path: specs/01-product
    schema_path: schemas/01-product
    purpose: Defines product identity, stakeholders, surfaces, scope, non-goals, and
      owner-approved behavior.
    required: true
    documents:
    - id: 01-product.product-baseline
      path: specs/01_product_baseline.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/01-product/product-baseline.schema.json
      status: draft
  - id: 02-system-architecture
    path: specs/02-system-architecture
    schema_path: schemas/02-system-architecture
    purpose: Defines system architecture, module boundaries, runtime topology, frontend
      application isolation, backend solution structure, and architectural decisions.
    required: true
    documents:
    - id: 02-system-architecture.architecture-style
      path: specs/02_system_architecture.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/02-system-architecture/architecture-style.schema.json
      status: draft
    - id: 02-system-architecture.frontend-application-split
      path: specs/02_system_architecture.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/02-system-architecture/frontend-application-split.schema.json
      status: draft
    - id: 02-system-architecture.backend-solution-structure
      path: specs/02_system_architecture.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/02-system-architecture/backend-solution-structure.schema.json
      status: draft
  - id: 03-domain
    path: specs/03-domain
    schema_path: schemas/03-domain
    purpose: Defines business capability boundaries, aggregates, domain events, commands,
      queries, ownership rules, and operation workflows.
    required: true
    documents:
    - id: 03-domain.station-order-routing
      path: specs/03_domain_model.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/03-domain/station-order-routing.schema.json
      status: draft
    - id: 03-domain.module-inventory
      path: specs/03_domain_model.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/03-domain/module-inventory.schema.json
      status: draft
    - id: 03-domain.tenant-setup-workflows
      path: specs/03_domain_model.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/03-domain/tenant-setup-workflows.schema.json
      status: draft
    - id: 03-domain.customer-order-session-workflow
      path: specs/03_domain_model.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/03-domain/customer-order-session-workflow.schema.json
      status: draft
    - id: 03-domain.bill-session-workflow
      path: specs/03_domain_model.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/03-domain/bill-session-workflow.schema.json
      status: draft
  - id: 04-tenancy
    path: specs/04-tenancy
    schema_path: schemas/04-tenancy
    purpose: Defines tenant resolution, tenant lifecycle, tenant isolation, provisioning,
      and tenant context propagation.
    required: true
    documents:
    - id: 04-tenancy.tenant-resolution
      path: specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/04-tenancy/tenant-resolution.schema.json
      status: draft
    - id: 04-tenancy.tenant-lifecycle-provisioning
      path: specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/04-tenancy/tenant-lifecycle-provisioning.schema.json
      status: draft
  - id: 05-identity-access
    path: specs/05-identity-access
    schema_path: schemas/05-identity-access
