    purpose: Defines authentication, authorization, user roles, permissions, activation,
      and session behavior.
    required: true
    documents:
    - id: 05-identity-access.permission-catalog
      path: specs/03_domain_model.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/05-identity-access/permission-catalog.schema.json
      status: draft
    - id: 05-identity-access.tenant-staff-identity
      path: specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/05-identity-access/tenant-staff-identity.schema.json
      status: draft
  - id: 06-application-surfaces
    path: specs/06-application-surfaces
    schema_path: schemas/06-application-surfaces
    purpose: Maps user-facing and device-facing application surfaces to capabilities,
      routes, permissions, and contracts.
    required: true
    documents:
    - id: 06-application-surfaces.platform-control-plane
      path: specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/06-application-surfaces/platform-control-plane.schema.json
      status: draft
    - id: 06-application-surfaces.platform-provisioning-retry
      path: specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/06-application-surfaces/platform-provisioning-retry.schema.json
      status: draft
    - id: 06-application-surfaces.platform-settings-catalog
      path: specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/06-application-surfaces/platform-settings-catalog.schema.json
      status: draft
    - id: 06-application-surfaces.platform-audit-catalog
      path: specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/06-application-surfaces/platform-audit-catalog.schema.json
      status: draft
    - id: 06-application-surfaces.tenant-setup-console
      path: specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/06-application-surfaces/tenant-setup-console.schema.json
      status: draft
  - id: 07-api-contracts
    path: specs/07-api-contracts
    schema_path: schemas/07-api-contracts
    purpose: Defines HTTP API contracts, request and response shapes, errors, pagination,
      idempotency, and authentication requirements.
    required: true
    documents:
    - id: 07-api-contracts.platform-api
      path: specs/04_application_contracts.yaml

    - id: 07-api-contracts.tenant-setup-api

    - id: 07-api-contracts.tenant-staff-identity-api

    - id: 07-api-contracts.customer-order-api

  - id: 08-data-contracts

    - id: 08-data-contracts.platform-data

    - id: 08-data-contracts.tenant-setup-data
      path: specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/08-data-contracts/tenant-setup-data.schema.json
      status: draft
    - id: 08-data-contracts.customer-order-data
      path: specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/08-data-contracts/customer-order-data.schema.json
      status: draft
    - id: 08-data-contracts.bill-session-data

      schema_file: schemas/08-data-contracts/bill-session-data.schema.json

  - id: 09-realtime-device-contracts

  - id: 13-security-compliance

  - id: 14-ui-ux-system
    path: specs/14-ui-ux-system

  - id: 15-engineering-rules
    path: specs/15-engineering-rules
    schema_path: schemas/15-engineering-rules
    purpose: Defines coding standards, naming conventions, dependency rules, error
      handling, logging, and repository conventions.
    required: true
    documents:
    - id: 15-engineering-rules.technology-version-baseline
      path: specs/02_system_architecture.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/15-engineering-rules/technology-version-baseline.schema.json
      status: draft
  - id: 16-research-decisions
    path: specs/16-research-decisions
    schema_path: schemas/16-research-decisions
    purpose: Defines researched technical decisions, accepted recommendations, rejected
      options, and source-grounded decision records.
    required: true
    documents:
    - id: 16-research-decisions.background-cache-locking-decision
      path: specs/02_system_architecture.yaml
      schema_file: schemas/16-research-decisions/background-cache-locking-decision.schema.json
      status: draft
  folder_creation_policy:
    rule: A specification section folder MAY exist before documents are added. Empty
      section folders are allowed only as repository structure markers.
  schema_policy:
    rule: Every YAML specification document MUST have a matching JSON Schema file.
    schema_location_rule: Schema files MUST mirror specification section paths under
      the schemas root.
  open_items: []
llm_execution_policy:
  purpose:
    rule: Define mandatory behavior for LLM agents that create, modify, or use the
      IoTable Implementation Specification.
    applies_to:
    - specification_authoring_agents
    - coding_agents
    - review_agents
    - research_agents
  session_bootstrap:
    rule: No IoTable specification work may start before the required meta documents
      are loaded from the repository.
    required_reads:
    - specs/00_meta_policies.yaml
    - specs/00_meta_policies.yaml
    - specs/00_meta_policies.yaml
    - specs/00_meta_policies.yaml
    - specs/00_meta_policies.yaml
    memory_policy: Repository files are binding. LLM memory is not binding.
  execution_modes:
  - id: specification_authoring
    description: The agent creates or modifies specification documents.
    required_behaviors:
    - read_meta_documents
    - preserve_final_state_wording
    - write_machine_readable_yaml
    - write_matching_json_schema
    - avoid_unapproved_product_decisions
    - record_open_decisions_when_needed
  - id: technical_research
    description: The agent researches technical, convention, or stack-specific decisions.
    required_behaviors:
    - use_authoritative_sources
    - separate_fact_from_recommendation
    - record_options_and_tradeoffs
    - mark_product_decisions_for_owner
  - id: implementation_execution
    description: The agent implements code from the specification.
    required_behaviors:
    - follow_task_order
    - create_specified_files
    - modify_specified_files
    - write_required_tests
    - run_verification_commands
    - report_blockers
  allowed_actions:
  - read_repository_files
  - create_specification_files
  - update_specification_files
  - create_matching_schema_files
  - update_matching_schema_files
  - research_authoritative_sources
  - record_decision_options
  - recommend_technical_options
  - report_blockers
  prohibited_actions:
  - rely_on_memory_for_binding_rules
  - invent_product_behavior
  - invent_role_permission
  - invent_api_contract
  - invent_database_field
  - skip_required_schema
  - write_freeform_unvalidated_specification
  - introduce_external_context_dependency
  - use_chronology_based_wording
  - use_patch_style_wording
  - hide_uncertainty
  - skip_verification_when_verification_is_defined
  blocker_policy:
    rule: A blocker record is required when execution cannot continue without inventing
      product behavior or violating a binding rule.
    blocker_required_when:
    - product_behavior_missing
    - owner_decision_required
    - source_conflict_affects_requirement
    - technical_claim_requires_current_research
    - schema_contract_missing
    - task_dependency_missing
    blocker_record_required_fields:
    - blocker_id
    - blocked_area
    - reason
    - required_decision_or_source
    - safe_next_action
  specification_write_rules:
  - id: SWR-001
    rule: Every YAML specification file MUST declare its matching JSON Schema path.
  - id: SWR-002
    rule: Every specification file MUST be written as current final system state.
  - id: SWR-003
    rule: Every added field MUST be represented in the matching schema.
  - id: SWR-004
    rule: Every open product behavior MUST be recorded as an open decision.
  - id: SWR-005
    rule: Every implementation task MUST include done_when criteria before it can
      be used by a coding agent.
  - id: SWR-006
    rule: All repository-level documentation and technical specifications MUST be written exclusively in structured formats (such as YAML, JSON Schema, and ETL-compiled binary indexes) designed for AI/LLM consumption. Human-oriented Markdown (.md) or text (.txt) files are strictly prohibited inside the codebase root to prevent documentation drift and minimize LLM context token overhead.
  implementation_task_execution_rules:
  - id: ITER-001
    rule: The coding agent MUST implement tasks in dependency order.
  - id: ITER-002
    rule: The coding agent MUST NOT implement unspecified API behavior.
  - id: ITER-003
    rule: The coding agent MUST NOT add packages that are not allowed by the specification.
  - id: ITER-004
    rule: The coding agent MUST run verification commands when specified.
  - id: ITER-005
    rule: The coding agent MUST report a blocker instead of guessing.
  reporting_policy:
    required_report_sections:
    - files_read
    - files_created
    - files_updated
    - decisions_used
    - open_blockers
    - validation_status
    rule: Agent reports MUST be concise, factual, and tied to repository files.
  done_when:
  - session_bootstrap_is_defined
  - execution_modes_are_defined
  - allowed_actions_are_defined
  - prohibited_actions_are_defined
  - blocker_policy_is_defined
  - specification_write_rules_are_defined
  - implementation_task_execution_rules_are_defined
research_policy:
  purpose:
    rule: Define how research is performed before technical, convention, and implementation
      decisions enter the IoTable Implementation Specification.
    applies_to:
    - technology_selection
    - architecture_decisions
    - framework_usage
    - security_controls
    - deployment_patterns
    - ui_ux_patterns
    - coding_conventions
  research_triggers:
  - id: RT-001
    trigger: A version-sensitive technology claim is made.
    action: research_authoritative_current_sources
  - id: RT-002
    trigger: Multiple valid technical options exist.
    action: compare_options_using_fit_criteria
  - id: RT-003
    trigger: A convention is proposed as proven practice.
    action: verify_against_authoritative_or_mature_sources
  - id: RT-004
    trigger: A security-sensitive behavior is specified.
    action: check_official_and_owasp_aligned_sources
  - id: RT-005
    trigger: A stack-specific implementation pattern is required.
    action: check_framework_and_vendor_guidance
  technology_selection_policy:
    rule: Select modern-proven technology.
    prefer:
    - current_stable_major_versions
    - active_maintenance
    - production_adoption
    - strong_ecosystem_support
    - official_documentation_quality
    - security_maturity
    - long_term_viability
    - operational_fit_for_ubuntu_vps
    - llm_implementation_clarity
    avoid:
    - bleeding_edge_for_core_system
    - obsolete_stable_choices
    - hype_driven_selection
    - unmaintained_libraries
    - unclear_licensing
    - weak_documentation
  fit_criteria_order:
  - correctness
  - security
  - maintainability
  - operational_simplicity
  - llm_implementability
  - performance
  - developer_convenience
  - cost_efficiency
  source_order:
  - official_documentation
  - formal_standards
  - framework_author_recommendations
  - mature_open_source_projects
  - major_engineering_organizations
  - reputable_engineering_blogs
  research_output_statuses:
  - id: accepted
    meaning: The recommendation is accepted into the specification.
  - id: rejected
    meaning: The recommendation is not used.
  - id: needs_owner_decision
    meaning: The research affects product behavior or business policy and requires
      project owner decision.
  - id: research_only
    meaning: The research is recorded for context and does not define specification
      behavior.
  research_record_format:
    required_fields:
    - decision_id
    - topic
    - status
    - problem
    - options
    - selected_option
    - source_records
    - fit_assessment
    - risks
    - spec_impact
    option_required_fields:
    - id
    - description
    - advantages
    - disadvantages
    - fit_score
    - source_support
  prohibited_research_behaviors:
  - using_llm_memory_as_authority
  - selecting_newest_without_maturity_check
  - selecting_oldest_without_fit_check
  - omitting_security_tradeoffs
  - omitting_operational_tradeoffs
  - presenting_recommendation_as_owner_decision
  - using_single_low_authority_source_for_binding_decision
  recommendation_policy:
    rule: The assistant MAY recommend the best technical option after research, but
      MUST distinguish recommendation from binding product decision.
    owner_required_when:
    - product_behavior_changes
    - business_workflow_changes
    - role_or_permission_scope_changes
    - payment_or_fiscal_scope_changes
    - tenant_lifecycle_changes
  reference_model_policy:
    ui_product_references:
    - Figma
    - Canva
    - Notion
    ui_design_system_references:
    - Ant Design
    - Material Design
    - shadcn/ui
    - Radix UI
    engineering_ecosystem_references:
    - Microsoft
    - Google
    - Linux
    - Meta
    - PostgreSQL
    - CNCF ecosystem
    rule: Reference models are used for comparison and pattern evidence. They do not
      define IoTable requirements.
  done_when:
  - research_triggers_are_defined
  - technology_selection_policy_is_defined
  - fit_criteria_order_is_defined
  - research_output_statuses_are_defined
  - research_record_format_is_defined
  - prohibited_research_behaviors_are_defined
source_policy:
  purpose:
    rule: Define how sources are classified, evaluated, and allowed to influence the
      IoTable Implementation Specification.
    applies_to:
    - all_specification_documents
    - all_research_decisions
    - all_llm_generated_specification_changes
  source_authority_levels:
  - id: project_owner_binding_decision
    authority: binding
    can_define_product_behavior: true
    can_define_technical_constraint: true
    approval_required: false
    description: A direct decision from the project owner.
  - id: provided_product_source
    authority: high
    can_define_product_behavior: true
    can_define_technical_constraint: false
    approval_required: true
    description: A project-owner-provided product note, workflow, role definition,
      surface list, or domain rule.
  - id: official_technical_documentation
    authority: high
    can_define_product_behavior: false
    can_define_technical_constraint: true
    approval_required: false
    description: Official documentation from the vendor, standards body, framework
      team, or project maintainers.
  - id: formal_standard_or_rfc
    authority: high
    can_define_product_behavior: false
    can_define_technical_constraint: true
    approval_required: false
    description: A formal specification, standard, RFC, or published protocol definition.
  - id: framework_author_guidance
    authority: medium_high
    can_define_product_behavior: false
    can_define_technical_constraint: true
    approval_required: false
    description: Guidance from maintainers or authors of the framework used by IoTable.
  - id: mature_open_source_project
    authority: medium
    can_define_product_behavior: false
    can_define_technical_constraint: false
    approval_required: true
    description: A mature open-source project used as pattern evidence, not as a binding
      source.
  - id: major_engineering_organization_practice
    authority: medium
    can_define_product_behavior: false
    can_define_technical_constraint: false
    approval_required: true
    description: Engineering practice from major organizations used as comparison
      evidence.
  - id: reputable_engineering_article
    authority: low
    can_define_product_behavior: false
    can_define_technical_constraint: false
    approval_required: true
    description: A reputable article used only to support research context or tradeoff
      analysis.
  - id: llm_memory
    authority: none
    can_define_product_behavior: false
    can_define_technical_constraint: false
    approval_required: true
    description: LLM memory can propose search directions only. It cannot define specification
      content.
  source_usage_rules:
  - id: SUR-001
    rule: A source is evidence, not inspiration.
  - id: SUR-002
    rule: Product behavior MUST come from a project owner decision or an owner-accepted
      product source.
  - id: SUR-003
    rule: Technical facts that are version-sensitive MUST be checked against current
      authoritative sources before entering the specification.
  - id: SUR-004
    rule: Reference products MAY influence comparison, but MUST NOT be copied as requirements.
  - id: SUR-005
    rule: A source conflict MUST be recorded as a decision item before the specification
      is frozen.
  - id: SUR-006
    rule: No requirement may depend on external context that is not represented in
      the repository.
  source_record_format:
    required_fields:
    - source_id
    - source_type
    - authority_level
    - locator
    - retrieved_at
    - used_for
    - extracted_facts
    - limitations
    - spec_impact
    optional_fields:
    - version
    - publisher
    - maintainer
    - related_decision_id
  conflict_resolution:
    rule: Source conflicts are not silently resolved by the LLM.
    required_process:
    - identify_conflict
    - classify_conflict
    - list_affected_specification_area
    - present_resolution_options
    - record_owner_decision_when_product_behavior_is_affected
  prohibited_source_handling:
  - treating_llm_output_as_source
  - turning_examples_into_requirements_without_owner_acceptance
  - using_reference_product_behavior_as_binding_behavior
  - combining_conflicting_sources_without_decision_record
  - omitting_source_limitations
  - using_unchecked_version_sensitive_memory
  done_when:
  - all_source_authority_levels_are_defined
  - source_usage_rules_are_machine_readable
  - conflict_resolution_rules_are_defined
  - prohibited_source_handling_is_defined
specification_constitution:
  planning_constitutional_law:
    rule: Acele edilmeyecek, 10 adım sonrasını planlamadan harekete geçilmeyecek.
    description: This is the first and most absolute law of the IoTable specification constitution. It is an idiomatic mandate meaning 'do not take action or begin code implementation without fully securing your footing first' (sağlama almadan harekete geçmeme). No execution or coding may begin until all specifications, schemas, architectural boundaries, and design details are fully validated, aligned, and approved.
  product_identity:
    canonical_name: IoTable
    canonical_definition: IoTable is a multi-tenant cafe and restaurant operations
      platform.
    platform_domain: platform.iotables.net
    tenant_domain_pattern: '{tenant}.iotables.net'
  specification_purpose:
    primary_purpose: Define a binding, pre-code implementation specification for LLM
      coding agents.
    intended_reader:
    - LLM coding agent
    non_intended_reader:
    - end user
    - marketing team
    - sales team
    - general business stakeholder
    code_generation_binding: true
    code_must_follow_specification: true
    specification_precedes_code: true
  source_of_truth_policy:
    highest_authority: project_owner
    project_owner: user
    source_priority_order:
    - project_owner_binding_decision
    - provided_product_source
    - official_technical_documentation
    - formal_standard_or_rfc
    - framework_author_guidance
    - mature_open_source_project
    - major_engineering_organization_practice
    - reputable_engineering_article
    llm_memory_authority: none
    llm_output_authority: none
    rule: No requirement may enter the specification unless it is derived from an
      accepted source or accepted owner decision.
  missing_information_policy:
    rule: Missing, incomplete, or ambiguous information MUST NOT remain unresolved.
    prohibited_behaviors:
    - inventing_product_behavior
    - silently_filling_gaps
    - presenting_guess_as_fact
    - using_llm_memory_as_binding_source
    - hiding_uncertainty
    required_behaviors:
    - identify_gap
    - classify_gap
    - research_technical_or_convention_gap
    - present_options_when_multiple_valid_paths_exist
    - separate_fact_from_recommendation
    - request_owner_decision_for_product_behavior
    gap_types:
      technical_gap:
        action: research_authoritative_sources
      product_gap:
        action: request_owner_decision
      convention_gap:
        action: research_proven_industry_conventions
      implementation_gap:
        action: research_stack_specific_patterns
      decision_gap:
        action: present_options_and_recommendation_before_freezing
  research_policy:
    goal: Select modern, proven, suitable options for IoTable.
    technology_selection_rule: Prefer modern-proven technology over both bleeding-edge
      and obsolete-stable choices.
    source_order:
    - official_documentation
    - formal_standards
    - framework_author_recommendations
    - mature_open_source_projects
    - major_engineering_organizations
    - reputable_engineering_blogs
    forbidden:
    - choosing_newest_without_maturity_check
    - choosing_oldest_only_because_stable
    - treating_blog_content_as_binding
    - copying_reference_product_behavior_without_fit_check
    reference_models:
      ui_product_references:
      - Figma
      - Canva
      - Notion
      ui_design_system_references:
      - Ant Design
      - Material Design
      - shadcn/ui
      - Radix UI
      engineering_ecosystem_references:
      - Microsoft
      - Google
      - Linux
      - Meta
      - PostgreSQL
      - CNCF ecosystem
    reference_rule: References are evidence and comparison material, not requirements.
  document_final_state_policy:
    rule: Every specification document MUST be written as the current final form of
      the system.
    prohibited:
    - chronology_based_wording
    - rename_history_wording
    - transition_wording
    - patch_language
    - temporary_fix_language
    - external_context_dependency
    required:
    - self_contained_current_state
    - direct_final_wording
    - current_names_only
    - no_changelog_style_requirements
    rule_detail: Corrections MUST be applied directly as final wording.
  format_policy:
    source_format: yaml
    validation_format: json_schema
    every_yaml_requires_schema: true
    invalid_conditions:
    - yaml_file_without_matching_json_schema
    - schema_without_corresponding_yaml_file
    - undocumented_field
    - freeform_requirement_without_type
    preferred_structures:
    - explicit_ids
    - typed_records
    - enumerated_statuses
    - done_when_blocks
    - acceptance_criteria_blocks
    - forbidden_blocks
    - dependencies_blocks
  branch_policy:
    single_branch_only: true
    canonical_branch: master
    pull_request_workflow: false
    feature_branches_allowed: false
    direct_commits_allowed: true
  writing_policy:
    tone: strict_literal_technical
    allowed_sentence_types:
    - requirement
    - constraint
    - decision
    - dependency
    - validation_rule
    - open_question
    - research_finding
    - risk
    - recommendation
    forbidden_sentence_types:
    - marketing_language
    - motivational_language
    - vague_advice
    - ungrounded_best_practice
    - hidden_assumption
    - narrative_filler
    ambiguity_policy:
      forbidden_terms_unless_defined:
      - appropriate
      - as needed
      - etc.
      - similar
      - usually
      - basically
      - normal
      - simple
      - standard
  llm_execution_policy:
    target_executor: LLM coding agent
    expected_capabilities:
    - read_specification
    - follow_task_order
    - create_files
    - modify_files
    - generate_code
    - generate_tests
    - run_verification_commands
    - report_blockers
    - avoid_unapproved_assumptions
    prohibited_behaviors:
    - skip_specified_tests
    - change_architecture_without_spec_update
    - create_undocumented_dependency
    - invent_api_contract
    - invent_database_column
    - invent_role_permission
    - rename_domain_concept_without_approval
    blocker_rule: If required product behavior is missing, the LLM MUST stop that
      task and emit a blocker record.
  session_context_policy:
    rule: The LLM MUST NOT rely on memory for binding project rules.
    required_behavior: The LLM MUST load the current meta specification documents
      before performing specification work.
  architecture_documentation_policy:
    primary_partitioning_method: business_capability_boundary
    secondary_partitioning_methods:
    - aggregate_boundary
    - transaction_ownership
    - data_ownership
    - api_contract_boundary
    forbidden_primary_partitioning_methods:
    - frontend_backend_split
    - stakeholder_split
    - screen_split
    - route_split
    rule: Frontend, backend, data, and tests are implementation aspects inside a capability
      boundary.
  engineering_principles:
    architecture:
    - separation_of_concerns
    - dependency_inversion
    - explicit_boundaries
    - modularity
    - high_cohesion
    - low_coupling
    - clean_architecture_where_useful
    - domain_driven_design_where_useful
    code_quality:
    - DRY
    - SOLID
    - KISS
    - YAGNI
    - fail_fast
    - explicit_over_implicit
    - no_magic_values
    - no_hidden_side_effects
    - deterministic_behavior
    api_and_contracts:
    - contract_first
    - explicit_error_models
    - idempotency_where_needed
    - pagination_for_lists
    - stable_resource_naming
    security:
    - least_privilege
    - defense_in_depth
    - secure_by_default
    - secrets_never_in_source
    - auditability_for_sensitive_actions
    testing:
    - testable_units
    - contract_tests_for_apis
    - integration_tests_for_boundaries
    - database_schema_change_tests
    - no_task_done_without_tests
    - reproducible_test_commands
    operations:
    - observable_by_default
    - health_checks_required
    - structured_logging
    - safe_failure_modes
    - recovery_documented
    - deployment_steps_reproducible
    documentation:
    - source_grounded
    - machine_readable
    - self_contained
    - no_unstated_assumptions
    - no_vague_language
    - every_requirement_traceable
    - every_task_has_done_when
  done_definition:
    for_specification_document:
      done_when:
      - document_has_matching_json_schema
      - all_required_fields_are_present
      - all_product_behavior_is_owner_approved_or_marked_as_open_decision
      - all_technical_claims_are_researchable_or_source_grounded
      - all_ambiguous_terms_are_removed_or_defined
      - document_can_be_consumed_by_llm_without_external_context
    for_implementation_task:
      done_when:
      - specified_files_created_or_modified
      - specified_behavior_implemented
      - api_contracts_respected
      - database_changes_applied_if_required
      - unit_tests_added_or_updated
      - integration_tests_added_or_updated_when_boundary_is_touched
      - verification_commands_pass
      - no_undocumented_assumptions_introduced
  open_decisions: []

documents.title: IoTable Testing and Operations
documents.raw_text:
document:
  id: 05-testing-operations.testing-operations
  title: IoTable Testing and Operations
  project: IoTable
  version: 1.0.0
  language: en
  format: yaml
  schema_required: true
  schema_file: schemas/05_testing_operations.schema.json
  status: draft
acceptance_criteria:
  purpose:
    rule: Define stable, testable acceptance criteria for owner-approved IoTable behavior.
    applies_to:
    - implementation_tasks
    - unit_tests
    - integration_tests
    - contract_tests
    - end_to_end_tests
    - release_verification
  governance:
    id_format: AC-[0-9]{3}
    stability_rule: Acceptance criterion identifiers MUST NOT be reused for different
      behavior.
    source_rule: Each acceptance criterion MUST cite at least one authoritative IoTable
      specification source.
    granularity_rule: Each acceptance criterion MUST describe one externally verifiable
      behavior or invariant.
    change_rule: Changing accepted product behavior MUST update the source specification
      and the affected acceptance criteria in the same specification change.
  criteria:
  - id: AC-001
    category: tenancy
    statement: Platform data MUST be stored in the platform database.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/01_product_baseline.yaml#multi_tenancy
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
  - id: AC-002
    category: tenancy
    statement: Tenant operational data MUST be stored only in the database assigned
      to that tenant.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/01_product_baseline.yaml#multi_tenancy
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
  - id: AC-003
    category: tenancy
    statement: Tenant databases MUST NOT store global platform control-plane data.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/01_product_baseline.yaml#multi_tenancy
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
  - id: AC-010
    category: route_namespace
    statement: Platform routes MUST be served only from platform.iotables.net.
    verification_type: contract
    source_refs:
    - specs/01_product_baseline.yaml#route_namespace_standard
    - specs/02_system_architecture.yaml#routing_contract
  - id: AC-011
    category: route_namespace
    statement: Tenant staff routes MUST be served under /app on the tenant domain.
    verification_type: contract
    source_refs:
    - specs/01_product_baseline.yaml#route_namespace_standard
    - specs/02_system_architecture.yaml#routing_contract
  - id: AC-012
    category: route_namespace
    statement: Tenant configuration routes MUST be served under /console on the tenant
      domain.
    verification_type: contract
    source_refs:
    - specs/01_product_baseline.yaml#route_namespace_standard
    - specs/02_system_architecture.yaml#routing_contract
  - id: AC-013
    category: route_namespace
    statement: Customer guest routes MUST be scoped under /g/{token}.
    verification_type: contract
    source_refs:
    - specs/01_product_baseline.yaml#route_namespace_standard
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#customer_access_model
  - id: AC-014
    category: route_namespace
    statement: Root-level tenant routes MUST NOT be used for tenant staff, console,
      customer cart, menu, or order-complete flows.
    verification_type: contract
    source_refs:
    - specs/01_product_baseline.yaml#route_namespace_standard
    - specs/02_system_architecture.yaml#routing_contract
  - id: AC-020
    category: authorization
    statement: Backend authorization MUST enforce permissions, not role names.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/01_product_baseline.yaml#authentication_authorization
    - specs/03_domain_model.yaml#permission_model
  - id: AC-021
    category: authorization
    statement: Platform permissions and tenant permissions MUST use separate namespaces.
    verification_type: contract
    source_refs:
    - specs/03_domain_model.yaml#permission_namespaces
  - id: AC-022
    category: authorization
    statement: Tenant roles MUST NOT grant platform control-plane authority.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/01_product_baseline.yaml#authentication_authorization
    - specs/03_domain_model.yaml#permission_namespaces
  - id: AC-023
    category: authorization
    statement: Customer diner access MUST NOT use staff roles or tenant staff permissions.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/03_domain_model.yaml#permission_model
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#session_security
  - id: AC-024
    category: authorization
    statement: Firmware generation and download MUST be limited to tenant_owner and
      tenant_admin.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/03_domain_model.yaml#operation_authorization_rules
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#firmware_generation
  - id: AC-030
    category: customer_access
    statement: Old or expired QR codes MAY open the public menu.
    verification_type: end_to_end
    source_refs:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#customer_access_model
    - specs/01_product_baseline.yaml#customer_ordering_session_baseline
  - id: AC-031
    category: customer_access
    statement: Old or expired QR codes MUST NOT create or join a customer session.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#customer_access_model
  - id: AC-032
    category: customer_access
    statement: Old or expired QR codes MUST NOT expose bill content or active bill
      indicators.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#bill_visibility_policy
    - specs/01_product_baseline.yaml#customer_ordering_session_baseline
  - id: AC-033
    category: customer_access
    statement: A fresh current QR code MUST be required to create or join a customer
      session.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#customer_access_model
  - id: AC-034
    category: customer_access
    statement: A fresh current QR code MUST be single-use and consumed atomically.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#qr_token_security
  - id: AC-035
    category: customer_access
    statement: The table QR MUST rotate immediately after successful fresh QR consume.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#qr_token_security
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#server_qr_authority
  - id: AC-036
    category: customer_access
    statement: Cart mutation MUST require an active customer session.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#cart_policy
  - id: AC-037
    category: customer_access
    statement: Order submission MUST require an active customer session.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#active_session
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
  - id: AC-038
    category: customer_access
    statement: Successful order submission MUST move the customer session offline.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#on_successful_order_submit
  - id: AC-039
    category: customer_access
    statement: Offline customer sessions MUST NOT view bill content, mutate cart,
      or submit orders.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#session_security
  - id: AC-050
    category: bill_ordering
    statement: A table bill session MUST be created when the first order is submitted
      for the table.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/01_product_baseline.yaml#customer_ordering_session_baseline
    - specs/03_domain_model.yaml
  - id: AC-051
    category: bill_ordering
    statement: A table bill session MUST NOT exist only because the menu was opened.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/01_product_baseline.yaml#customer_ordering_session_baseline
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#conceptual_model
  - id: AC-052
    category: bill_ordering
    statement: Customers joining the same table MUST write submitted orders to the
      same table bill session.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/01_product_baseline.yaml#customer_ordering_session_baseline
    - specs/03_domain_model.yaml
  - id: AC-053
    category: bill_ordering
    statement: Submitted cart contents MUST become immutable.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#cart_policy
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
  - id: AC-054
    category: bill_ordering
    statement: The cashier MUST close the table bill session manually after external
      payment collection.
    verification_type: end_to_end
    source_refs:
    - specs/01_product_baseline.yaml#customer_ordering_session_baseline
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
  - id: AC-060
    category: payment_scope
    statement: IoTable MUST store menu item prices and calculate order and bill totals
      from tenant menu prices and ordered items.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/01_product_baseline.yaml#payment_checkout_scope
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml
  - id: AC-061
    category: payment_scope
    statement: IoTable MUST NOT collect money directly from customers.
    verification_type: contract
    source_refs:
    - specs/01_product_baseline.yaml#payment_checkout_scope
  - id: AC-062
    category: payment_scope
    statement: IoTable MUST NOT integrate with payment gateways, POS devices, fiscal
      devices, or e-invoice and e-archive systems.
    verification_type: contract
    source_refs:
    - specs/01_product_baseline.yaml#payment_checkout_scope
  - id: AC-070
    category: device
    statement: One ESP32 QR display device MUST belong to exactly one tenant and exactly
      one table.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#identity_model
  - id: AC-071
    category: device
    statement: A table MAY have at most one active QR display device.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#identity_model
  - id: AC-072
    category: device
    statement: ESP32 devices MUST NOT generate QR tokens.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#responsibilities
    - specs/01_product_baseline.yaml#device_baseline
  - id: AC-073
    category: device
    statement: ESP32 devices MUST display only server-generated QR payloads.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#server_qr_authority
  - id: AC-074
    category: device
    statement: Device secret MUST be generated by the server and stored on the server
      only as a hash.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#device_authentication
  - id: AC-075
    category: device
    statement: Firmware artifacts, Wi-Fi credentials, and device secrets MUST NOT
      be logged in plaintext.
    verification_type: security
    source_refs:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#firmware_generation
  - id: AC-076
    category: device
    statement: Tables without an ESP32 QR display device MUST NOT support customer
      self-ordering through table QR.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#table_device_option
  - id: AC-077
    category: device
    statement: Device reconnect MUST authenticate using the device secret and MUST
      NOT rotate QR by itself.
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#realtime_connection
  - id: AC-090
    category: frontend_stack
    statement: The frontend implementation MUST use separate Next.js applications
      named platform_web and tenant_web.
    verification_type: contract
    source_refs:
    - specs/02_system_architecture.yaml#selected_split
    - specs/02_system_architecture.yaml#baseline
  - id: AC-091
    category: frontend_stack
    statement: The frontend implementation MUST use Next.js 16.2.4 and React 19.2.5.
    verification_type: contract
    source_refs:
    - specs/02_system_architecture.yaml#frontend_stack
    - specs/02_system_architecture.yaml#baseline
  - id: AC-092
    category: frontend_stack
    statement: Blazor and Razor UI MUST NOT be used as IoTable frontend substitutions.
    verification_type: contract
    source_refs:
    - specs/02_system_architecture.yaml#forbidden_technology_substitutions
  - id: AC-093
    category: infrastructure_and_dns
    statement: "Multi-tenant dynamic SSL/TLS certificates MUST be provisioned dynamically and automatically on the first handshake using Caddy On-Demand TLS, after validating the tenant domain via /api/platform/tenants/validate-domain."
    verification_type: end_to_end
    source_refs:
    - specs/02_system_architecture.yaml#infrastructure_and_dns_policy
  - id: AC-094
    category: websocket_protocol
    statement: "ESP32 devices MUST communicate with the backend using JSON event frames, sending a 'heartbeat' frame every 60 seconds, and MUST implement exponential backoff reconnection up to a 300-second maximum upon disconnection."
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#websocket_device_protocol
  - id: AC-095
    category: database_migrations
    statement: "Schema migrations MUST target PostgreSQL 17, run inside single explicit transactions (except concurrent index creation), and strictly follow the multi-phase Expand-Contract pattern for all destructive schema updates."
    verification_type: integration
    source_refs:
    - specs/04_application_contracts.yaml#database_migration_rules
  open_questions: []
  done_when:
  - acceptance_criteria_have_stable_ids
  - acceptance_criteria_cite_authoritative_sources
  - acceptance_criteria_cover_current_clear_product_invariants
  - ambiguous_product_values_are_not_invented
  - open_questions_are_recorded
test_taxonomy:
  purpose:
    rule: Define the verification tiers used by IoTable implementation tasks and release
      checks.
    applies_to:
    - specification_validation
    - backend_tests
    - frontend_tests
    - api_contract_tests
    - database_contract_tests
    - device_contract_tests
    - security_tests
    - operational_smoke_tests
  taxonomy_rules:
  - id: TT-001
    rule: Every implementation task MUST identify the verification tiers affected
      by the task.
  - id: TT-002
    rule: A task that changes a public or internal API contract MUST include API contract
      verification.
  - id: TT-003
    rule: A task that changes database schema, indexes, constraints, seed data, or
      ownership rules MUST include database contract verification.
  - id: TT-004
    rule: A task that changes authentication, authorization, customer access, device
      identity, token handling, or secret handling MUST include security verification.
  - id: TT-005
    rule: A task that changes a user-facing workflow MUST include workflow-level verification.
  - id: TT-006
    rule: A task that changes deployment, health checks, configuration, background
      jobs, or recovery behavior MUST include operational verification.
  - id: TT-007
    rule: Specification validation MUST pass before implementation verification is
      considered complete.
  tiers:
  - id: spec_validation
    purpose: Verify that YAML specifications and JSON schemas are structurally valid
      and mutually complete.
    required_when:
    - any_specification_yaml_changes
    - any_specification_schema_changes
    - documentation_index_changes
    verifies:
    - yaml_has_matching_schema
    - schema_has_matching_yaml
    - specification_documents_validate_against_schemas
    - documentation_index_references_existing_documents
  - id: unit
    purpose: Verify deterministic logic inside a small implementation unit.
    required_when:
    - domain_rule_changes
    - pure_validation_logic_changes
    - calculation_logic_changes
    - mapping_logic_changes
    verifies:
    - domain_invariants
    - validation_branches
    - calculation_results
    - error_paths_inside_unit
  - id: integration
    purpose: Verify coordination between application services, persistence, authorization,
      background processing, and realtime components.
    required_when:
    - application_service_changes
    - authorization_enforcement_changes
    - persistence_behavior_changes
    - background_job_behavior_changes
    - device_realtime_behavior_changes
    verifies:
    - module_boundary_behavior
    - database_interaction
    - authorization_policy_effect
    - transactional_behavior
    - realtime_message_flow
  - id: api_contract
    purpose: Verify HTTP request, response, authentication, authorization, error,
      pagination, and idempotency contracts.
    required_when:
    - api_route_changes
    - request_schema_changes
    - response_schema_changes
    - api_error_model_changes
    - authentication_requirement_changes
    verifies:
    - route_namespace
    - request_validation
    - response_shape
    - error_shape
    - authentication_and_authorization_requirements
  - id: database_contract
    purpose: Verify database table, column, index, constraint, relationship, ownership,
      and migration contracts.
    required_when:
    - data_contract_changes
    - schema_migration_changes
    - tenant_isolation_rule_changes
    - seed_data_changes
    - database_constraint_changes
    verifies:
    - table_and_column_shape
    - required_constraints
    - tenant_data_isolation
    - migration_application
    - seed_data_presence
  - id: workflow
    purpose: Verify complete user, staff, platform, customer, and device workflows
      across multiple implementation boundaries.
    required_when:
    - customer_ordering_flow_changes
    - tenant_setup_flow_changes
    - platform_lifecycle_flow_changes
    - bill_closure_flow_changes
    - station_workflow_changes
    - firmware_generation_flow_changes
    verifies:
    - end_to_end_flow_outcome
    - cross_boundary_state_changes
    - required_authorization_gates
    - visible_user_result
  - id: security
    purpose: Verify security-sensitive invariants for identity, authorization, customer
      access, device secrets, QR tokens, and secret handling.
    required_when:
    - authentication_changes
    - authorization_changes
    - qr_token_changes
    - customer_session_changes
    - device_secret_changes
    - firmware_artifact_changes
    - logging_of_sensitive_data_changes
    verifies:
    - least_privilege
    - tenant_scope_enforcement
    - token_single_use
    - secret_not_logged
    - plaintext_secret_storage_forbidden
  - id: operational_smoke
    purpose: Verify that a deployed runtime is routable, observable, and operationally
      ready for basic traffic.
    required_when:
    - deployment_changes
    - health_check_changes
    - configuration_changes
    - background_job_runtime_changes
    - reverse_proxy_or_domain_changes
    verifies:
    - backend_process_routability
    - health_endpoint_behavior
    - database_connectivity
    - background_job_availability
    - frontend_deployable_availability
  verification_mapping:
    acceptance_criteria_document: specs/11-testing-verification/acceptance-criteria.yaml
    mapping_rule: Each acceptance criterion MUST be verifiable by at least one taxonomy
      tier.
    implementation_done_rule: An implementation task is not done until every affected
      taxonomy tier has an explicit verification result or a recorded blocker.
  release_verification_commands:
    command_sets:
    - id: spec_repository_validation
      command: pnpm validate
      run_from: specification_repository_root
      required_when:
      - any_specification_yaml_changes
      - any_specification_schema_changes
      - documentation_index_changes
      verifies:
      - specification_documents_validate_against_schemas
      - documentation_index_references_existing_documents
    - id: backend_test_suite
      command: pytest
      run_from: implementation_repository_root
      required_when:
      - backend_code_changes
      - backend_contract_changes
      - database_contract_changes
      - authorization_or_security_changes
      verifies:
      - unit
      - integration
      - api_contract
      - database_contract
      - security
    - id: frontend_build
      command: pnpm build
      run_from: frontend_workspace_root
      required_when:
      - next_js_or_react_code_changes
      - frontend_route_changes
      - frontend_contract_changes
      verifies:
      - frontend_deployable_availability
      - frontend_compile_time_contracts
    - id: frontend_test_suite
      command: pnpm test
      run_from: frontend_workspace_root
      required_when:
      - frontend_behavior_changes
      - user_facing_workflow_changes
      verifies:
      - workflow
      - frontend_interaction_behavior
    - id: operational_health_smoke
      command: HTTP GET /health/live and /health/ready against the deployed backend
        hosts defined in specs/05_testing_operations.yaml
      run_from: deployed_environment
      required_when:
      - deployment_changes
      - health_check_changes
      - configuration_changes
      - release_candidate_verification
      verifies:
      - backend_process_routability
      - health_endpoint_behavior
      - readiness_dependency_gate
    rules:
    - Required command sets MUST be reported with pass, fail, or blocked status before
      an implementation task is considered done.
    - If a required command does not exist in the current repository state, the verification
      result MUST be recorded as blocked with the missing command named explicitly.
    - Ad hoc manual inspection MUST NOT replace a required executable command set.
  source_alignment:
    authoritative_project_documents:
    - specs/00_meta_policies.yaml
    - specs/00_meta_policies.yaml
    - specs/05_testing_operations.yaml
    reference_documents_used_as_inspiration:
    - C:\Users\Victus\Desktop\dr\2\docs\explanation\concepts\test-taxonomy.md
    reference_fit_rule: Reference test tier names may inspire structure but MUST NOT
      add timing budgets, framework choices, or CI commands that are not defined by
      IoTable specifications.
  open_questions: []
  done_when:
  - verification_tiers_are_defined
  - required_when_rules_are_defined
  - verification_scope_is_defined_for_each_tier
  - acceptance_criteria_mapping_rule_is_defined
  - release_verification_commands_are_defined
health_checks:
  purpose:
    rule: Define IoTable health check endpoints, liveness behavior, readiness behavior,
      response shape, and operational probe rules.
    applies_to:
    - single_backend_deployable
    - platform_api
    - tenant_internal_api
    - customer_public_api
    - device_realtime_endpoint
    - background_jobs
    - ubuntu_vps_deployment
    - reverse_proxy
    - release_verification
  endpoint_contract:
    endpoints:
    - id: health_alias
      path: /health
      classification: liveness_alias
      purpose: Provide a simple liveness-compatible endpoint for basic uptime tooling.
    - id: health_live
      path: /health/live
      classification: liveness
      purpose: Report whether the backend process can answer HTTP requests.
    - id: health_ready
      path: /health/ready
      classification: readiness
      purpose: Report whether the backend process is ready to receive real application
        traffic.
    rules:
    - id: HC-001
      rule: IoTable backend MUST expose /health, /health/live, and /health/ready.
    - id: HC-002
      rule: /health MUST behave as a liveness-compatible alias.
    - id: HC-003
      rule: /health/live MUST NOT fail because of database, cache, background job,
        or external dependency unavailability.
    - id: HC-004
      rule: /health/ready MUST fail when a required dependency prevents safe request
        handling.
  liveness_policy:
    endpoint: /health/live
    dependency_checks_allowed: false
    success_condition: backend_process_can_respond_to_http
    failure_meaning: backend_process_is_not_responding_to_basic_http_requests
    rules:
    - id: HC-LIVE-001
      rule: Liveness MUST check process responsiveness, not business readiness.
    - id: HC-LIVE-002
      rule: A temporary database outage MUST NOT cause /health/live to fail.
    - id: HC-LIVE-003
      rule: A saturated background job queue MUST NOT cause /health/live to fail.
  readiness_policy:
    endpoint: /health/ready
    scope_resolution:
      platform_host:
        host: platform.iotables.net
        readiness_scope: platform
        database_checks:
        - platform_database_connectivity
        - database_migration_state
      tenant_host:
        host: '{tenant}.iotables.net'
        readiness_scope: tenant
        database_checks:
        - platform_database_connectivity
        - tenant_database_connectivity
        - database_migration_state
      rule: Readiness scope MUST be resolved from the request host before running
        scoped dependency checks.
    required_checks:
    - id: backend_configuration
      component_type: configuration
      failure_status: fail
      rule: Required runtime configuration MUST be present.
    - id: platform_database_connectivity
      component_type: datastore
      failure_status: fail
      rule: The backend MUST be able to connect to the platform database when serving
        platform-scope behavior.
    - id: tenant_database_connectivity
      component_type: datastore
      failure_status: fail
      rule: The backend MUST be able to connect to the resolved tenant database when
        serving tenant-scope behavior.
    - id: database_migration_state
      component_type: datastore
      failure_status: fail
      rule: The active database schema MUST match the schema version required by the
        deployed backend.
    - id: background_job_runtime
      component_type: background_worker
      failure_status: fail
      rule: Required in-process background job runtime MUST be available.
    rules:
    - id: HC-READY-001
      rule: Readiness MUST verify dependencies required for safe request handling.
    - id: HC-READY-002
      rule: Readiness MUST fail when the required database for the addressed scope
        is unreachable.
    - id: HC-READY-003
      rule: Readiness MUST fail when required migrations are not applied.
    - id: HC-READY-004
      rule: Readiness MUST fail when required runtime configuration is missing.
    - id: HC-READY-005
      rule: Readiness MUST NOT include business-state checks such as open order count,
        active table count, menu item count, or tenant revenue.
    - id: HC-READY-006
      rule: A readiness request for platform.iotables.net MUST evaluate platform-scope
        readiness.
    - id: HC-READY-007
      rule: A readiness request for a tenant host matching {tenant}.iotables.net MUST
        evaluate readiness for that resolved tenant.
    - id: HC-READY-008
      rule: Release verification MUST check readiness for platform.iotables.net and
        at least one provisioned active tenant host.
  response_contract:
    content_type: application/health+json
    status_values:
    - pass
    - warn
    - fail
    required_fields:
    - status
    - service
    - version
    - releaseId
    - checks
    http_status_mapping:
      pass: 200
      warn: 200
      fail: 503
    rules:
    - id: HC-RESP-001
      rule: Health responses MUST use application/health+json.
    - id: HC-RESP-002
      rule: Health responses MUST include status, service, version, releaseId, and
        checks.
    - id: HC-RESP-003
      rule: A failed health response MUST use HTTP 503.
    - id: HC-RESP-004
      rule: A health response MUST NOT include secrets, connection strings, token
        values, customer session identifiers, bill content, order content, or firmware
        secrets.
  exposure_policy:
    public_exposure:
      health_alias: allowed
      health_live: allowed
      health_ready: restricted
    rules:
    - id: HC-EXP-001
      rule: /health and /health/live MAY be exposed through the reverse proxy for
        uptime monitoring.
    - id: HC-EXP-002
      rule: /health/ready MUST be restricted to trusted operational callers because
        readiness detail can expose internal dependency names.
    - id: HC-EXP-003
      rule: Health endpoints MUST NOT require platform_owner or tenant staff authentication
        when called by trusted operational infrastructure.
  release_gate_integration:
    required_checks:
    - GET /health/live returns HTTP 200 with status pass
    - GET /health/ready on platform.iotables.net returns HTTP 200 with status pass
      after deployment
    - GET /health/ready on a provisioned active tenant host returns HTTP 200 with
      status pass after deployment
    - GET /health/ready returns HTTP 503 with status fail when a required platform-scope
      test dependency is intentionally unavailable
    - GET /health/ready returns HTTP 503 with status fail when a required tenant-scope
      test dependency is intentionally unavailable for the checked tenant host
    rules:
    - id: HC-RG-001
      rule: Release verification MUST include liveness and readiness checks against
        the deployed backend.
    - id: HC-RG-002
      rule: Release verification MUST prove that readiness can fail closed for a required
        dependency.
  source_alignment:
    authoritative_project_documents:
    - specs/01_product_baseline.yaml
    - specs/02_system_architecture.yaml
    - specs/05_testing_operations.yaml
    - specs/02_system_architecture.yaml
    external_references:
    - id: EXT-HC-001
      source: FastAPI Path Operation Configuration health check patterns
      url: https://fastapi.tiangolo.com/tutorial/path-operation-configuration/
      usage: Supports FastAPI health check endpoints implementation as the backend stack
        mechanism.
    - id: EXT-HC-002
      source: Kubernetes liveness, readiness, and startup probes documentation
      url: https://kubernetes.io/docs/concepts/configuration/liveness-readiness-startup-probes/
      usage: Supports the liveness and readiness separation used by IoTable operational
        probes.
    - id: EXT-HC-003
      source: IETF Health Check Response Format for HTTP APIs draft
      url: https://datatracker.ietf.org/doc/html/draft-inadarei-api-health-check-06
      usage: Supports the application/health+json media type and pass, warn, fail
        response status values used by IoTable health responses.
    reference_fit_rule: External references define operational probe convention and
      framework mechanism; IoTable endpoint names and readiness checks are binding
      only as written in this document.
  forbidden_behavior:
  - single_health_endpoint_that_mixes_liveness_and_readiness
  - database_dependency_failure_causing_liveness_failure
  - business_state_check_inside_readiness
  - health_response_exposing_secrets
  - public_unrestricted_readiness_detail
  open_questions: []
  done_when:
  - health_endpoint_paths_are_defined
  - liveness_policy_is_defined
  - readiness_policy_is_defined
  - response_contract_is_defined
  - exposure_policy_is_defined
  - release_gate_health_checks_are_defined
  - slo_and_recovery_values_are_defined_in_slo_recovery_targets
slo_recovery_targets:
  purpose:
    rule: Define IoTable service-level objectives, recovery targets, backup retention,
      and operational review rules.
    applies_to:
    - release_verification
    - operations_monitoring
    - incident_response
    - backup_restore
    - health_checks
    - customer_ordering
    - device_realtime_endpoint
  startup_probe_policy:
    startup_probe_endpoint_required: false
    rule: IoTable does not define a separate startup health endpoint for the Ubuntu
      VPS deployment baseline.
    rationale:
    - The deployment baseline is Ubuntu VPS with a single backend deployable.
    - /health/live and /health/ready are sufficient for the current process supervision
      and release verification model.
    - A startup probe endpoint requires a separate owner-approved operations decision
      when IoTable adopts a deployment environment that supports startup probes.
  availability_targets:
  - id: SLO-AVAIL-001
    scope: backend_monthly_availability
    target: 99.5%
    measurement_window: calendar_month
    measurement_source: health_check_and_request_success_monitoring
  - id: SLO-AVAIL-002
    scope: customer_ordering_monthly_availability
    target: 99.5%
    measurement_window: calendar_month
    measurement_source: customer_ordering_request_success_monitoring
  latency_targets:
  - id: SLO-LAT-001
    scope: api_p95_latency
    target_ms: 500
    measurement_window: rolling_30_days
    measurement_source: server_request_duration_metrics
  - id: SLO-LAT-002
    scope: customer_order_submit_p95_latency
    target_ms: 1000
    measurement_window: rolling_30_days
    measurement_source: customer_order_submit_duration_metrics
  - id: SLO-LAT-003
    scope: qr_consume_p95_latency
    target_ms: 500
    measurement_window: rolling_30_days
    measurement_source: qr_consume_duration_metrics
  - id: SLO-LAT-004
    scope: health_ready_p95_latency
    target_ms: 250
    measurement_window: rolling_30_days
    measurement_source: health_ready_duration_metrics
  device_targets:
  - id: SLO-DEV-001
    scope: websocket_reconnect_target
    target_value: 10
    target_unit: seconds
    measurement_window: rolling_30_days
    measurement_source: device_connection_metrics
  - id: SLO-DEV-002
    scope: qr_push_after_rotation_p95_latency
    target_value: 1000
    target_unit: milliseconds
    measurement_window: rolling_30_days
    measurement_source: device_qr_push_duration_metrics
  backup_recovery_targets:
    backup_retention_days: 30
    rpo_hours: 24
    rto_hours: 4
    recovery_drill_interval_days: 90
    rules:
    - id: SLO-REC-001
      rule: Backups MUST be retained for 30 days.
    - id: SLO-REC-002
      rule: IoTable recovery point objective MUST be 24 hours.
    - id: SLO-REC-003
      rule: IoTable recovery time objective MUST be 4 hours.
    - id: SLO-REC-004
      rule: A recovery drill MUST be performed at least once every 90 days.
  release_gate_rules:
  - id: SLO-RG-001
    rule: Release verification MUST confirm that health-check release gate checks
      defined in specs/05_testing_operations.yaml pass.
  - id: SLO-RG-002
    rule: A release MUST NOT knowingly ship with a broken backup path.
  - id: SLO-RG-003
    rule: A release MUST record an operational blocker when current measurements show
      a breach of a defined availability, latency, device, or recovery target.
  revision_policy:
    rule: SLO and recovery target changes MUST be explicit specification changes.
    allowed_reasons:
    - measured_production_baseline
    - owner_approved_operational_risk_change
    - infrastructure_model_change
    - customer_contract_change
    forbidden_reasons:
    - silent_target_relaxation_after_failure
    - untracked_metric_definition_change
    - changing_targets_without_updating_release_gate_rules
  source_alignment:
    authoritative_project_documents:
    - specs/05_testing_operations.yaml
    - specs/05_testing_operations.yaml
    - specs/02_system_architecture.yaml
    external_references:
    - id: EXT-SLO-001
      source: Google SRE Workbook error budget policy
      url: https://sre.google/workbook/error-budget-policy/
      usage: Supports SLO and operational review framing.
    - id: EXT-SLO-002
      source: NIST contingency planning guidance
      url: https://csrc.nist.gov/topics/security-and-privacy/security-programs-and-operations/contingency-planning
      usage: Supports explicit recovery objective and contingency planning framing.
    owner_decision:
      decided_by: project_owner
      decision: Startup probe is not defined for the Ubuntu VPS baseline; initial
        SLO, RTO, RPO, backup retention, and recovery drill targets are accepted.
  open_questions: []
  done_when:
  - startup_probe_policy_is_closed
  - availability_targets_are_defined
  - latency_targets_are_defined
  - device_targets_are_defined
  - backup_recovery_targets_are_defined
  - release_gate_rules_are_defined
  - revision_policy_is_defined
  - open_questions_are_closed
customer_access_security:
  purpose:
    rule: Define the customer QR, session, bill visibility, cart mutation, order submission,
      and abuse protection model.
    applies_to:
    - customer_ordering
    - customer_public_api
    - customer_qr_presence
    - tenant_customer_sessions
    - tenant_cart
    - tenant_ordering
    - tenant_bill_sessions
  security_priority:
    priority_order:
    - security
    - correctness
    - operational_simplicity
    - ux
    rule: UX improvements MUST NOT weaken the customer access security model.
  customer_access_model:
    old_or_expired_qr:
      menu_view: true
      session_entry: false
      bill_view: false
      cart_mutation: false
      order_submit: false
      active_bill_indicator: false
      rules:
      - Old or expired QR codes MAY open the public menu.
      - Old or expired QR codes MUST NOT create or join a customer session.
      - Old or expired QR codes MUST NOT expose bill content.
      - Old or expired QR codes MUST NOT expose whether a table has an active bill
        session.
      - Old or expired QR codes MUST NOT allow cart mutation.
      - Old or expired QR codes MUST NOT allow order submission.
    fresh_qr:
      session_entry: true
      consumes_qr: true
      rotates_qr_after_consume: true
      creates_or_joins_session: true
      rules:
      - A fresh current QR code is required to create or join a customer session.
      - A fresh current QR code MUST be single-use.
      - A fresh current QR code MUST be consumed atomically.
      - The table QR MUST rotate immediately after successful consume.
      - Fresh QR session entry MUST NOT require a cart item.
    active_session:
      bill_view: true
      cart_mutation: true
      order_submit: true
      rules:
      - An active customer session MAY view the current table bill content.
      - An active customer session MAY create and mutate its cart.
      - An active customer session MAY submit an order.
      - Cart mutation MUST NOT require scanning a new QR for every item.
      - Order submission MUST require an active customer session.
    on_successful_order_submit:
      session_status: offline
      cart_status: submitted
      further_bill_view_requires_new_fresh_qr: true
      further_cart_mutation_requires_new_fresh_qr: true
      further_order_submit_requires_new_fresh_qr: true
      rules:
      - Successful order submission MUST move the customer session offline.
      - After successful order submission, the submitted cart MUST NOT be mutable.
      - After successful order submission, bill view MUST require a new fresh QR session
        entry.
      - After successful order submission, additional cart mutation MUST require a
        new fresh QR session entry.
      - After successful order submission, additional order submission MUST require
        a new fresh QR session entry.
  conceptual_model:
    old_qr: Public menu access only.
    fresh_qr: Physical table presence proof for session entry.
    customer_session: Temporary authorization to view bill, mutate cart, and submit
      one order flow.
    order_submit: Critical action that finalizes the current cart and moves the customer
      session offline.
    bill_session: Table-scoped operational adisyon created on first submitted order
      and closed by authorized staff after external payment.
  qr_token_security:
    ttl_seconds: 60
    single_use: true
    server_generated: true
    token_storage: sha256_hash_only
    plaintext_storage_allowed: false
    atomic_consume_required: true
    rotate_after_successful_consume: true
    rules:
    - QR tokens MUST be generated by the server.
    - QR tokens MUST be stored only as hashes.
    - Plaintext QR tokens MUST NOT be persisted.
    - QR consume MUST be atomic.
    - QR reuse MUST be rejected.
    - Known expired, used, or replaced QR tokens SHOULD return a controlled recovery
      response.
    - Malformed or unknown QR tokens MAY return a generic invalid or not found response.
  session_security:
    session_scope:
    - tenant
    - table
    - browser_device
    session_entry_requires_fresh_qr: true
    session_entry_without_cart_allowed: true
    session_entry_purpose:
    - bill_view
    - cart_start
    - order_flow_start
    session_offline_after_order_submit: true
    idle_timeout_required: true
    absolute_timeout_required: true
    rules:
    - Customer session authority MUST be scoped to tenant, table, and browser device.
    - A customer session MUST NOT grant access to any other table.
    - A customer session MUST NOT survive bill session closure.
    - A customer session MUST become offline after successful order submission.
    - Offline sessions MUST NOT view bill content, mutate cart, or submit orders.
  bill_visibility_policy:
    public_bill_visibility: false
    old_qr_bill_visibility: false
    active_session_bill_visibility: true
    rules:
    - Bill content MUST NOT be public.
    - Old or expired QR codes MUST NOT expose bill content.
    - Bill content MAY be shown only to an active customer session for the same table.
    - Bill content MUST NOT be visible after the customer session becomes offline
      unless a new fresh QR session entry occurs.
  cart_policy:
    cart_mutation_requires_active_session: true
    cart_mutation_requires_fresh_qr_per_item: false
    cart_submit_requires_active_session: true
    cart_revalidation_before_order_submit: true
    rules:
    - Cart mutation MUST require an active customer session.
    - Cart mutation MUST NOT require a fresh QR for every item.
    - Order submission MUST revalidate cart contents against current menu item orderability,
      pricing, and station assignment.
    - Submitted cart MUST become immutable.
  edge_protection:
    production_required: true
    provider: Cloudflare
    controls:
    - WAF
    - rate_limiting
    - bot_detection
    - Turnstile_when_suspicious_or_rate_limited
    rules:
    - Production customer public endpoints MUST be protected by an edge security layer.
    - Turnstile MUST NOT replace QR physical presence proof.
    - Edge protection reduces bot abuse but does not prove table presence.
  rate_limiting:
    required_dimensions:
    - per_ip
    - per_tenant
    - per_table
    - per_qr_token_hash
    - per_customer_session
    protected_operations:
    - menu_view_by_old_qr
    - fresh_qr_session_entry
    - cart_mutation
    - order_submit
    - bill_view
    - invalid_qr_attempt
    rules:
    - Customer public API MUST rate limit abuse-sensitive endpoints.
    - Invalid QR attempts MUST be rate limited.
    - Order submission MUST use idempotency protection.
  forbidden_behavior:
  - old_qr_session_entry
  - old_qr_bill_view
  - old_qr_cart_mutation
  - old_qr_order_submit
  - public_bill_content
  - fresh_qr_reuse
  - plaintext_qr_token_storage
  - cart_mutation_without_active_session
  - order_submit_without_active_session
  - session_remaining_active_after_successful_order_submit
  - turnstile_as_replacement_for_qr_presence
  open_decisions: []
  done_when:
  - old_qr_behavior_is_defined
  - fresh_qr_behavior_is_defined
  - active_session_capabilities_are_defined
  - post_order_session_offline_behavior_is_defined
  - qr_token_security_is_defined
  - session_security_is_defined
  - bill_visibility_policy_is_defined
  - cart_policy_is_defined
  - edge_protection_policy_is_defined
  - rate_limiting_dimensions_are_defined
  - forbidden_customer_access_behavior_is_defined

documents.title: IoTable Application Contracts
documents.raw_text:
document:
  id: 04-application-contracts.application-contracts
  title: IoTable Application Contracts
  project: IoTable
  version: 1.0.0
  language: en
  format: yaml
  schema_required: true
  schema_file: schemas/04_application_contracts.schema.json
  status: draft
platform_audit_catalog:
  purpose:
    rule: Define platform-scoped audit policy, event catalog, required fields, exclusions,
      and retention behavior.
    audience:
    - platform_owner
    applies_to:
    - platform_control_plane
    - platform_api
    - tenant_lifecycle
    - tenant_provisioning
    - tenant_schema_jobs
    - platform_settings
    - platform_authentication
  platform_audit_policy:
    audit_read_only_views: false
    audit_state_changes: true
    audit_security_events: true
    audit_failed_mutations: true
    audit_system_job_failures: true
    audit_retention_days: 365
    rules:
    - Read-only page views MUST NOT create platform audit records.
    - State-changing platform actions MUST create platform audit records.
    - Security-relevant platform authentication events MUST create platform audit
      records.
    - Failed platform mutations MUST create platform audit records.
    - System job failures related to platform-owned tenant lifecycle, provisioning,
      or schema changes MUST create platform audit records.
    - Audit records MUST be append-only.
    - Audit records MUST NOT be edited after creation.
  platform_audit_record_contract:
    required_fields:
      id: uuid
      event_type: enum
      actor_type: platform_owner_or_system
      actor_id: nullable_uuid
      tenant_id: nullable_uuid
      job_id: nullable_uuid
      severity: info_warning_or_critical
      reason: nullable_string
      metadata: json_object
      created_at: timestamp
    rules:
    - actor_id MUST be present when actor_type is platform_owner.
    - actor_id MUST be null when actor_type is system.
    - tenant_id MUST be present for tenant-related platform audit events.
    - job_id MUST be present for job-related platform audit events.
    - reason MUST be present when the action requires a reason.
    - metadata MUST contain only structured machine-readable fields.
  event_catalog:
    tenant_lifecycle_events:
    - id: tenant_created
      actor_type: platform_owner
      severity: info
      required_fields:
      - tenant_id
      - actor_id
      - created_at
    - id: tenant_creation_failed
      actor_type: system
      severity: critical
      required_fields:
      - tenant_id
      - failure_message
      - created_at
    - id: tenant_suspended
      actor_type: platform_owner
      severity: warning
      required_fields:
      - tenant_id
      - actor_id
      - reason
      - created_at
    - id: tenant_reactivated
      actor_type: platform_owner
      severity: info
      required_fields:
      - tenant_id
      - actor_id
      - reason
      - created_at
    - id: tenant_deactivated
      actor_type: platform_owner
      severity: critical
      required_fields:
      - tenant_id
      - actor_id
      - reason
      - created_at
    activation_events:
    - id: tenant_owner_activation_invite_created
      actor_type: system
      severity: info
      required_fields:
      - tenant_id
      - created_at
    - id: tenant_owner_activation_invite_resent
      actor_type: platform_owner
      severity: info
      required_fields:
      - tenant_id
      - actor_id
      - reason
      - created_at
    provisioning_events:
    - id: tenant_provisioning_started
      actor_type: system
      severity: info
      required_fields:
      - tenant_id
      - job_id
      - created_at
    - id: tenant_provisioning_failed
      actor_type: system
      severity: critical
      required_fields:
      - tenant_id
      - job_id
      - failed_step
      - failure_message
      - created_at
    - id: tenant_provisioning_retry_requested
      actor_type: platform_owner
      severity: warning
      required_fields:
      - tenant_id
      - job_id
      - actor_id
      - failed_step
      - retry_attempt_number
      - reason
      - created_at
    - id: tenant_provisioning_retry_completed
      actor_type: system
      severity: info
      required_fields:
      - tenant_id
      - job_id
      - created_at
    schema_job_events:
    - id: tenant_schema_job_started
      actor_type: system
      severity: info
      required_fields:
      - tenant_id
      - job_id
      - created_at
    - id: tenant_schema_job_failed
      actor_type: system
      severity: critical
      required_fields:
      - tenant_id
      - job_id
      - failure_message
      - created_at
    - id: tenant_schema_job_completed
      actor_type: system
      severity: info
      required_fields:
      - tenant_id
      - job_id
      - created_at
    settings_events:
    - id: platform_setting_updated
      actor_type: platform_owner
      severity: warning
      required_fields:
      - actor_id
      - setting_id
      - old_value
      - new_value
      - created_at
    - id: platform_setting_update_rejected
      actor_type: system
      severity: warning
      required_fields:
      - setting_id
      - attempted_value
      - reason
      - created_at
    authentication_events:
    - id: platform_login_succeeded
      actor_type: platform_owner
      severity: info
      required_fields:
      - actor_id
      - created_at
    - id: platform_login_failed
      actor_type: system
      severity: warning
      required_fields:
      - attempted_identifier
      - failure_reason
      - created_at
  excluded_events:
  - id: dashboard_viewed
    reason: Read-only dashboard viewing does not change state and would create audit
      noise.
  - id: tenants_list_viewed
    reason: Read-only list viewing does not change state and would create audit noise.
  - id: tenant_detail_viewed
    reason: Read-only tenant inspection does not change state and would create audit
      noise.
  - id: health_page_viewed
    reason: Read-only health inspection does not change state and would create audit
      noise.
  - id: settings_page_viewed
    reason: Read-only settings inspection does not change state and would create audit
      noise.
  platform_audit_ui:
    route: /audit
    filters:
    - event_type
    - severity
    - actor_type
    - tenant_id
    - job_id
    - created_at_range
    default_sort: created_at_desc
    rules:
    - Audit UI MUST show event type, severity, actor, tenant, job, reason, and created_at
      when available.
    - Audit UI MUST show metadata as structured key-value details.
    - Audit UI MUST NOT allow editing or deleting audit records.
  forbidden_behavior:
  - auditing_read_only_page_views
  - editing_audit_records
  - deleting_audit_records_from_ui
  - free_text_only_metadata
  - missing_reason_for_reason_required_actions
  open_decisions: []
  done_when:
  - platform_audit_policy_is_defined
  - audit_record_contract_is_defined
  - tenant_lifecycle_audit_events_are_defined
  - activation_audit_events_are_defined
  - provisioning_audit_events_are_defined
  - schema_job_audit_events_are_defined
  - settings_audit_events_are_defined
  - authentication_audit_events_are_defined
  - excluded_audit_events_are_defined
  - audit_ui_behavior_is_defined
platform_control_plane:
  purpose:
    rule: Define the platform owner control plane surface used to manage tenant lifecycle,
      provisioning, schema jobs, audit, support, and system health.
    audience:
    - platform_owner
    scope_boundary:
      included:
      - tenant_lifecycle
      - tenant_creation
      - tenant_provisioning
      - tenant_schema_jobs
      - platform_audit
      - support_visibility
      - system_health
      - platform_settings
      excluded:
      - tenant_customer_operations
      - tenant_menu_management
      - tenant_order_operations
      - tenant_station_operations
      - tenant_checkout_operations
  route_map:
    base_domain: https://platform.iotables.net
    routes:
    - id: platform_dashboard
      path: /
      purpose: Show platform overview, tenant counts, provisioning health, job failures,
        and system health summary.
    - id: tenants_list
      path: /tenants
      purpose: List tenants and their lifecycle, domain, provisioning, and activation
        state.
    - id: tenant_create
      path: /tenants/new
      purpose: Create a new tenant with minimal manual input and strict system-generated
        infrastructure identifiers.
    - id: tenant_detail
